text: Brilliant value, excellent staff. The beautiful setting is to be expected for this part of the world, but what makes this hotel exceptional is the "Can-do" attitude and friendly helpfulness displayed by every staff member.  The rooms are clean, comfortable and well appointed, the quiet location engenders a restful ambience, yet it is quite close to the London-Brighton motorway.  Easily the best value for many miles around, as a frequent business traveller in need of a little serenity, I have made this hotel a regular stopping point.

Total bargain! A stay at the Hickstead & a day at Brighton races. We went to the hotel as part of a package day with Brighton Racecourse, which is 15 minutes away.

It was the most incredible value, £55 per person for overnight accomodation, breakfast and a day at the races.

I wasn't expecting the hotel to be very good for the price we paid, but it was excellent! The staff were friendly and helpful and the room a good size. The decor was a little bit dated but clean, but I understand from the staff that a refurbishment is about to start.

There was a good choice for breakfast and the hotel was in a lovely quiet spot.

I have told so many people about it, our stay was such good value!

Shame that our luck at the races wasn't as good as with the hotel!!

Meet Basil here. I strongly recommend the Hickstead Hotel to fans of Fawlty Towers who would like to see what it is like to stay in a tumble down building managed by people who have not a clue about how far they are from the standards freely available from competitors.  

Photographs on various web sites show a nice rural aspect with a wood fence, and describes the hotel as a great base to explore the local countryside.  The hotel is in fact right by the busy A23, and looks out on industrial-looking buildings.

Web sites also describe the hotel as having an indoor pool and a mini gym.  It's hard for two people to swim in the pool at the same time, and the mini gym consists of three or four machines in a room the size of a couple of lavatories.

There is battered paintwork and threadbare carpet everywhere.  To be fair, there is a refurbishment programme under way; but the curtains in the newly done-up rooms don't match the carpets.

The best feature is the food.  It can take a while to arrive (half an hour to be brought any coffee at breakfast), but it is good when it appears.

fantastic. A beautiful hotel for a wonderful weekend away. 
The staff and the building have a very homley feeling, 
comfortable and friendly, excellent cuisine and service, allround a wonderful hotel experience.
I am looking forward to going back soon.
very shabby. clean rooms, but hotel overall "shabby", and £11.00 for 4 cups of tea!!

Really nice hotel with friendly staff. Stayed for two nights when visiting Brighton in Oct 07.  My daughters also stayed in a twin room for one night.

Given the poor reviews on trip advisor, I was a bit worried about what to expect.  The reviews are totally out of date and the hotel highly recommended.  Really nice friendly reception staff, the room rate was competitive for that area but offers better value.  

The room was very clean and large for a double, nice bathroom with everything you could want.  If you are a very light sleeper, ask for a room upstairs, but otherwise it was very quiet during the night.

Breakfast was the best I’ve had in a long while, huge selection and very helpful staff.  If you like a good cooked breakfast, then this is the place for you.  I do about 40 - 60 hotel nights per year around the world, this was one of the best breakfasts I’ve had for many years.

There is the added advantage of the swimming pool and sauna.  The gym is a mini one, so don’t expect to burn off your breakfast.

Would stay again and recommend for families and business trips as both are catered for very well

Quiet. Very Piecefull hotel, cleen rooms, comfortable.

I slept in till 10:50, and made the 11:00 checkout, even with a room at the busy end of the corridorr, overlooking the car park, I was not woken by any noise - you can't say that about many hotels.

I did have the burger on room service, and I eat a lot of burgers, this one was really really bad

We stayed............ for 30 miniutes!. I'd booked the Hickstead for a short Easter weekend break, hoping for a nice dinner on Sunday night, comfortable stay, use of the pool etc on Monday morning, and a lunch before we left for home. No-one greeted us at reception - too busy serving drinks in the bar. Eventually, we were booked in, by a member of staff who couldn't have been more cheery - whilst she was telling us about the amenities - restaurant - closed, and the pool - closed...."oh that's been closed for a while - it's a general maintenance problem!" Then why, we asked, are we here? We'd ordered some bar food [burgers, etc] as that was all there was on offer. We thought about the £80 we'd be spending, booked a cab, and caught a late train home. As we waited the promised 10mins for our cab, Cheery Receptionist asked if we wanted our food! 
I checked the hotel website a few days later and saw they'd sneakily removed the reference to the 'pool'. Still nothing saying the restaurant was "always closed on Sunday evenings!", as the cheery one assured us.
Back home, the welcome was sincere, food delicious, there was no smell of damp, and we went to our local lido the following morning

Quality evening meals & friendly receptionists. We would like to give positive feedback to the hotel after enjoying a weekend break there, primarily to visit Brighton.
We stayed in one of the two executive superior rooms, which came complete with new decor, bottles of still & sparkling spring water, hygienically-sealed bathrobes and a plate of sliced strawberries, marshmallows & dusted maltesers. Only slight grumble was the view. The bed was very comfortable and we slept soundly on both nights. 
We can honestly say that the staff were all professional: the receptionists and the dining room manager were good; the breakfast service did lack a little consistency around the room. 
The evening meals, prepared at the weekend by the sous-chef, were of a very high quality indeed. The slightly plain descriptions on the menu and the very reasonable prices gave no hint of the delights that awaited you. Everybody seemed pleased with their meals - a very rare experience in any restaurant. The cooked breakfast was generally good and the continental one adequate.
Our stay coincided with The London-Brighton Veteran Car Rally and I would recommend anyone participating in or spectating at this event to give this hotel a try. Don't be put off that it is not in Brighton itself as it is well-located and has free parking. And you will probably get a much better night's sleep than many of my fellow reviewers have done when staying in Brighton!
A good value 3* hotel with friendly staff and great evening meals

3 Star hotel 5 STAR SERVICE. My wife and I had a brilliant stay. Patricia Cole on reception was outstanding. Room was spotless. Valentine night restaurant was full but the service was still great. Cannot believe this is only a 3 star hotel. I have stayed at much more expensive hotels that were not as good. I will be returning and will recommend this hotel to my friends. Do not confuse this hotel with the Travellodge up the road

very relaxing weekend. Stayed friday and saturday night nice room , very pleasant and friendly staff . We ate in the Bistro restaurant both nights and really enjoyed the food. We were glad we chose to stay outside Brighton as it was a lovely quiet location. Would definately return

Nice Place to Stay. Conveniently located to explore South Downs and Brighton.

We stayed at The Hickstead on the Tesco Clubcard Deals scheme and were not disappointed. Firstly the Classic Lodges web site displays the price of a leisure break clearly and this makes it easy to order your vouchers ( unlike some other hotel chains where the price you pay on the clubcard deals scheme is far more than the price on their web sites).

The rooms at the hotel are fairly characterless but this did not matter to us because:
1. The rooms are a decent size
2. The bed was extremely comfortable
3. The en-suite had a decent bath and nice toiletries
4. Liked the standard items such as tea/coffee machine, hairdryer and trouser press
5. Especially liked free WiFi & Sky Sports on the TV ( although no mini bar )

We were initially worried about the noise from a wedding party but actually never heard a thing all night and always had a feeling that the guests were courteous to each other.

We ate at the hotel one evening and were pleasantly surprised at the quality ( Sea Bass was well cooked and imaginatively presented ) and wished we had eaten there the second night ( don't recommend the cheese and biscuits option though as the cheese wasn't particularly inspiring). Breakfasts were very good with a buffet style range of cereals and fruits and then full English - Delicious.

As for the local area, we enjoyed Borde Hill Gardens ( azaleas and rhododendrons were a delight in May ) and the walks at The Jack and Jill Windmills and Devils Dyke with sweeping views of the Downs.

Overall The Hickstead was a nice place to stay and I would definitely stay there again

Nice hotel but lacks character. Stayed Thurs/Fri night with my partner using Tesco club card. We felt slightly disappointed on arrival as the Hotel didn't match up to the way it was portrayed on the website. We were expecting the rooms to be within the house itself and not in a purpose built modern annex and expected more to have been made of the "7 acres" on which it is situated. However we thoroughly enjoyed our stay. The rooms were large, clean and well equiped although characterless with little view. The staff were very friendly and helpful. The food in the Bistro was excellent! and the breakfast was very good too. The hotel is well situated for many local attractions within easy reach

Better than expected. It's worth noting that this hotel is not in Bolney, and if you take the Bolney exit off the A23 you will get lost! You need to go two junctions further south on the A23 then take the Burgess Hill exit. Once you find it the place is OK, very quiet countryside location, friendly attentive staff and reasonable food. The room was clean, and excellent water pressure in the shower

Lovely Food and Service. I have stayed at this hotel in the past on business.  I stayed in March 2010 for one night.  I arrived after a 3.5 hour drive and check in was smooth and hassle free.  The room I stayed in was of a good size and included a large dressing table a small table and two chairs.  When I stayed here previously the room I was in had a small sofa rather than a table and chairs.  I ordered room service and the food arrive quickly and was cooked to perfection.  I have to say that the food at the Hickstead is lovely.  The bed was comfortable and I had a good nights sleep.

I had breakfast delivered to my room the following morning and the one downside was that this was delivered a little later than I had requested.

I have stayed in various hotels on business and find that the Hickstead is one of the better hotel I stay at.  I will most certainly be staying here again

Weekend Stay Outside Brighton. Stayed on The Spring and Summer Saver which was excellent value for money.  Staff were extremely attentive and couldn't do enough for you, the food was excellent value for money and very good quality, with an excellent choice.  Rooms were very comfortable, although the rooms at the back did not have the good views at the front of the hotel.  Would have liked air con as it was a very warm weekend but apart from that an extremely enjoyable weekend will definitely going back

Fantastic Wedding Venue with Excellent Staff. Bit late writing this review!  We got married at the Hickstead in February this year and cannot fault the staff or venue.  Giles was our main contact from the initial booking through to the event itself and was fabulous.  The staff were unflappable and dealt with leaking babies nappies and vomiting children without fuss and so unobtrusively that those who hadn't seen the "accident" were totally unaware it had occurred.  A small party arrived the night before and we had a fantastic meal in the restaurant.  Rooms were a good size, clean and very pleasant.  Wedding Breakfast was excellent.  Most guests stayed overnight and we all enjoyed an excellent breakfast.  Highly recommend the venue for a place to stay or a wedding.  We will definitely return

Quite simply too expensive for what it is!. Overall I would give this two stars and only just that! I will explain:

Character : 0/5. It looks and feels like a Nuffield hospital, but a but more shabby. 

Rooms : 2/5. Decent size, but no coffee table to sit at, just a single chair in the corner and a plastic shower curtain aaaaarrrgh !!!!!!  There is absolutely no excuse fir curtains in any bathrooms, especially ones at £130 a night!

View and grounds : 1/5 ignore the 7 acres, as they only use about 1 which is made up of carpark and a few bushes. The view from my (and many other) windows was of an overflow carpark, a few trees and an I austral unit! See the photo for proof! 

Food and restaurant : 2/5. Even though others have rated this aspect, I have to disagree. Whilst the service and staff were very good, the quality was poor. The atmosphere in the restaurant in the evening was very "bright lights and 80's " Come on Hickstead you can improve that with a bit if thought and effort !!

Location: 4/5. To be fair this is all that is has going for it at present, along with nice supportive staff. Great access to Brighton (15 mins) and easy to find. 

Overall, we were very disappointed with our trip and felt that if it had not been with the Tesco vouchers we would have been absolutely gutted. With them was bad enough!  The hickstead need to be realistic, it is a very small hotel with no character and a dated interior. Not what I want to spend £130 a night on!

Friendly staff and all round good hotel.. We stayed at The Hickstead for a weekend getaway and to explore surrounding areas including Brighton.  

Firstly the booking process (using clubcard vouchers) was quick and easy.  When we got there the reception staff were welcoming and friendly.  We were pleased with the hotel room and general standard of the hotel.  It was very clean and comfortable and the bedroom and bathroom were also decorated to a high standard. 

We had breakfast twice and dinner in the hotel once.  The food was to a very good standard and the service from the staff was again excellent.

I would recommend this hotel to others and would be happy to stay there again!

Good Job. Hotel in handy location just off A23, in Jobs Lane, about 10 miles from Brighton.  Easy to park, and nice hospitality is the winning feature of the hotel.  Had a good deal with the winter warmer offer for dinner, bed and breakfast, plus a bottle of prosecco, and we could pick anything we liked from the a la carte menu for dinner!

Feels like an upmarket Premier Inn. Room was supposed to be a superior but apart from an additional table and two chairs it was nothing more than a room you could expect from the likes of Premier Inn. The hotel employs that many chefs who all have their own takes on the menu that it is possible as happened to us that no two dishes will be the same. When it was queried with the waiter I was told, after he had consulted with the chef, that the chefs vary the dishes because of complaints from regulars that the meals are always the same??? (surely you change the menu not each dish). Having said all that the food was good

Did the job. This place did the job as we needed somewhere to stay near Brighton. We used Tesco vouchers otherwise it would have been £135 per night, which does seem very expensive. The rooms were nothing special, the hotel is not particularly attractive from the outside and the view from our window was of an industrial estate.

However, the bedroom was clean, the bed was comfy, the breakfast was very nice and all the staff we dealt with were incredibly pleasant and helpful, which made up for any gripes I may have had. The customer service at every point really was superb and it's a pity that more hotels in the UK can't follow suit

Fantastic Value. Clean hotel with excellent service. Wasn't sure what to expect but very impressed. The room was big, clean and had everything you'd expect from a good hotel. We wanted somewhere to stay whilst visiting the local vineyard and glad we chose this hotel. A taxi to the vineyard was about £10. We paid £99 for dinner, bottle of wine, bed and breakfast direct with the hotel. Food was ok, waitress was very helpful and friendly

Good food, nice room, good service. We've stayed here before and had up and down experiences. Happily this was an up visit!

The staff were welcoming and the dinner and breakfast service was good.

It's had at least one previous owner, if not 3 or 4. The present management are doing better on strength of our one night stay, although an excellent rating is not quite justified - some of the decor in corridors and the room was looking slightly tired, but not seriously so

Very Very Nice. We stayed 4 nights at this hotel.   We got a really good deal -   £400  for the room,  breakfast,
dinner every night, and a bottle of wine every night.    Breakfast was excellent -  they had
everything.  The dinner menu was also very good,  with specials every night, although
these were a bit repetitive.  The portions of food were too big --  unusual for UK. The room and bathroom were nice and clean.  The hotel is set in a lovely rural area,  but not too far 
from anything.  Not sure how noisy it could be when they have weddings,  conferences etc.
The staff were really friendly,  and extremely helpful.   We would definitely stay here again.
We paid the bill when we checked out, not before

Could not be better. Thank you so much to all the wonderful staff here, we stayed a few nights when our new granddaughter was born at Haywards Heath hospital. 
The kindness and service was exceptional (I own my own Travel Agency so I have visited many hotels from hostels to 7* over the years).
It's a good quality 3* in terms of rooms/decor and amenities, but in terms of customer service it's 5* and above.
We felt welcome at all times, the cleaners were among the best that I have ever experienced.
Will we return - yes 100% we will next time we visit our family.
Thank you so much

A chain hotel that thinks it is a family concern. This hotel benefits from its proximity to two places. Hickstead, where many equestrian events take place and Burgess Hill, where commercial pilots train. The relationship between London Gatwick pilot training and the hotel is very good and much of their business is derived from training pilots. I understand their courses last for 40 days; that sounds like good business to me but obviously the hotel had to earn their reputation.
The hotel stands on grounds in the middle of fields without any particular attraction to them a few hundred yards from a main road where traffic is fairly noisy during the day, though not disturbing at night. It is a small hotel, with a restaurant with very good chefs and you were greeted on entry with politeness and helpfulness.
There was no beer on tap when I arrived, and there was no Guinness so I had to make do with a gin and tonic my partner had some bottled beer. For a large gin and tonic, a bottle of beer and two packets of peanuts we paid £12.60 so the prices are pretty standard for mid-level hotels in my experience. I was glad they kept the background music down in the lounge, it is after all called background music not foreground music.
Our room was large enough the shower was delightful and the mattress very comfortable.
We were on special offer £99. double DBB and we did not have to stick to a 'special' menu so we could take our choice from anything on the main bistro menu. That was a big plus. We also had a bottle of very decent wine.
In short, I find this hotel unpretentious, friendly, and does the job. Deborah, our waitress for the breakfast, had been working for the place for seven years and had a deep love and affection for it. Her mother was in charge of room service so it is a family thing. Yes, I would stay there again.
PS they are already booked out for Hickstead next year

Staff, Hotel, Food Great. Stayed for one night as went to the local wine tasting event.  Booking was easy and very friendly. Upon arrival staff welcoming, ordered a taxi for us to local vineyard, room spacious, clean.  Staff from the receptionist, bar and restaurant staff were fantastic and no fault there.
The only negative would be the decor of the place, a little dated (apart from the bar and restaurant) but this no way spoilt our stay. Food was excellent and really good portions, the breakfast choice was great either hot or cold so choices for all.  Where hotel situated was great and although we only stayed for one night it really felt like we went away. Would come again and recommend.  I would like to add well done to the hotel as whole with their service and staff.  MANY THANKS
Nice comfortable and homely.. Nice quiet place. Good for pilots visiting CAE. Recommended for long stays

Nice hotel, very pleasant staff. Stayed here one night for my husband’s birthday on the dinner, bed and breakfast package. Check in was quick and easy. Room was clean. Dinner portions were large but the food was plain and breakfast was very simple. Was slightly disappointed as when booking I was assured that dinner staff would make a bit of a fuss over my husband for his birthday, but no one did as much as say happy birthday.
Still hotel staff were friendly, professional and polite. There was a large tourist group the night we stayed but the hotel was still peaceful and quiet. Free wifi was also included. For what we paid it was good value for money, especially considering it was a bank holiday weekend

a real friendly caring hotel. Stayed for a week due to a family illness. This hotel has an excellent staff, rooms were cleaned efficiently, restaurant service first class and welcoming and food was thoroughly enjoyed by us all.. Staff were pleasant and friendly and went out of their way to help us when we needed it. I would definately stay there again and would recommend this hotel just on it's relaxed friendly atmosphere. The staff were a definate plus side to our stay I just wish it had been under better cicumstances. Thankyou from us all

Brilliant weekend. We stayed at The Hickstead for 2 nights (Sat-Sun) in August. We got a great deal through Classic Lodges which got us a free three course dinner in the on-site restaurant and a choice of either cream tea or a bottle of wine. We chose the cream tea. Our second night was just bed and breakfast but that was also a fairly reasonable price as we booked in advance. 
The room was very nice and comfortable. Our bed was very firm, which is not to everyone’s taste but I would rather have that than too soft, and it is better for your back. I slept pretty well. The bathroom was clean and had a great shower. The toiletries were lovely too. The hotel decor is fairly modern and most of the rooms are in a modern extension not the older main building, but we didn’t have a problem with that. The restaurant was really nice and the service was of a very high standard. I suffer from a food allergy and the lady who was serving us was extremely helpful in making sure my food was safe for me to eat. Although I thought my main course was only okay (I had a burger), you could tell the food was fresh and good quality. My pudding (apple pie) was delicious.  Breakfast was also really good. It was plentiful and very tasty. The surrounding countryside is pretty, yet you are a short drive from the main road which is convenient. I’m not sure whether there are places to go close by which you could walk to, we went everywhere by car. Brighton is about half an hour away. 
I would definitely recommend The Hickstead for a comfortable and relaxing stay

Friendly Hotel. Stayed here August Bank Holiday Weekend-for a party .This is one of the most staff friendly hotels I have ever stayed in nothing was too much trouble. Small hotel. Room clean ,comfortable bed, everything you need in room..Dinner in restaurant was good value, again staff first class. Breakfast was good plenty varitey & lots  of it......., just avoid scrambled eggs,!!! would have liked a choice of eggs.Again staff were first class,Overall would def recommend this hotel & would stay here again

GREAT FOOD!!. We stayed here for one night, the room was very clean and comfortable, the only problem we found was insufficient lighting- perhaps a little too ambient!! We had dinner in the restaurant and it was fabulous! three courses and not one complaint and possibly the best shortbread we have tasted!. Breakfast was just as good, plenty on the plate and very tasty. Staff are very friendly and helpful and although there was a wedding reception on we were not disturbed at all, would certainly recommend this hotel and very much look forward to staying here again

"GREAT GETAWAY". My husband and I stayed here for one night to celebrate our wedding anniversary, the room was very clean and comfortable. We had a three course dinner in the restaurant which was excellent (treacle tart best ever) Buffet breakfast was just as good. Staff are very friendly and helpful and although there was a wedding reception on we were not disturbed at all, would certainly recommend this hotel and very much look forward to staying here again

The Easy Getaway.... Situated just off the A23, it was near where we wanted to be. Far enough away from the main roads to be quiet enough to hear the quacking ducks in the nearby pond. Rooms were a decent size; we had the double + single family room with a rear facing aspect. No aircon but the window was sufficient and quiet enough. Looks like a popular special occasion venue, weddings etc. but we were not disturbed by the Wedding in full flight on the Saturday we stayed. There might be a slight concern over proximity to water/open windows for bug life during the summer but as we stayed in autumn, not an issue.
Only issue was with the TV signal dropping out every now and again - but who stays at a hotel to watch the TV? Countered by the excellent free Wi-Fi throughout the hotel (or at least where we went). Overall the hotel is a little dated compared to some 'chain' hotels but was clean and well managed. Breakfast was adequate and makes a change to get waiter service these days (instead of the more common buffet for hot food).
Good value for money. We paid £79 for B&B for the 2 of us booked online. Would stay again

Not what you expect. It's not glamourous, no comfy lounge, lighting too bright. 

But the team work very hard to keep guests happy and looked after. 

Hassan and Patricia work long hours and very hard, always with a smile. 

£99 room, dinner (inc wine) and breakfast - a bargain

Make an excuse to stay here. This is a fantastic hotel - and you should find an excuse to stay here.

In order of growing 'impressiveness':-

1. The restaurant and bar are pleasant and well furnished. There seems to be a few locals that dine in the restaurant as well as the guests - which is always a good sign. Both had a good buzz without being crowded.
2. The rooms looked freshly decorated (in modern greys and browns) and were of a good size. Beds were comfortable and there was free wi-fi that worked well. Everything was spotless.
3. The food in the restaurant was absolutely fantastic. Although I ate there the same night as a large wedding reception was being held in the hotel I was served very quickly (less than 50 minutes from sitting down to finishing a 2 course meal) and the food quality was superb.
4. The staff are astonishingly good here - probably the best level of service I have experienced (an that includes a large number of 4 and 5 star hotels). They provide a very very professional service but they also think about you as a guest. Here are a few examples; 
a. when I booked in they asked me if wanted dinner. When I said that I couldn't as I was working until 02:00 in the morning she asked me what time I was going to sleep to in the morning so she could make sure housekeeping didn't wake me up.
b. After dinner on the second night the waitress reminded me of the times for breakfast. When I said that I would be away working too early to get breakfast - she went out of her way to try and arrange something very early for me.
c. During dinner an elderly couple arrived for dinner. They could not read the menu as they did not have any reading glasses, so the waitress gave them her reading glasses for 5 minutes to read the menu.

All in all a lovely place to stay - with truly world class staff. Unfortunately for me the work I was doing in this area is now complete - so I am looking for an excuse to go back and stay here. 

One minor gripe - Trouser Presses! Trouser presses remind me of some second rate has-been hotel that is grasping for ratings with the English Tourist Board. Does anyone ever use these ridiculous things? The Hickstead doesn't need to grasp for ratings - it already is top class. Please sell them on eBay and use the cash to by an iron and ironing board to stick in the wardrobe

Celebration Evening. Another party evening, this time at the Hickstead Hotel, and not too far from home for a change.

Hotels seems a reasonable place - staff are very good and attentive. Food and entertainment were first class, although the room was not very full (about 30 persons I guess in total).

Rooms are basic but OK - adequate for the price. Only niggle was that the TV remote didn't work, but that was nothing.

The only downside is a rather strange aroma to the place. Strongest in the reception area, one of my companions said it reminded them of an old people's home - not what I would have said, but there is something there.

Location is excellent - just off the A23 means it has good links to Brighton and the coast, as well as Gatwick.

All in all, a good working hotel - not glamourous but they look after the guests and the things that count are done well

visiting family. good hotel, good location, friendly staff, great english breakfast, good value.
room was too dark due to poor lighting, (simple to fix)!!
will stay again

Afternoon Tea at The Hickstead Hotel. Just returned home after having a very pleasant 'afternoon tea'.  We felt the need to express our thanks to the staff who looked after us and who made it a very warm and pleasant experience.  A good choice of sandwiches coupled with victoria sponge, strawberry meringues, chocolate eclairs and jam and cream scone all washed down with a nice pot (or two!) of tea.  Now its time for a snooze.  Definitely will recommend to friends

Afternoon Tea. I should like to post my agreement with J.W who had afternoon tear at The Hickstead recently. We also had tea there yesterday and it was fantastic. It was a gift from a family member, arranged through the Activity Superstore and we can highly recommend The Hickstead for friendliness and ambiance as well as the quality and flavour of the food. It was delicious and we could not eat it all, far superior to a previous experience elsewhere

Functional & perfectly fine. This place is not somewhere I’d go out of my way to travel for pleasure, but that doesn’t mean I wouldn’t stay here again.

For business this place is perfectly acceptable and the local competition is either a Travelodge or a pub. The rate I paid was very fair for a UK hotel today.

The rooms however are a little dated and the bathrooms look a little tired; and I have to agree with a previous report about poor lighting in the room. Although this wasn’t a problem for me, as I spent very little time in my room. But when I did the bed was comfortable and the room quiet. 

I can’t comment on breakfast, but the dinner I had was OK. Decent whitebait and an average steak, but a poor dessert. The dining room and bar area is clean and function, but (unlike the bedrooms) heavily lit.

The staff are very good though. Polite, helpful, attentive and a credit to the owner.

I’d stay again if in the area

Wonderful Hotel. Stayed with Friends for Husbands 60th - the Staff are second to none with nothing being too much trouble - Evening Meal was excellent as was Breakfast being as good as any hotel we have stayed in before.

Would have no trouble recommending this hotel where you will be made to feel welcome and very special - Thanks to you all at The Hickstead - will see you again!
The Glovers

Great Hotel. We originally stayed at the Hickstead as we were attending a wedding there in September 2010.  The hotel put on a great spread (I was Best Man!).  My wife and I have returned twice since as a getaway gift for her birthday.  Jenny (who was toastmaster at the wedding) remembered us each time and made us very welcome, as did her daughter, Hannah!  The restaurant serves excellent food.  Cannot fault the hotel in any respect, and we inturn to return again.
We certainly would not hesitate to recommend this hotel

Excellent Staff. We stayed for 2 nights on a package arranged for us.  What stood out for us about the weekend was the friendliness and helpfulness of all the staff.  Everyone went out of their way to make sure you had everything you need and with smiles that were genuine!  We were especially impressed with the lady who was in charge of the dining room in the evening - I think her name was Jenny.  She was extremely busy with all sorts of different Menus to cater for but remained jolly at all times. The food was really well cooked we couldn't fault anything we had.  The menu was a bit "safe" but did give choice.  It is a shame that on the Sunday it was a buffet breakfast because the lovely breakfast cooked really well by the staff then lost some of it's appeal as it had got a bit cold. Our room was lovely - plenty of room and very comfortable.  Our only slight criticism, and it is only slight was the atmosphere in the dining room in the evening.  It seemed a bit stark due to the lighting being too bright.  Just a bit of dimming of the lights and maybe tablecloths on the tables for the evening would make all the difference

Oh My Aching Back. Nice enough hotel and conveniently close to the A23 but not too close. The service quality was acceptable but no more. Perhaps the best thing about the Hickstead was the breakfast that was included in the room rate - freshly prepared to order.
However, the bed was excruciatingly hard and it was impossible to get comfortable. My wife and I would have been more comfortable sleeping on the floor! Very disappointing

Convenient, clean place to stay. Clean and convenient place to stay if travelling. Plenty of car park space. 
Not tested the dinners but breakfast is good. Plenty to choose from including a cooked breakfast if needed. 
Rooms look a little dated but are clean

Hickstead Visit. We stayed at The Hickstead last weekend.  Room was clean and modern.  Breakfast very good and plentiful.  Staff extremely friendly.

Only thing that spoilt it for us, we went for a quiet weekend.  However, there was a wedding on Saturday evening.  The wedding party completely took over the public areas of the hotel and so there was no-where for non-party goers to escape from the revellers. I think that this should be something that is advised at booking

Hickstead Hotel overnight stay. Nice location just off the A23 but in the sticks a bit. Nice warm welcome from friendly and helpful staff. Very pleasant evening meal and breakfast.Plenty of choice and amount. Spacious, clean, warm room with everything you need. Only negative was a hard bed. Wouldn't stop me from staying again though. Excellent value for money

HICKSTEAD HOTEL. Excellent overnight stay - staff at reception and in the restaurant were friendly and helpful.  Both dinner and breakfast were excellent.  Room was spacious and clean.  Highly recommend

Ticks all the Boxes. Stayed here for 3 nights, the staff were brill, extremely helpfull and pleasent. Rooms were good size (bathroom bit small but adequate) excellent shower and nice toiletries. Well stocked tea tray and somewhere to sit, bed bit hard but you get used to it :-) very quiet and slept well. Was a wedding on one eve but we were not disturbed at all by it. Breakfast was cooked fresh to order 2 out of the 3 morns and was excellent. Easy to get into London by train from Burgess Hill and would also use train or park and ride to go into Brighton as carparks really expensive. Would love to stay here again

Hickstead stay. Great experience, excellent service, comfortable clean rooms. Only negative was the overpowering strong smell of the toilet near reception which created a smell in the upstairs corridor of a men's urinal !

great little hotel. stayed here for a week in sept 11. rooms were great size with good bathroom size. It was always clean. The food was excellent and filling, great choice also. You could eat in the bar area too if you wanted to watch the football on tv

Nice, clean and picturesque. Stayed at the Hickstead Hotel for a wedding and found it to be very sufficient for our needs. The staff were friendly and the location was lovely. It was convenient to take a taxi wherever needed, some places were within walking distance, if you're up for a scenic hike. Recommend the afternoon tea, it was very nice! Compared to other hotels seen in the area, this was truly a great choice.
Some of the details, like carpeting and paint in public areas of the hotel could be touched up (carpet viewed behind the reception desk) and ceiling in the suite bathroom (also, shower curtain was irritatingly clingy when water was running) but other than that, it was peaceful, quaint and pretty. Beds were comfortable and the suite had lots of room.
Continental breakfast in our room was a great option for a small fee. We didn't try the restaurant because of the wedding festivities but the smell was delicious.
All in all, a brilliant choice and would stay again!

quiet weekend?. Just got back from a weekend here.

The plus points - very clean and smart everywhere.  Excellent service, breakfast and room.  Staff all very smartly-dressed, clean and polite.  The surrounding fields were pleasant, with geese and rabbits outside.  Plenty of car-parking spaces.  Well-stocked tea tray in the room.  Powerful shower.  Nice coplimentary toiletries.

The down-side - The drinks were expensive.  2 pints of beer and 2 Smirnoff Ice, £20!  We didn't eat dinner here, as the prices were steep for what the meals were.  There was a wedding on yesterday.  Fine, until the evening, when it would have been impossible to sit in the bar anyway.  The party went on until 2am today, and from when we went to bed at 11, until 2am, there were wedding guests backwards and forwards along the corridors, visiting each others' rooms, knocking on each others' doors, kids running about, people shouting, etc.  NOT relaxing.  This was a weekend away that our son had booked as a gift for us.  DON'T go when there is a function on!

Wedding Weekend at the Hickstead. Stayed at the Hickstead Hotel for 2 nights as a couple for a family wedding. The location is very scenic, the room was spacious and very clean.
We ate in the restaurant on the first night, had 2 breakfasts and the meal at the wedding on all occasions the food was 1st class.
The service from the staff was very good nothing was too much to do.
Overall it was a very enjoyable stay

A lovely hotel. I have just enjoyed a two night stay at The Hickstead
This is a comfotable and friendly place to stay, the rooms are a good size   and very clean.The food in the restaurant is delicious and nicely presented with a good choice and variety of dishes.
The best thing about The Hickstead is the staff ,they are all so friendly and welcoming and make sure that you have everything you need, service is very efficient but personal.
This is the second time I have stayed here and the standards have been consistent it is a lovely hotel.

What a gem!. My husband and I have had a two night stay at the Hickstead and throughly enjoyed our visit.  The staff are all friendly and efficient.  Jenny in the restaurant was especially first class and an asset to the hotel. The food was very well presented and delicious, with a good variety and choice on the menu.  Our room was clean, comfortable and spacious.  We liked the ample tea tray and toiletries in the room. Although there was a party downstairs during our stay, this did not impact on our evening at all, either in the bar or noise within our bedroom.  We agreed that we would like to return for another stay at this hotel and will probably explore the other hotels within the same group
Nice hotel pritty setting. Was given this hotel stay as a gift was in a lovely pritty setting not to far frpm brighton lots of nice little pubs to visit would stay again

Weekend at the Hickstead. Just returned from a great 2 night break at the Hickstead.  On arrival it was obvious that good customer service was paramount,  the reception staff was very friendly & check in was seamless.  A special mention goes to Jenny, nothing was too much for her, she provided a first class service to all the guests, certainly an asset to the hotel! Room was very clean & spacious and had everything you needed for a enjoyable weekend

Perfect stay!. Two nights - couldn't fault it! Clean, comfortable room, just the right size. Staff with a perfect blend of friendliness and efficiency - very difficult combination to achieve - and made it feel like a family-run hotel. We received careful individual attention, from booking to arrival and right through our stay. The food was superb - way above the hotel's star rating - and the scrambled eggs . well, I wish I could make them taste as good! As for dinner - superb quality and wonderful service. Lovely countryside location - try a walk down the country lanes (you might need it after the dinner!). 
We didn't want to leave and we'll stay again

such friendly staff. we just wanted a few nights away fairly near home following a slipped disc. Found this hotel and the customer reviews helped us chose it. The staff couldn't have been more helpful, polite and friendly. Would highly recommend it for a short stay near Brighton, Lewes etc. We loved Rottingdean. Hannah, Deborah and jenny were especially helpful

quiet peaceful hotel. Was told about Classic Lodges website froma friend of ours and booked one of their special offers at this Hotel. from the moment we got there we were made welcome and met by friendly staff. Being disabled and in a wheelciar we had booked an evening meal here to save going out looking for somwhere to get in  and all thought there was step up into the restaurant this was no problem for the staff who had a ramp in place before we arrived for our meal. the food was excellent and you wasnt rushed with the meal even with the restaurtant being busy with a large party of guests. all this made for a great and peaceful weekend for me and my wife.

If there is a negative which i hate to add after such a great time, and i only add this for any other disabled guests who use this Hotel.  And it is that in the bathroom you are unable to get under the sink to wash as it is all build in, there is also an hand rail in front of the sink and the only way i could wash was sideways on which is not easy. if you dont need a wheelchair and disabled then i dont feel this is a problem for you. Apart from this all other acess is great and like i said above the staff are more then happy to help you in anyway they can

Afternoon tea. Four of us went to the Hickstead for afternoon tea on Sunday afternoon. We were made very comfortable in easy chairs by a bay window. Two of our party were vegetarians. The tea was served first and was supplied with plenty of hot water for top ups. We asked the waitress twice for extra tea and coffee and this was served with no extra charge.
The vegetarian tea stand was served seperately from the normal one with fresh cakes. We were given egg mayonaise, fresh ham, and smoked salmon sandwiches and the vegetarian option was cheese and pickle, cheese salad, and egg mayonaise sandwiches. There were warm fruit scones with jam and cream (not clotted), chocolate elair, strawberry merange, lemon drizzle cake and shortbread.
We were there nearly two hours and were not hurried. All in all, very enjoyable

Such a great Hotel, with the most attentive staff. I celebrated my 40th Birthday here with 20 of my family. The hotel was very clean and the rooms very comfy. Dinner was brilliant as was the breakfast. The staff here are utterly amazing Patricia who took so much effort in booking my stay and to get it just right for us, Jenny who got 20 people organised for dinner and had everything working like clockwork and Hannah for being so attentive the next morning. Nothing was too much trouble, the bar stayed open until we couldnt possible drink anymore and I had asked for tea and coffe when we arrived and this was set out lovely.I had a fantastic time here made perfect by the lovely staff and hotel. Thank you so much

Our Experience. Hotel, quiet location, clean & tidy, 
Room, Clean spacious & Comfortable.
Restaurant, Frendly service, Menu good Choice & food was GREAT.

Barman, was a colourful & inntresting.

Really Enjoyed the stay

Short Break. This was our second and my fourth visit to The Hickstead Hotel, We booked our stay by email which was quickly and curteously answered by Patricia, we asked to extend our stay this was not a problem, we were contacted a few days before we were due to arrive to confirm the time of our dinner reservation as the restaurant was getting booked up and they wanted to ensure there was a table available at the time we wanted to eat, thats service! During our stay we found the Hotel to be clean and tidy throughout, the service we received from the team was excellent; Special Thanks to Jenny and the Deputy Manager (sorry didnt get your name) who went the extra mile, Would happily recommend this Hotel to anyone wishing to stay in a quiet location with good food and excellent service:

Hotel Overnight. This was our third visit to the hotel since christmas as we need to visit the hospital in Haywards Heath which is about 15 minutes away. Every single time the whole stay has been excellent. All of the staff are so friendly and cannot do enough for you. Particularly the restaurant staff. They really go out of their way to make your stay very welcoming.Rooms are nice and food is excellent both dinner and breakfast and the free wine included in the meal was nice.The rooms are very clean. Thank you for an excellent stay

Great Staff - Rooms tired. Stayed for a night a couple of months ago on business and whilst the rooms were clean and well equipped mine was looking a little tired. Staff were great, especially the waitress in the restuarant that evening who looked after our table of 8 brilliantly and was back next morning when I went for a very early breakfast. We stayed on an offer with dinner and wine included which made this hotel great value

A mid range Hotel. We only stayed one night at this hotel, the greeting at reception was very friendly and welcoming. Our room was on the second floor at the end of a long corridor, it was very clean but very like lots of chain hotels, I had not expected this, we booked via late rooms and I had not realised it was part of a group. The photo looked like it was an old house and does not show the modern accomodation at the side. Having said all that our room was very comfortable, very warm but we managed to part open a window. In the dining room the staff were so friendly and helpful, nothing seemed too much trouble. We had a very nice breakfast, there was plenty of it, it could have been hotter, but unless you are at the table when food is replenished you have what is under the warmer. We would stay at this hotel again if we could get a deal but would not want to pay the full price advertised at £130 for B & B

Very good value mid range hotel.. We got a deal in a comfy smart deluxe room on a Saturday with dinner including bottle of wine  and breakfast for £129. Slippers, dressing gowns included. The staff are one of The Hicksteads key assets. Without fail all were charming and genuinely keen to please. 
The location is down a quite lane with fields, ducks, geese and large numbers of rabbits all enjoying what the area had to offer. Close to the A23 but very little traffic noise in the grounds and none at all in the room. 
The dinner  had a good choice and most items were included in the deal. Only one starter and a mains required any form of supplemet. As well as the main menu there was a specails menu with another good selection and we mixed and matched happily. The wine was just a chardonnay, merlot or rose. We fancied none of these so went for a Pinot Grogio and agreed to pay any extra costs. There weren't any.
The room was smart and a good size. Bathroom clean and modern with an excellent shower and super fast bath-don't leave it for more than a few seconds! One slight quibble-no conditioner and us ladies like that. Bed very comfy.
There was a wedding and that could have been noisy but heavy doors were shut and this cut all noise off.
Breakfast the next morning was delicious. All hands were to the pump and it was good to see the manager mucking in with all the other staff who were agian unfailingly polite and helpful. Training is good at this hotel. Jolly good bacon by the way!

All in all a lovely short weekend break in a lovely part of the world. The hotel is very good at what it does and its staff add that bit extra. It's not luxury nor does it pretend to be but it thoroughly desreves its high TA ratings

Incredible customer service. Whether it was at reception or in the restaurant, both at dinner and at breakfast, the customer service at this hotel was excellent.  We felt welcomed and respected, during every aspect of our stay.  Nothing was too much trouble, and all staff were extremely friendly.  The hotel was clean although it could probably do with a coat of paint soon, but at this time all businesses are having to tighten belts.  We felt everything possible was being done to improve the customers' experience

Fantastic Staff. We stayed at this hotel for one night on our way to Brighton,overall great value for money and the food and staff were excellant.They were so helpful and genuinely welcoming (not just forced) nothing was too much trouble. The Hotel is a little tired looking and had no air con in the rooms, but apart from that we would def stay again.
The staff make this hotel, thank you

good quality hotel and customer service. Very good room on the second floor 9/10 for cleaniness. Customer service was exceptional. This hotel might have a feel and look of a mid-range hotel but it deserves more than an AA 3star rating. A Visit England 4 star silver award would be more suited

Travel review. We stayed overnight as my daughter's cricket team used the hotel before their game the next day. It was a really good experience, a pity there was no air- conditioning. Staff were great naturally without going over the top

Lovely staff, good value if using Explore Sussex package. Quiet location.  Customer service was very good, all staff were extremely helpful and seem to enjoy working at the hotel.  Dinner was very good value as part of the Explore Sussex package.

Our room was a good size,  clean, but seemed a little dark and "tired".  Will be a real gem after re-decoration

Wedding hotel. We stayed at the Hickstead Hotel while attending our niece's wedding. The reception was held at the hotel and the evening function.  Both were well organised, food good, staff friendly and helpful, and the grounds lent themselves for wedding photographs.  Our room was on the ground floor and was comfortable and well appointed - our only comment was that only one set of towels was put in the bathroom, while the second set was left in the wardrobe.
Overall a good experience and a hotel which I could recommend

Good hotel with great value package. Two nights mid-week at this hotel at the beginning of August using their "Discover Sussex" package - amazingly good value at £99 for three course dinner for two, bottle of house wine and double room and full English breakfasts. The meals were well prepared, with plenty of range and generous portions - unlike some places that have a restricted choice (Premier Inn's package comes to mind), the full restaurant menu was available to chose from, although there were one or two items within each course that attracted a supplement if you chose them. To be honest, the quantity of food was more than we could manage by the second evening! The staff were really good, and as the food is freshly prepared they coped admirably with a couple of special requests from us (and we heard others being treated equally as well). Congratulations to the restaurant manager who is clearly passionate about customer service!

I was a bit worried about the location as it appears to be close to the A23; but it is actually set back enough for the road noise to be a distant buzz rather than a raucous roar. With the windows closed in the East facing rooms it could not be heard, although with the windows open it was faintly audible.

The reason I've only given this hotel four out of five is that we felt the room wasn't quite perfect. All the hotel's bedrooms are accommodated in a modern block (see picture by "thekings4" taken May 2012) whilst the reception, restaurant, bar and meeting rooms are in the older house; the bedrooms would be familiar in size, style and layout to anyone who has stayed in the modern Premier Inn or Travelodge chains. The rooms are either ground or first floor and they are very generously proportioned. We asked for a quiet room and were given room 210, first floor, East facing at the end of the corridor adjacent to the South facing premium rate rooms. The room has much darker décor than the room shown in pictures on their website and other traveller's photos here - and combined with the absence of overhead lighting and the use of energy saving lamps in the wall lights this made for a dingy room. There was a small table and two chairs in the bay window; the table lamp which was on the table might have helped with the overall ambience but there wasn't a socket close enough to plug it in to! The king-size bed was equipped with a 10.5 tog duvet, which for us is a winter weight one, and the arrival of summer temperatures and the lack of air conditioning made for an uncomfortably hot night. The second night we slept with the bay window open, but were woken by geese at the adjacent fishery around dawn and then (but only having been wakened) became aware of the background hum of traffic noise. Of course, once the peak of summer has passed, temperature will no longer be an issue and other travellers might not be as affected as we were on our stay. One final criticism is that there was no obvious clock anywhere. Many hotels now-a-days have clock-alarms with i-Pad/i-Phone docking stations, and hopefully this hotel will follow suit soon.

Having said that, on the positive side there was plenty of storage to unpack and hang clothes on, the free Wi-Fi worked a treat, the flat screen TV was good, the bathroom was adequately sized, the shower worked effectively and there was a good range of toiletries supplied.

Overall, the customer service was excellent, the food fabulous, the location good and the package rate fantastic value - we would use it again, though probably not in peak summer months and we might be more choosy about the room allocated (unless room 210 hasn't yet been redecorated).

PS - If you've read RoundedGriffin's review, it focusses on a swimming pool that I don't think exists at this hotel anymore - I didn't see one and it's not mentioned on the hotel's website

Escape from the Kids. We only stayed the one night but were welcomed and treated well. The Hotel was busy with a Wedding Party but the staff were attentive and helpful even though all were busy. We had a lovely meal and a free bottle of wine "Discover Sussex" and the room was very nice. All in all we would highly recommend

good wedding. went to the hotel for a family wedding. good accomodation good food well oganised reception 
Because the windows were small they let in little light therefore the choice of grey wall paper was poor. There was no light over the wardrobe so had to use the open bathroom door

Weekend Stay. Having a weekend with Friends in Heywards Heath, needed a local hotel found Hickstead Hotel which suited local taxi travel, very suprised to find this was a lovely place to stay. Staff excellent all provided a welcoming service, breakfast very good, rooms clean and comfortable, very nice to have windows which open with no adverse noise in background, other than geese in morning, which made us laugh. 
Did not have dinner as out for two nights with friends however did have a ham sandwich snack home cooked ham with salad and chips excellent, Highly recommended to all

Very friendly. Staff are really friendly and helpful, made us very welcome and comfortable. Thanks go to the chef who was happy to accommodate a special vegetarian request. Food was excellent, both for dinner and  breakfast, fresh well prepared and served happily. Menus not exceptional but sufficiently interesting to remember with some good specials.  Rooms comfortable, clean and tidy - my wife even wondered how they got the bath to look so clean that it sparkled! We stayed here last year, in May, enjoyed it so much went back again when passing through the area. This time very surprised to find that we could book the 'Discover Sussex' deal for a Bank Holiday weekend, but did book early. Very good deal for one night visit, with dinner & wine included. Convenient location for airport, coast and countryside. Popular for weddings, but party did not disturb us. The staff really make this place work well

Fantastic day, service, hotel and price!!. We had our wedding here on the 18th August 2012, and from the very first day of enquiring, the staff at the hotel were very helpful.  They helped us plan for the day, they made recommendations which were welcome to us as they hold a lot of wedding at their venue and they certainly know what they were doing.  They recommended a DJ and photographer for us to which we used both and we were not let down at all.  They bend over backwards to make sure your day is what you need, going as far as going out to get different beers if one of your guests is particularly fussy and they don't serve the beer they want.  They made the day go so smoothly for us and me and my wife didn't have to worry about a single thing all day.  The hotel is conveniently placed just off the A23 and also just enough into the country which is brilliant for our photo's and just made for a more pleasant setting.  The food was fantastic, and the way they changed the room round from dinner to evening reception was quick and as the day was very very hot (hottest day of the year) this was welcome as the room is very well air conditioned.  We would certainly recommend this hotel for its friendliness, efficiency, help, organisation and our experience from our wedding day right through to the lovely breakfast the next morning was a truly fantastic one! Also the hotels prices were very good, we looked around at prices for weddings and the Hickstead Hotel were by far the cheapest and they gave us more!! then what the other venues were offering.  We'd like to thank the hotel for their help and support and for making our big day a truly special one!

Very friendly and comfortable hotel with good food.. We stayed at this hotel in August 2012. The staff were very friendly from the moment we walked in the front door. We booked for a special offer which was £99 for bed and breakfast, including 3 course dinner and a bottle of wine. There was a choice for dinner for all three courses and a choice of the wine. The food was cooked very well and served by very friendy staff.
Our hotel room was very nice and comfortable. In all a very enjoyable stay.
We used this hotel as the following day we were visiting Bolney wine estate on one of their wine tasting tours. This was an ideal place to stay as it was only a few minutes drive away

Happy Hickstead!. The Hickstead is an outstanding hotel, food was amazing and hotel staff were fantastic! Rooms were very modern and clean. Value for money and a great stay!! We are going back again in December and can't wait.

Great staff but poor rooms. First the good...... Hotel staff are friendly and efficient. Food is good, particularly the cooked to order full English breakfast. Quiet and very pleasent location. Tea/Cofee in room. Free internet.
Now the bad.... On entering the rooms they have a stong chemical/ cleaner smell. There is no aircon so in summer the rooms can be very hot with poor ventilation, particularly the upper floor. Bathrooms are small and not particularly clean with mould around the tiles in the bath/shower area. Grounting around floor stiles also stained and dirty. Double beds are "small" and uncomfortable.Pillows and matress are of poor quality.Central corridors of hotel particularly un-appealing and dirty. All rooms doors noisy and slam closed. Dont count on the wake up calls either...I asked for 2 but got neither. I would not recommend

Felt like uninvited wedding guests. Unfortunately whilst the food and staff were fine we wouldn't stay here again, I rang this hotel prior to booking to check vegetarian options were available in the restaurant and I gave the dates I would be looking to stay, at no point was I advised a full on wedding and reception were also taking place, we could not use the bar all night, the barman suggested we sit at the end of the garden to avoid the wedding guests, then, despite requesting one of their loveliest rooms for my friends birthday, we were allocated a room overlooking the car park which was picking up wedding guests until the very early hours, who naturally were loudly celebrating their friends nuptials. I had seen previous reviews about wedding parties and stupidly thought I would've been informed, so you have been warned. this is clearly a wedding venue that squeezes additional guests in to fill the rooms. Very disappointed, and I gave feedback at reception and was told, yes, you should've been told when you booked, but no real apology. (The food was very good quality though so well done chef)

mixed bag. We had a 2 day stay here, the positive features were the restaurant food, which was very good, and the staff,  who were quite helpful and friendly. The bed was comfortable and the in-room wifi worked well and was free, there was adequate parking. We were less impressed by the look and ambiance - rooms were in a  motel-like block to the side of the impressive looking facade and this part of the hotel internal decor really needs a face lift as it is dated and looking rather weary.  The room could be quite stuffy  on a  (rare)  hot  English summer day and the window in our room had only 2 modes- latched at an inadequate distance to allow decent air exchange or fully open with the fear you'll fall out or never catch hold of it again. We stayed during 2 wedding receptions and these can dominate the facilities - we were warned in advance of these being on though, in fairness to the hotel.  At the internet advertised rate and brochure image this hotel is over priced for the experience to our minds

A great trip. My friend and I were overwhelmed with the friendliness and help that the staff gave us, after a bad experience the previous evening. The room was large, clean and very comfortable. Fresh towels every day, the breakfasts and evening meals were excellent. Within easy reach of all the good tourist places in Sussex. We were sad to leave, and felt we had been among good friends

good and not so good. staff extremely friendly. We were here for sat night only.
breakfast and dinner very good. We were on special package but if had to pay usual price would say that was over priced
Bed uncomfortable, room too warm.
more seating on lawn would have been nice
yes it did look like a travel lodge where additional buiding has been added to accomodate bedrooms

short break. great stay, spacious room,good food,excellent staff and quiet location.our three day stay was 
 
        unbelievable value we booked the discover sussex package which included a three course meal and wine each night.  strongly recommended.would visit again

Very nice hotel. Booked an Autumn Deal with Classic Lodges direct. Deal included a bottle of wine and dinner/bed and breakfast. Location is about 25 mins outside of Brighton. We had a lovely stay and the food was very nice

Great value. Warm welcome, very friendly & helpful staff. Food was excellent. Good value for money with the 'Discover Sussex' package. Comfortable room but bathroom could do with a bit of a facelift (didn't like the plastic clingy shower curtain). Nice quiet location. Would definitely stay again

Friendly staff, great food!. I booked the hotels romantic package for me and my girlfriend, which included champagne, roses, 3 course meal, etc. On arrival the staff were very friendly and the champagne, roses and chocolates were in our room like planned. The rooms were good and we had everything we needed. 
The meal in the evening was was very nice, there was a good variety of food too. The steak was very nice

Great value for money. We stayed in this hotel for one night, the hotel is clean and the surroundings are quite adequate, the room was clean and well fitted, the beds were very comfortable, the room overlooked an overflow carpark to the back of the hotel which was not a problem, because  I did not spend my stay looking out the window, nor did I ask for a room with a view.
The bar was very small which I liked, also the barman was very good and very humourous.
I had dinner there and I have to say it was excellent, the staff were very nice and also very helpfull,considering the price I thought it was excellent value for money, no it,s not the Ritz Carlton, but is a very nice reasonable priced hotel in a nice peacefull location, I would have no hesitation in staying there again should the need arise

Critical camper. Day: October 8th 2012 
Almost impressive country style drive leading to ample parking. Boarded by a tired 70's style motel with a pleasant older property attached. On entering mediocre reception area was pleased to be greeted by welcoming staff. Room was spacious, clean and tidy but tired. Restaurant staff were excellent surroundings a little characterless and un inspiring however the food was good. Overall a reasonable stay but wouldn't rush back

Sunday afternoon tea. My husband received a voucher for his birthday, for afternoon tea for two.  Excellent food - sandwiches (3 types), chocolate gateaux, chocolate eclairs, strawberry & cream meringues, homemade shortbread biscuits, warm scones with plenty of cream & jam.  (I am sure that everything was home-made).  Plenty of tea & coffee, with hot water to top-up. We nearly managed to eat everything (but not quite!).  Good service - will definately go again!

comfortable, friendly and good value. We stayed at the hickstead for 1 night as we were attending a nearby family wedding. Check in was from 3pm but the wedding was at 2.30pm. I telephoned the day before to see if it was possible to check in earlier at 1.30ish and was told that it was possible but not definite. After an argument with satnav (ok - operator error) we actually arrived even earlier at 12.45. The lady on reception was very helpful and although our room wasn't ready, she changed us into another room and we checked in. 
The room was comfortable and clean. Certainly way better than a travelodge (which I had read on a previous review), with plenty of room and the normal facilities.
When we returned from our reception at Old Pangdean Barns, there was a wedding reception or party going on in the function room. Of course this is expected at a hotel and we found it of no trouble to us. The bar was still open to all guests.
After a good nights sleep the breakfast was excellent, with a choice of cereals, croissants, cooked, tea, coffee, juices, etc.... 
Comfortable, friendly & good value

Only good comments for The Hickstead!. Every single member of staff was so pleasant and helpful. Best service I have ever received in a British hotel. Food tasted very fresh and flavoursome and the portions were excellent. The room was clean with good facilities and the bed was so comfortable I slept right through the night (which I often struggle to do in hotels)!. The icing on the cake was when I realised I had left my bracelet in the room. The receptionist was really helpful - she personally went to the room and found it for me then put it in an envelope which I collected a few hours later. Thank you all so much for such a lovely stay. My husband and I will look forward to seeing you again in the future!

away from it all. we were greeted by the lady who had taken our reservation who checked us it at 13.00 which is before the usual time, we had a lovely lunch on the terrace although it is in a corner of the car park but a lovely sun trap, the room was clean and tidy  the bed comfy. the main hotel is a lovely manor house and the accommodation a more modern block connected with good views, The restaurant was very nice, a shame that the white wine was warm but an ice bucket came up with it so they had realised their error. We had a set package which offered a choice of about four for each course and none disappointed.
the only negative was that after dinner there was nothing happening,boards games by a the lounge TV were off-putting there is a small bar which is manned but sometimes you had to call staff to serve, we went off to bed at 22.00 but slept well. Breakfast was a buffet and very good.
Staff were friendly and helpful.
Overall the hotel is a great choice just outside Brighton, and we had a lovely relaxing break,we will definitely return

Very good hotel, with excellent staff. The hotel itself was very good. The room was large and clean, and at a very good rate for the area. The only criticism I'd make was the temperature. With a maximum daytime temperature of 15C, the temperature in our room was over 23C, which was a bit uncomfortable during the night. The window could only open a small amount (understandable, being on the ground floor), and there was no air conditioning. I'm not sure I'd want to stay here in very hot weather.

The food was good, and surprisingly reasonably priced for a hotel.

But what made it for me were the excellent staff. They were all friendly, but professional, and quickly responded to all questions and requests

HICKSTEAD HOTEL. Lovely Hotel with happy, cheerfull and very attentive staff. Good quality food and beutiful rooms. Well done everyone who works here. Our company held its conference here this week. The staff went out of their way to offer great service and provide a memorable stay. Food was very good, breakfast particularily good and probably more than enough for even the biggest breakfast man. The bedrooms were stylish and exceptionally clean and well presented. The hotel is a little out in the country, but that helped with a great nights sleep

Nice hotel, great staff. My wife and I have just returned from a weekend in the Hickstead Hotel, and we really enjoyed our stay there. As other reviewers have mentioned, every single member of the staff was friendly and helpful - it's a bit of a cliché but they really made us feel like valued guests. Our bedroom was large, clean and comfortable, well decorated and equipped. We ate dinner in the restaurant and there was a wide choice on the menu, the food was good quality, with generous servings. We had chosen the 2 night "garden break" which included dinner on the first night, and tickets to the nearby Borde Hill Gardens, and was excellent value for money. Overall, there's really nothing to complain about, and we would definitely recommend the Hickstead Hotel

Quiet, clean and comfortable. Our room was fairly spacious, the bathroom was a little tired, but everywhere was clean. We stayed on a Saturday night but it was very quiet despite the hotel being busy that weekend. The reception staff were very friendly and helpful. Breakfast was excellent

A pleasant hotel to stay at. We stayed at the Hickstead for a short break in Sussex.  It was ideally situated for visits to Brighton and Lewes.  All of the staff were unfailingly polite and enthusiastic.  It was great to enjoy this high level of customer service.  We were on an inclusive package which worked well for us; the range of food for breakfast and dinner suited us well.  We were pleased to have stayed at The Hickstead

Fantastic staff made it a memorable stay.. We booked The Hickstead as a base for a weekend in Brighton.  The hotel itself is not huge, but has plenty of parking and a pleasant countryside location. It was only 20 minutes drive to Brighton.  We were greeted very warmly, and our room was clean, fresh and peaceful.  What made the stay here special was the way every member of staff we encountered was unfailingly cheerful, helpful and seemed to genuinely care that we had a good experience.  Good old fashioned customer service!  In particular, the lovely restaurant staff were fantastic, and the both the breakfasts and dinner we had there were great

Worth a visit for a relaxing break.. I booked cream tea and two deluxe rooms on the autumn special deal and stayed on 3rd November.  From the moment we arrived nothing was too much trouble. We were able to book in an hour early. The cream tea was marvellous, with all the cakes made at the hotel. Worth just visiting for the afternoon. Spacious rooms with comfortable beds. Pleasant location with rabbits hopping around the car park. Evening meal was very tasty. What makes this hotel outstanding is the staff, in particular middle aged dark haired lady whose name I unfortunately cannot remember. She served us our afternoon tea and evening meal and was extremely attentive. We would definitely stay here again

Everything you could wish for.. We chose the Hickstead because it is part of the Classic Lodges chain and we had enjoyed our stay at The Old Swan in Harrogate. The Hickstead was rated highly by previous guests and was well located for the sort of break we were looking for (Sheffield Park, Brighton and other attractions are very close by). We initially booked three nights B & B through an internet booking agency. We were telephoned by a member of staff enquiring whether we wished to reserve a table in the restaurant for dinner. I asked if their Autumn Break deal was available as this provides exceptional value and included dinner and a bottle of wine. Happy, it was.
After a friendly welcome at reception we found the accommodation was very comfortable with a large, well furnished bedroom fitted with plenty of storage space. The bathroom was modern and well fitted including bath with power shower over.
In the attractively laid out restaurant the food was plentiful and of a high standard. There was a good choice and the service was friendly and attentive. Breakfasts were generous and cooked to order - one of the best cooked breakfasts we have enjoyed in any hotel. Again, the experience was enhanced by the charming service.
Overall, the delightful staff were the key to making our break most memorable. We highly recommend this hotel and hope to return

Pleasant one night stay. The room was lovely, clean with a wonderfully comfortable bed. The view was awful, ask for a room with a good view! Breakfast was very good with a few minor exceptions; service was slow, we had to ask several times for tea, coffee & toast. Fruit was tinned, should have been fresh. Plates for hot food were cold and jam was budget! There are some fab jams available and these were tasteless! 
Staff were friendly and common parts were very clean and tidy. Public toilets need refurbishing!

wonderful afternoon tea. We booked for afternoon tea and it was just what we hoped for. The service was good and the venue full of old world charm. Traditional sandwiches (no crusts), scones with jam and cream and a selection of cream cakes, all made at the hotel. We had pots of traditional and Earl Grey tea. Alltogether a very nice way to spend a wet and windy afternoon in the Sussex countryside

A lovely hotel. I have stayed at The Hickstead many times. This is a great hotel. The staff are so helpful and friendly. Unlike many hotels, the attention to those staying is key to the pleasant and friendly atmosphere. The rooms are always clean and they are a good size - well furnished and comfortable. The food is excellent - breakfasts are very good with everything you could possibly want. There is plenty of parking - the location is lovely and it is very quiet. I stayed recently when it was extremely cold, but the room and the whole hotel was warm and comfortable.

This is a 'proper' hotel - professionally run and offers excellent value for money. I shall certainly stay again

Lovely stay & good value. I thought i would surprise my husband on a one night stay as it was his 40th birthday and booked the Sussex Dinner, Breakfast & Bottle of House Wine Double Room for £99.  I am very pleased that i did!  I have paid more for a 'deluxe' room in other hotels and the size of the room was about the same if not bigger so thought this was very good value for money, especially having a 3 course meal, wine & breakfast all included.  I would definitely recommend this hotel to others, the staff were very friendly but because we went on a Sunday there were less staff than usual so did have to wait a little bit to get served, but not a major problem.  Dinner was lovely and the lounge area was in a relaxing environment. One thing we did notice though was that we had an extra charge of £3.50 that we didn't recognize on our bill but because we had such a nice stay we didn't bother to chase this up as it was only a small amount

we stayed at hotel 22nd Dec for a wedding.. From the time we arrived till the time we left we could not find fault with anything the wedding was faultless and food superb .evening disco was presented well and could even get a drink very late if you so wished.so to sum up if you need a hotel to cater for all needs this ticks all the boxes

new years eve gala delight. we stayed in room 117 for the gala new years eve dinner and disco. We arrived early and was allowed to check in, the receptionist was very friendly and helpful. The room was nice, had all we required, extra pillows etc, it was very clean. The bar man was really outgoing and helpful, infact all of the staff went out of their way to ensure everything was ok. The dinner was excellent, I really could not find fault with anything. Lamb was cooked to perfection. The wine choice was ok and reasonably priced for a hotel. Cheese and port was great, but a massive portion of cheese really was enough for 2. The DJ was great and did a quizz after dinner to get everybody mixing and joining in. I was a little surprised that not all guests had made an effort with their dress, I expected it to be a black tie event and was a little shocked to see some gents without ties and jackets.
