This evening was not overbooked and could have catered for more guests, there were probably under 40 people. I originally tried to book some 6 weeks ago and was told that they were not catering for 2's (i was really surprised to hear this) I contacted Dawn again by email about 3 weeks ago and again was told that no couples tables were being sold, as they wanted larger groups. We managed to book on friday - I was surprised to see several 2's there, so I can only guess that didn't sell as many places as they expected. I would certainly go again next year, we usually go to a hotel in brighton, but they were hosting a wedding this year instead! The breakfast was excellent and so was the service. This is a hotel that employes staff that really care about their jobs, good old customer services goes a long way! We would definaltely come back and would recommend this place to our friends and family. The only reason I did not give 5 stars is because we had trouble booking initially. Great place keep doing what your doing The Hickstead as it is working well!

Value for money. My wife and I enjoyed a relaxing weekend at this hotel recently.  The service from all staff was excellent, the rooms clean and tidy, the food was as expected from a 3 star hotel and there was lots of it. Overall this was excellent value for money and we would happily return

Great traditional hotel. Stayed here several times on business never been let down.

Rooms are a good size, clean with nice beds and good sized bathrooms. The staff are always helpful and cheerful. The restaurant offers good food and the breakfast options are good as well.

Location is ideal to get to several places being just off the A23 with good views of downs

Wedding reception. We chose the Hickstead for our wedding reception september 2012. We had such an amazing day. The staff are so fantastic & friendly. We felt so at home and could not have asked for better. Thank you Giles, Hannah, Jenny, Patricia and all that helped make our day completely perfect x

Lovely staff but hotel needs an upgrade to be 'country house'. My husband and I stayed overnight with the £99 D,B&B offer (including bottle of wine) which was great value.  Surprisingly (for first weekend in Jan) there was a wedding on and we had to fight our way through the wedding line-up to get to the reception desk - not a huge deal but a little planning could have avoided this.  The receptionist was polite and welcoming.  Our room was a reasonable size and clean but decor is more on a par with a travel lodge.  We had a drink before dinner in the bar area with a very friendly barman but, again, the whole bar area could do with some focus - maybe putting in a wood burning stove or similar to give a country-house feel (as the hotel is clearly aiming for).  At the moment, there is just a large TV which made it feel slightly more like a waiting room.  Lighting was too bright as well, didn't create any intimacy which a good hotel bar should have.

There was an excellent choice of menu at dinner and the food was reasonable, however the dining room really needed an upgrade.  Not necessarily a huge spend needed but, for example, making sure that all the tables have cloths on rather than the dated placemats we had on ours. They also needed soft lighting, to draw the curtains (dark and cold outside) and light the candles to create a better atmosphere. The staff again were great which lifts this above similar hotels.

We can't argue with the value of this hotel and overall it was a good stay, however someone needs to cast a critical eye over this hotel (who knows what a real country-house hotel looks like) and make some improvements to decor.  Again, this wouldn't require a big investment but just some little touches such as flowers on the reception desk, changes to lighting, better crockery and cutlery in the dining room

Great, friendly, like home. What a great place to stay, the location is fantastic and nice and quiet, thanks to the wonderful staff for making the 2 weeks we stayed there so pleasant. The staff really make this hotel, and will go out of their way to accommodate your needs

Hickstead Hotel, Bolney, Sussex. We stayed at The Hickstead Hotel for two nights 18 months ago, and really enjoyed our stay and re booked for one night again staying on the night of 26th of January.  We were pleased to see the hotel standard was the same as before.  The rooms are large and well decorated and furnished, and the bathroom was exceptionally clean too.  The Oak Tree restaurant within the hotel serves wonderfully tasty and well presented dinner and a fabulous buffet breakfast too!  The staff in all areas, restaurant and reception were friendly, helpful and charming too

Hickstead Hotel. As the Hickstead is near family we stay a few times a year and have for the last few years.  From our first visit we find the hotel relaxing, comfortable,with good sized rooms and always peaceful.  The staff are exceptional and the food in the restaurant excellent.  And as we often book on the DBB packages - the value is unquestionable

Wedding. We have just had my daughters wedding at the Hickstead on Feb 2nd 13 and it was a truly wonderful day and weekend. We have been planning this for 2 years and was not disappointed. The staff and especially Hannah was exceptional and were more then helpful over this time and on the day ..The rooms for our guests were clean and comfortable and all our guests and ourselves were accommodated extremely well and they even gave us breakfast in our room on the morning of the wedding for my daughters, mother ,myself and mother in law all beautifully presented. The ceremony room looked lovely and the reception room was stunning, The wedding breakfast was exceptional and hot and people complimented on it and also how the accommodated varying meal needs, the service and staff were excellent and the whole day was really well planned, The sun shone albeit cold we had pictures taken in the grounds which was lovely.. Breakfast Sunday morning was very good and everyone that attended the wedding said what a wonderful time they had.. On behalf of my daughter , son in law i would like to thank the staff and hotel for a truly fantastic day and will hold special memories for us all...We will be back for mothers day where they serve a lovely afternoon tea and intend to make visiting the hotel a regular occasions..many thanks Debs x

Very accommodating, a solid night's sleep and a great breakfast. We, my husband and I, stayed here for one night when hubby decided he wanted to run the Brighton half marathon (which is 20 minute drive away). We tried for ages to find somewhere actually in Brighton but a) he'd left it a bit late to organise and everywhere was full and b) everywhere that did have rooms available required a two night stay on a weekend. Unfortunately we had a family committment on the Friday so could only stay for one night. We originally had a B&B booked in Bishopstone but they were difficult (in fact slightly rude) when I asked if we could have a late check out (hubby not finishing his race until 10.30am at the earliest and needing a shower, and possibly a short rest). 

When I phoned The Hickstead to check if we could have a late check out the answer was "of course", is there a charge for that? "only if you're staying until the evening, but until 1pm we won't charge you". Brilliant. Can we have an early breakfast for hubby? Of course! Do you have gluten free bread (me being somewhat of a 'wheatard') - of course! Well, for £89 for both of us including breakfast, I'm sold. 

Finding the hotel is easy, come off the A23 onto the A2300 and head towards Bolney, there's signs for The Hickstead Hotel almost straight away. Follow the signs and you're there, down a country road with bunnies hopping off the side. The 'drive way' feels like you're entering a farm, there's a horse in a field, ducks on a pond, an angling lake and lots of fields and trees. Coming from London I started to relax immediately. The hotel building looks a little like a converted barn crossed with an office block, but it's welcoming and the surroundings are peaceful with lots of birdsong. The main car park is a good size, with an overflow car park round the back of the building. 

Check in was quick and uncomplicated, we got our bags and went up to our room. We had a standard double - which normally would be fairly small, but the size was really good. There's plenty of room to walk around without getting in each other's way or tripping over bags or clothes. The bed is a large double (hubby wasn't kicking me in the night!) and super comfy. The duvet is so soft and warm, and combined with extra pillows gave me a super night's sleep. We had a little table and chairs by the window, at which I was able to do a little work on my laptop using the free wi-fi in the hotel (no password, just log on, nice and simple). The TV is Freeview and also has Sky Sports, so we were able to watch a little sport and a film. Standard teas/coffees in the room, plus good mirrors, trouser press, wardrobe, plugs, drawers etc. Curtains are proper black out curtains which is great. Good bathroom - huge soft towels (my favourite kind), large lit mirror, body wash/soap/shower cap etc provided, super-strong shower with easy to adjust temperature, although the bath is teeny tiny (about as wide as the length of my foot). The only other downside I can think of with the room is the lighting, there's only four small wall lamps, it makes the room a little darker than I'd like. I would prefer to have one or two large ceiling lights. 

Our room was on the first floor, overlooking the overflow car park and what looks like a builders yard. Fortunately, it was the weekend so we weren't disturbed by anything going on in the yard, but I imagine during the week there may be some noise coming from there. The only other outside noise we heard was the geese and ducks wandering around the back of the building and having a go at each other, the bird song, the occasional tractor and the main road. Inside the hotel the sound proofing is good, although you can hear loud TVs in other rooms and the cleaners hoovering, but it's not too bad and I've certainly had worse. 

Breakfast is served 8am-10am on a weekend, which is a little earlier than I would have liked, but that's probably because I was so enjoying sleeping in that bed! Choice of breakfast is the usual juices, cereals, yoghurt, pastries and a good quality cooked breakfast, with gluten free bread and rooibus (red bush) tea. I do like being accommodated properly, it makes all the difference. The staff were helpful and friendly at all times, not once have I had to wait, despite the hotel being almost full and everything I needed/wanted has been provided without difficulty or issue. Plus, we stayed at the hotel until 1pm and had no problems checking out at that later time. 

Overall we've been very happy with our stay, if we ever need to stay in this area again we'll definitely consider coming back

Peaceful Setting. A warm welcome greeted us on our arrival. The room was very spacious, and very clean, and the bathroom was also very clean. We stayed on a Bed and Breakfast package. The Breakfast was lovely both mornings. All Staff were friendly, which is so nice, can't be doing with a frosty reception. The setting is lovely. Would highly recommend, and if needs be when visiting our daughter, would definatley use again. Think all the Staff have been trained well, and as they say, 'The Proof Is In The Pudding', so they should be praised by their superior's. Many Thanks

A Warm Welcome. A warm welcome, and a table in a bay window looking out over the hotel grounds, and beyond them the Downs. We enjoyed a leisurely afternoon tea – one of the best we have had ever. Thick filled sandwiches, scones jam and cream, and home made cakes warm from the oven. Excellent value for money and a very pleasurable afternoon out

Wonderful friendly service. We stayed recently for a week. If you have to be in this area of England this is the place to stay. The staff are very friendly, the breakfast is first rate and the other menus are also very good. Will definitely be back

Excellent Afternoon Tea. On arrival at the hotel the receptionist was very welcoming and showed us into the lounge. We sat on the sofa in front of the large window and was quickly served our afternoon tea which was amazing! When booking I asked if we could have plain scones instead of fruit which we were served, the sandwiches were full and fresh, we could not fault anything. It was very relaxing and we were not rushed to finish. All staff we came into contact with were friendly. Would definately recommend and look forward to visiting again

Disappointed bride.. As an Events Manager myself, I thought long & hard about writing this review (I got married here in September 2012) but recently heard further bad feedback from my guests, it's just not going away!
Firstly I have to say that we had a wonderful day. The hotel is ideally situated for a wedding and we had fireworks in the  grounds - which were amazing! Dawn, the wedding planner did a great job, from start to finish and the food was great.
However, I would say that the hotel failed on organisation overall. 
I found the Restaurant Manager to be patronising, pushy & unorganised - in fact I asked several times for her to be kept away from my wedding. When I was being led to my ceremony, one of the guests was talking to my mum and brother about her bad attitude, the last thing I want to hear right then!
During the meal, I had ordered a bottle of rose for the top table, when the wine was served this not materialise until all the other wine had  been served and my mum asked for it. (Myself and my mum didnt have wine until everyone else did.)
There was a huge delay in between every course, mainly due to the fact that while we were eating there were no staff in the room. When my guests started complaining, I got up and complained to the Restaurant Manager. No bride should have to be complaining on her special day!
The restaurant staff were wearing T shirts and cardigans, but the next day, came in for breakfast in shirts and waistcoats.
We had ordered 1/2 bottle of wine per person, but only 1 glass per person came out, until I asked where it was.
In the evening, I had asked for my cake to be put on the buffet (it was meant to be put on the centre of the tables during the day, but we had  run out of time due to slow service). When I went to the toilet, I saw my beautiful cake, sitting in the service room. When I asked the manager where my cake was, she told me it was on the buffet - until I pointed out to her that I could see it!

I thought that this was it, until we had dinner with some friends last week.
There was another wedding party in house, that were getting married elsewhere. My friends said that during the ceremony, all they could hear was the reversing sound of the coach that was picking the other guests up! Surely they should have been told not to make noise during the time of our ceremony??
They also told me that our cupcakes were put in the bar (where we were sitting) after the reception and the other wedding came back and started helping themselves.

I am an events manager, so have a keener eye than most, but I am so disappointed that my day wasnt the organised day that I would have wanted it to be. I have had a response from the hotel, but no amount of free weekends away will compensate for the fact that everytime someone asks me about my wedding, I say 'we had a lovely day, but...'
One of my friends has since commented that she would never get married in a hotel after my wedding and everytime someone talks about it - it's in a negative light!

Back Again. Our third return visit since originally attending a wedding in 2010 as Best Man.  On this occasion we returned with the bride and groom.

Efficient check in, despite arriving at the same time as other guests who were arriving en-masse for a Diamond Anniversary event.

After getting settled in our rooms, we enjoyed some time relaxing in the lounge playing games supplied by the hotel.  It was very quiet and made for a pleasant afternoon.

Dinner was to the high standard we have become used to, and we enjoyed the complimentary Proseco with the meal (we were on an inclusive package).

There were only two items on the menu that I could see that incurred a supplement when using the package, neither of which I considered expensive.  3 course dinner with complimetary bottle of Proseco, bed & breakfast for under £100 per room can't be sniffed at!

Staff again were friendly, attentive and efficient - and it was nice to be remembered again by Jenny and Hannah.  We look forward to returning again

fantastic wedding venue. after reading reading a review about how disappointed a bride was with her wedding here i just had to write this and disagree.
we got married here on the 22nd of december and can only say that it was everything we wanted and more.we live about an hour and a half away from the hotel so we could have chosen a nearer location but after meeting the hotel manager giles at a wedding fair we were convinced that this was the venue for us.
nothing and i mean nothing was to much trouble,i probably phoned the hotel atleast once a week with leading up to the wedding with many requirements and was never made to feel a nusience and everything i asked for we got.
i stayed the night before the wedding with my daughters and took all 7 of the mens suites with me all the shirts had to be ironed which i was prepared to do but giles took these off me ironed them all and all the suites and shirts were all neatly hung up in the correct rooms that what i call service.
not one of my guests complained quite the oppisite everyone said what a wonderfull time they had how great the service and staff were and that they had stayed in 5 star hotels that were not as good as this.
if i could renew my vows every year i would diffinately do it here not sure giles could put up with my phone calls though lol
just one more quick mention for the receptionist,sorry but i cant remember her name,she had everything dumped on her through the night and next day suites that we had to take back mine and my bridesmaides flowers even shoes but not once did she complain

Love staying here. Stay here quite often , great package value room evening meal breakfast and wine 
Staff really care about their guests and care about the hotel 
Rooms a big and well thought out.
I always stay here when on business , looking forward to my next stay early march

Pleasant and relaxing stay. Stayed last week for one night on business. A welcoming reception and staff whowere overall very friendly and attentive Rooms were large and well equipped. Had dinner which was adequate without being spectacular. Good nights sleep in a peaceful, quiet location and a most hearty breakfast.
Overall a most pleasant stay and would have no hesitation in returning

Wedding day and afternoon teas. We had our wedding day here and all I can say is that it was the most fantastic day!! Staff were exceptional!! After planning our wedding for 2 years I can honestly say it was the best day of our lives so far!! Hannah was our wedding co ordinator and what a fantastic job she did! Every time we visited she couldn't do enough for us!! We now visit a lot and have just had Mother's Day afternoon tea here which again Hannah was here and again did a fantastic job!! I love the hickstead and the staff that work there!! Will always be a very special place for us!

Disappointed.. We were looking forward to our stay here after reading the good reviews - but we left feeling that everyone else was talking about another hotel!

First of all, the hotel is very badly signed - there is no signage off the surrounding lanes (well small one, but very hard to see, especially in the dark!)'

The rooms and some of the public area are looking a little tired and dated, our first room had no heating at all and we had to be moved to another one.  The window did not fit properly in the second room so we were aware of traffic noise most of the night.  The bed was the hardest one I have ever slept in, as were the pillows, and the duvet only just fitted the bed so we were playing "duvet wars" all night.  I have stayed in many, many hotels around the world and it was definitely the most uncomfortable bed in a hotel room.

The saving grace was the very good cooked breakfast and the pleasant hotel staff, but it certainly was not the stay we were expecting.  The Premier Inn at Burgess Hill is a much better option for this area

birthday treat. We booked to stay with friends for a 50th birthday treat. The hotel staff were excellent and couldn't do enough for us, they even overheard us discussing the pudding menu as one of our party had a lot of food intolerances and the member of staff spoke to the chef and he offered to prepare a dessert to cater for her needs. We have stayed here before and each time they don't disappoint, from the welcoming staff, the fantastic rooms and the first class food. And even better the exceptional package price. Thank you

Outstanding. My wife and I visted the Hickstead hotel for 2 nights on their £99 package (dinner + B&B), it was outstanding value for money.  The staff are attentive, friendly & pleasant, the food was exceptionally good and it made for a good relaxing couple of days away.  It wasn't our first visit to the Hickstead - it certainly won't be our last

Enjoyable stay excellent staff.. Stayed at the Hickstead hotel for two nights discover Sussex package which includes dinner with wine and B&B. We were allocated a rooom at the back of the hotel as I had requested a quiet location in the hotel. The room was clean and a reasonable size. Although the bed and pillows were a too hard for my back-would request an alternative if available the next time we stay. I say "next time" as :- all staff were professional and welcoming-a special mention to the resturant staff who were exceptional. The food at dinner was cooked and presented very well,particulary impressed with the fact there was no seperate menu for the all inclusive package. We enjoyed dinner on boths nights in the relaxed and comfotable dinning area. Breakfast was cooked to order, most enjoyable and great coffee. The hotel is idealy situated for visiting Brighton and surrounding area-the hotel is very well signed by the way and easy to locate. Thanks to the staff for a relaxing short break which was appreciated

Good organisation,good service, good food. I stayed for 2 nights as a wedding guest. The service we received was excellent in every way. The staff were quietly efficient and managed the wedding ceremony incredibly well, all the arrangements went smoothly and it all seemed effortless,although there was obviously a lot of hard work involved. We ate several meals at the hotel including the wedding breakfast all of which were excellent, so full marks to the kitchen,breakfast itself was really, really good. I would definitely recommend the Hickstead as a hotel and wedding venue

fantastic wedding venue. Just back from 3 nights at this hotel where we were attending a wedding.  Everyone at the hotel was friendly, efficient and looked after us superbly.  The wedding was superbly organised and everyone was made to feel very welcome and were looked after superbly.  Nothing was too much trouble and this allowed the bride and groom to really enjoy their day knowing that everyone was being so well catered for.  The food both in the restaurant and at the wedding was excellent.  All round a great place to stay, great value for money and top service

Wedding March 2013. Me and my partner had our wedding day at The Hickstead and it really did live up to the hype, it was better than we could of dreamed it would be.
From our very first meeting with Giles the general manager who also had the job of our wedding co-ordinator right through to the morning after our wedding, all the staff were very friendly and helpful, putting all the finishing touches to our day and nothing was too much to ask of them, they even made some of the wedding guests sandwiches at 2.30am!!
The bedrooms are a generous size, comfortable beds and a beautiful bathroom.. they block all the sound out and are very peaceful.
The food was out of this world, they cater for all requirements including little mouths and fussy eaters. The breakfast is amazing, you will not go home hungry!!
The staff were on hand throughout the day and got to know us as a family which added a really personal touch to our day. They were there from the crack of dawn helping the grooms party with their button holes right through to the early hours of the morning, helping the grooms party find their rooms.
We would highly reccomend The Hickstead as a wedding venue, all of our guests couldnt compliment it enough and we didnt have one complaint.
My Nan lost her engagement ring during the day and the hotel staff found it and contatced us the following day to say they would keep it safe for us to collect.

We are going back in the summer for afternoon tea in the garden... and i already cant wait!!

Very mediocre. Stayed at this hotel as it was the venue for a friend's birthday celebration. Although there was a note at Reception to apologise for the inconvenience caused whilst toilets were being refurbished it was not sufficient to cover for the appalling odours that permeated the area. Thankfully our room was far away from this area and unaffected however the main hotel area stank and was highly unpleasant. Rooms were basic but we were there because do the function so there is no likelihood that we would return of our own volition

Fantastic wedding Venue. We held our wedding here in feb 2013, the staff were fantastic from the start. So helpfull. The hotel itself is clean and smart and the staff were all smiles and couldn't help enough. All of our guests commented on how lovely the atmosphere of the day was. The hotel is very family friendly, we had several young children and babies attend our day, a room was set aside as a baby changing / feeding area etc and pleanty of high chairs to use, and never a problem to heat a bottle! The food was great, lots of good feedback from our guests again. A very good day, can't thank them enough

Nice stay. Stayed here on their B&B & dinner offer. Hotel is situated off the A23 Hickstead turn off but is surrounded by fields & a lake. Car parks were packed due to a business conference but managed to eventually park up. Stayed in room 122 which was nicely appointed but was noisy from people in corridor & people moving bins in car park early. Dinner was were the hotel excelled. Warm greeting from the restaurant staff and good choices of food. Breakfast was also good. Recommended for a night away, especially if taking up one of their offers

An excellent hotel. We stayed for two nights, visiting family and friends in the area. The service from all the staff was excellent, especially considering that there was a large party on the first night. We were given a room, with a pleasant view, well away from the festivities and noise. All the staff were extremely helpful. The food was good, both at breakfast and dinner. We would have liked softer pillows!

Clean but looking a bit tired. The room was a good size and clean, but felt - along with the rest of the hotel - a bit dated and tired. Functional rather than somewhere to stay for a romantic break or get away. Breakfast was good - staff were friendly and attentive. However, we struggled to get a good night's sleep on a soft and uncomfortable mattress. Overall, we were disappointed and wouldn't plan a return trip

Afternoon Tea. We booked afternoon tea as part of grandaughter's birthday celebrations.  The tea was scrumptious - wonderful choice of sandwiches and cakes topped off with freshly made scones.  They even made me a smoked salmon salad as an option as I don't eat bread or cake but still wanted to be part of the celebrations.  Very good service in a lovely setting

visiting friends. We booked bed and breakfast .and evening meal at Hickstead as we were visiting friends in Brighton.By error we arrived one hour early to book in,2.00pm instead of 3.00pm but were welcomed by a charming young lady who said she would check to see if our room may be ready.Fortunately it was!!!so we were allowed in to our room one hour earlier.
The room was very spacious,and very clean ,with all the usual facilities[tea,coffee,etc].Our 
particular room did not have a nice view ,but some of the guests said they had nice views 
of the country side[maybe a one night]Our meal that evening was well cooked,but unless you have a big appetite they were plentyful!!.We had a three course ,but two would have been right for us?along with a bottle of wine,and coffee.The breakfast the next morning was mixture of continental and full english and you could have both or whatever.We found the staff very pleasant and helpful,and nothing was a problem for any of them.We would think about visiting 
again ,but would like a room with a better view

Cream Tea & Overnight Stay. We were given a gift to have a Cream Tea at this hotel.  The cream tea was plentiful and delicious although we did have to advise them 2 or 3 times that we were vegetarian before getting the correct sandwiches!  We found the room comfortable and very clean, as is the hotel, the breakfast had plenty to choose from and was tasty.  Apart from the first hiccup, the staff were extremely attentive and we would stay here again, as we were made to feel very welcome and comfortable

Worth a Go. Stayed here recently while on business in the area. Nice clean hotel, big rooms, good internet access. Evening meal of a decent standard and Breakfast cooked to order. Good deals available BB&D, so a good call if your looking for a short stay in this area

Hickstead winner. Had a nice overnight stay at the hotel - room was ample for one night and service in restaurant exemplary! Food was well presented and tasty. As it was a Saturday evening there was a function taking place and there was a little noise from those staying for this, but it wasn't intrusive

An excellent stay. Needed to be in the area overnight and the Premier was full which was a jolly good thing as I had a wonderful stay at the Hickstead Hotel.  From the moment I walked in to when I left I had fabulous service.  The friendly receptionist quickly signed me in and even came to my room to 'sort my TV out'!  I had arranged for a couple of friends who live locally to join me for a drink and Hassan, behind the bar, looked after us beautifully.  I ordered room service just before 9pm (they helpfully reminded me that 9pm was last orders) of sirloin steak, mushrooms, tomato and offered me a selection of vegetables instead of the fries.  The 'au poivre' sauce was in a separate jug.  It arrived promptly and was exceptionally good.  The steak was a good thickness and cooked perfectly and all the vegetables were delicious.  It was so good I was compelled to leave a thank you note on the tray for the chef!  The room was a very good size (Room 126), immaculately clean and well appointed.  If I was being very picky my slight niggles (which is what prevents a 5* review) are that the lighting in the room was generally dim (although I have noticed that hotels do this quite often), the bathroom floor tiling/grouting needs some attention and the very comfortable mattress was creaky on one side (quickly sorted by sleeping on the other side!).  The breakfast was superb; served by a couple of lovely and friendly staff.  The usual cereal and fruit and juice selection but it was a refreshing change not to have to cook your own toast and to have a large range of cooked breakfasts offered.  All brought with a smile and perfectly cooked.  Thank you Hickstead Hotel for a very comfortable stay.  Highly recommended

Wedding Anniversary Short Break. My wife and I stayed as a night away (only a very few miles from home) to celebrate our first anniversary. Appropriately there was a Wedding taking place in the hotel, which obviously reminded us of things even more. The deal we got was excellent, the 2 of us with a 3 course meal and B&B for around £100. The room was comfortable and well maintained, and the staff were welcoming from the start (esp. Jenny Baker in the the Dining Room). Thank you all for a lovely evening with great homemade/cooked food and a fantastic buffet full English in the morning

Do you like cold showers?. I had fully intended to write a positive review until I woke up in the morning. I woke up to a terrible view of a scrap yard when i requested a good view and to cold water in the shower. There water was room temperature warm at best. A huge fail for standard expectations. There was a long line at the check out desk on departure. I was once a hotel manager myself so when I walked out I left the key on the desk and told the clerk that I was checking out. I did this because they took my card details on arrival and even charged 1p to it. I was then told to take my key and get in line. I waited for 20 minutes until I was able to check out. When I worked at a hotel you could leave your key and we would charge the bill to the card and send the receipt if requested. 

I booked the room based on breakfast being included but it wasn't stated that it was a buffet breakfast. This should be stated.

A real positive of the stay was that it had a good WiFi connected and the staff were very polite. I think I could do a better job of managing it though

Nice short break. We had a very pleasant one night stay at The Hickstead.  Staff were very helpful and friendly.  Our room - 111 - was large and well-equipped.  The bed was comfortable and there was plenty of hot water in the evening and morning.  The view from the window was of the over-flow car-park ... not the best view but this was not a problem for an overnight stay.  Evening meal was good.  However - because it was a Sunday - the breakfast was buffet-style and not as hot as I would have liked it (not helped by cold plates).  Still tasty though - especially the fried bread.  Would definitely stay here again

Lovely setting, lovely staff, lovely food.. The Hickstead is a lovely three star hotel set in lovely surroundings.  

The main building where the bar,lounge,reception and dinning room are is well presented and we were warmly greeted by the receptionist.  We were booked in for a two night stay with breakfast and dinner.

The hotel rooms is the only area I could say lets the side down.  Although they are clean and tidy (and recently refurbished), the rooms on a whole do feel a little tired - our room (a deluxe double) - had water stains on the ceiling and paint peeling in the bathroom.  I felt that if they had just spent that little extra, this room really would've lived up to its name.

That has to be my only criticism, but hopefully the owners may read my review and act upon it.

The food we had was lovely and the service was prompt.

All in all, our stay was very comfortable and I would go back in the future to get away from things!

Fantastic Wedding Reception. We cannot fault this hotel, and this is down to the superior service from the friendly staff that run it. We have been impressed from the start when we made initial inquiries, through meal tastings and then including the day itself. Nothing was too much trouble for Giles and Hannah and the team. The whole team made us feel welcome and this helped make our daughter's wedding day feel a special occasion, which of course it was. What was really impressive was that not only was there our party of 50 guests, most of whom stayed over but there was also another party of 30 people there at the same time and at no point did it feel that we were encroaching upon one another. Yes there were queues at the check in/out, but there were 80 people staying there so that was to be expected. Well done to all, we will certainly be recommending you to our friends and family for the future

Good stay.. I've got to say I like the classic lodge chain. We booked this hotel based on previous stays with classic lodge. It wasn't meant to be anything special. However we had a great time. The staff from the start were fantastic and I think that this was the major strength of the hotel.  

The evening meal was good and for 250 pounds for two nights, two evening meals with some wine included I thought was great value. 

The receptionists were both great and the maitre d' on the first night and breakfast was very accommodating for our 1 year old. 

Thank you

An amazing venue and excellent wedding venue. Thank you!. I just want to say on behalf of myself and my new husband how over the moon we were with the hotel and the service we received for our wedding on the 1st June.

Right from the start we were made to feel very welcome by Giles, Dawn and Hannah and the other members of the Hickstead team. Nothing was too much trouble and everything from the food to accommodation was out of this world.

The finishing touches such as the engraved cake knife were simply lovely and it meant so much to us that you went to the trouble of providing us with that lovely keepsake.

There simply are not enough words to explain what a fantastic job you all did and we will remember it always. The room looked lovely and there simply is nothing we could fault at all.

Thank you so much and we will definitely be recommending you to friends and family and will be coming back very soon to enjoy more wonderful food and company.

Thank you all again for all your hard work especially to Hannah who was our wedding planner at the Hickstead and went above and beyond to fulfil our dreams.

We look forward to seeing you soon

Comfortable and mostly good. A fairly basic hotel in terms of facilities, but what it does is mainly done well.  The setting - rural but handy for the A23 - is good (apart from the adjacent industrial estate, the grottier part of which forms the view from some rooms).  The service is mostly good, especially in the restaurant,  (though little interest was shown in the issue I raised about TV reception in my room - no BBC channels were available).  The room was spacious, comfortable and clean.  My overall rating reflects the value for money given the price I paid

Afternoon tea. I bought afternoon tea for my friend through buyagift. The food was lovely. The service a wee bit slow, well we didn't really see anyone that much! We expected the hotel to be surrounded by lovely grounds as we wanted a walk after being so stuffed! But there was nothing on site, we had to leave the grounds and 5 mins up the road was the main road so not great to walk! But the food was very nice (so much!) so thats the main thing really

Great Peaceful Location & Friendly Efficient Staff. With Brighton Hotels fully booked, I chose the Hickstead House Hotel.  It is just 20 minutes from Brighton on the A23 and very easy to find with clear 'brown' hotel signposts to guide you in.
Lauren on reception was charming and very helpful - when I booked I was told there was a large wedding party on the Saturday evening but with rooms almost fully booked I thought it was worth the risk. She again showed concern on booking in that we knew about the wedding party and advised that the fire doors would exclude any sound. 
 I need not have worried.  The disco was loud but the fire doors completely blanked out any noise and the wedding guests were careful not to disturb other hotel guests - thank you.
Our rooms (123 and 220) were spacious, very clean and peaceful, and the ensuite bathroom had an excellent powerful shower.  It is a 3-star hotel and so that is what you get although this hotel is better than any other 3-star in Brighton and far better value.
Despite the wedding party filling the bar, we were able to order late at night 22:50 some very tasty sandwiches - I had smoke salmon which was really generously filled and the egg mayonnaise was also very abundant and all came with some very well cooked chips and fresh salad.  The delightful waitress was only too pleased to serve us so late and despite being there beyond her going home time seemed genuinely pleased to help us.
Perhaps the highlight was the breakfast; the restaurant manager coped brilliantly (constantly surveying the diners to ensure our needs were met - unusual in my experience, so very well done -  and despite the packed room the service was quick and efficient. Coffee and toast appeared very promptly and I noticed that the buffet food was topped up regularly - they don't put too much out at a time in order to have it kept piping hot.  The sausages were good quality and well cooked, the back bacon was also well cooked (I hate undercooked bacon), there were fresh grilled tomoatoes, loads of mushrooms, black pudding and hot baked beans, and we ordered poached eggs which were perfectly cooked too; the scrambled egg on the buffet didn't look too inviting. There were fresh warm croissants, a choice of juices, yoghurts and fresh fruit.  The bowls of jams and marmalades looked homemade although I doubt they were and there was the largest pot of Marmite I have ever seen - well done the chefs and restaurants staff.
This was a really great value hotel in a lovely rural location with a huge car park.  The staff were always smiling and attentive - you can't ask for more than that.  We will be going back.  You will need a car or a £25 taxi ride into Brighton

Fabulous Find. I have just been here for 2 nights for my best friends wedding. The staff were amazing and so helpful. It wasn't the setting that I thought but once it was all dressed for the wedding it looked amazing. 

The bedrooms where lovely and very clean with a fabulous bathroom. We all had free wifi as well. 

The breakfast was really good as well. Nice and close to Brighton also which was very handy and only a 15 min taxi ride to the train station. I will stay here again next time that I visit Brighton as it is cheaper but still close enough to get there cheaply on the train

A good out of Brighton base. A group of 4 couples stayed here as we were heading to Brighton for a night out. We got a large taxi which cost £40 so the savings on the hotel were significant than staying in Brighton, also you could stay just the one night, which seems to be an issue in Brighton for most hotels.
When we arrived the weather was beautiful so we sat outside and enjoyed the weather. The barman came to the table twice offering to get our refills, so we didn't have to go to the bar! Nice touch. In the gardens there were tables and chairs as well as deckchairs and the garden was  a sun trap.
The room was spacious and we had a large double and single bed in our room. 
The bathroom was well proportioned and the shower was reasonably powerful.
Tea/Coffee and biscuits were in the room too
The bed was comfortable, which is the main thing! So a good nights sleep was had.
There didn't appear to be any temperature control, so the room was warm early evening when we were trying to get ready.
Breakfast was good, even though it was a buffet.
I would definitely stay again, as an alternative to the expensive Brighton hotels

Lovely afternoon tea. My grandson bought me here for afternoon tea today and it was a really pleasant experience. The staff were welcoming and helpful. The food was delicious and plentiful.The atmosphere enjoyable and the tables spaced well for privacy. The only reason it lost a star from me was the lack of service. Once served with tea ,sandwiches and a selection of scones and cakes we were left alone which was fine for the most part,but an offer of more tea would have been nice. We did catch a staff member eventually to replenish the pot of tea ,and to be fair ,when asked the tea came promptly,but it would have been a nice touch have had it offered. This is a small point and we did enjoy our tea and would recommend it to friends. Good value

Our special day made more special!!!. We had our wedding on 1st December 2012 and it was the best day ever! Not just the event but the work the hotel did to make it all a special day!! We couldn't praise the staff enough for their efforts. Nothing was ever too much trouble for anybody. The food was amazing rooms were amazing and my dancing was dodgy!!!! We both loved it that much we will be booking in again when it's our 1st anniversary :-)

Summer break. We stayed three night on the all inclusive package, the weather was very hot, but we were provided with a fan which really helped us to sleep.  On the whole the restaurant food was good although if you stayed more than three nights you might struggle with the variety on offer.  The food was well presented, apart from the over cooked garlic bread and the breakfast bacon was dry and over cooked, the continental breakfast had a selection of fruit and cereal with natural yoghurt, flavoured yoghurt and a selection of juice. We would also recommend the cream tea especially if you can enjoy it outside under the umbrella. A little bit of extra attention to minor maintenance issues (bathroom fan not working) would make things even better.  This being said we would stay again when we next visit the area

Good - but could be better. We have now stayed there twice when visiting relatives in the area. Rooms are comfortable and well-appointed, and the breakfast was an improvement over our previous stay. The property is beginning to look a little frayed around the edges, but the staff are cheery and helpful.Just didn't seem to represent good value for the price we paid

Clean, comfortable and pleasant staff.. I stayed for two nights on business. The hotel was not my choice but I found it to be clean, comfortable and friendly.  It is a bit of stuck in the middle of nowhere, well if like I was having travelled by train and working in Haywards Heath.  The staff were very friendly from the reception staff, barman and the restaurant waiters.  The choice was fairly limited if staying for a while, however the food I had on the two nights was excellent. 10 ounce pork chop on mustard mash with a thick ju, gravy. Very nice. There were also a special meal choice on each night. I was served promptly and politely. I enjoyed the restaurant experience even on my own.  The place was quite busy but the standards were maintained.  The breakfast offered a good choice of continental and English  style, again prompt service as there were plenty of guests wanting to get away to work. The only criticism would be that there is no air conditioning in the bedrooms and it was very warm at night.  However there was good wifi freely available

Wot- no aircon!. This is a modern hotel in an attractive setting. It has been created by adding an extension to an older property and the mix blends very well. The rooms are large and equipped with table. chairs and the usual TV and hostess tray. However there is no air conditioning and the windows are not large. As a result the room was stifling and even the fan, produced on request did little to improve matters.  Despite the comfortable bed a good nights sleep was difficult.There appears to be air conditioning in the restaurant, so why not else where? The bathroom was spotless with good quality towels and toiletries.
We received a warm welcome at reception and the restaurant service was good, as was the buffet breakfast. However I felt the B& B rate was expensive

Fab staff and good food. We stayed at The Hickstead Hotel for one night whilst working at the Hickstead Showground. We found the staff very helpful and polite. The food was very good and the room comfortable. The price of the overall stay was very reasonable and we well definitely stay here again when we are down that end of the country

Tea for Two. We were given a voucher as a gift - 'Luxury Tea forTwo'. The Hickstead Hotel is an attractive red brick building set in pleasant grounds. The staff were polite and attentive, The room was airy and comfortable with a view of the garden. Tea ( or coffee ) arrived promptly, shortly followed by a generous quantity of finger sandwiches,cakes and scones.Nicely presented,no complaints at all - very civilised - very English!

LOVELY AFTERNOON. Have just enjoyed an afternoon tea for two at this beautiful hotel. On arrival was greeted with a smile and shown to our table.  Tea was served and a plate of sandwiches and a nice cake stand with chocolate ecclairs, scones with cream and jam , strawberry meringue and lemon drizzle cake. We were told to take our time and enjoy the tea. Not rushed and a good atmosphere. Must say enjoyed it very much, but did come away with full stomachs. Would recommened highly

Nice but not special. A gift for my parents.  We were well treated - the food was good but having experienced several other 'afternoon teas' this one lacked that extra something that makes it an occasion.
There was a good selection of sandwiches and cakes - warm scones (whipped cream not clotted but you can't have everything) - but no tea menu - we were offerred tea or coffee although when asked they found some fruit tea.  There may have been a vast selection of teas it was just not offerred.  The food and drinks were presented and then left with no follow up.  
The extra ooomph was missing

very comfortable hotel. We have stayed here a few times now.  The staff are welcoming and efficient, the rooms are a good size and have good TV and tea making facilities.  The breakfast is excellent.  It is quiet.
It is in a very good position to explore Sussex and fairly close to Gatwick airport.
The whole experience is very pleasant

Afternoon tea for two. The Hickstead is one of the growing ranks of hotels offering special afternoon tea.
This was delightful. Greeted by attentive, friendly staff and shown to a choice of table in the lounge.

Wonderful views of the Sussex countryside. Excellent spead including sandwiches, scones, jam and cream and fresh cream cakes and as much tea as you want.

Will go again.  FIRST CLASS

Pleasant but nothing special. A one night stay recently (with a special offer) when visiting family nearby. Wonderful setting and very tidy appearance of the hotel. Inside seemed a little dated and the room was clean and tidy but again lacked the finishing touches. 
Breakfast was hearty in the morning, but served in a restaurant that was too hot... there were no windows open and service that was delivered like it was a theatre performance to a large crowd! Loud and overstated!

Nice - but could do better. We recently stayed for 1 night as we were visiting relatives near-by. We had a family room for 2 adults and 1 child.

As other have mentioned, the hotel needs an update in certain areas, with paint/plaster peeling and very old carpets.

Our room was adaquate but everything had been set up for 2 people not 3. upon asking for the extras, they were sorted quickly and politely by the staff. The extractor fan wasn't working in the toilet and the toilet didn't seem to flush correctly, which left it dripping constantly all night. The room was hot and a wlarger window that opened fuly would have been better.

The breakfast was a very good range but the cooked parts were overcooked in parts.

The staff were very friendly and most helpful at all times.

I would stay here again - but subject to cost, as it is a little too much for what you get

Wedding. From the moment of looking for a venue to too accommodate out entire wedding package the service was of excellent standard. Giles the manager was extremely professional throughout and on the ball at all times.
The wedding itself was perfect and everyone commented on how perfect it was from venue to food to everything.
Jenny and Hannah and Laura and Lauren were all exceptional.
Would highly recommend for a day to remember

Not as advertised!. My wife and I booked a night in this hotel because one of our children had given us a Virgin Experience "Dining Treat for Two" voucher as a gift. From the list provided this hotel was nearest to our home and in the "blurb" it promised, "an intimate dining experience" and the experience of, "simply relax for an evening of pure indulgence". So...we expected a well cooked and professionally presented meal. We  hoped for perhaps even a gourmet meal- well  you would wouldn't you?
WRONG! First of all I must say that the staff were friendly and helpful throughout but that's as far as it went. Our table was graced with one sad and dusty silk flower with the cheapest paper napkins. There was a side plate with knife - never used because no bread was forthcoming in any shape or form. The meal itself was best described as an average to poor pub meal. 90% of the food was the commercial pre-prepared kind. My wife's meal was sent back since it was a dried up overcooked disaster.
Our room was big and clean, the bathroom had tiles lifting and a ventilation fan that never switched off - keeping my wife awake all night. At breakfast my wife's poached egg was a solid yolk surrounded by grey scum and...oh yes they forgot the bacon! The coffee was absolutely tasteless and my wife's tea left a great deal to the imagination. What a pity. Ok if you are a tired commercial traveller just wanting a nights stopover but certainly a great disappointment to us

One night. We went for one night as we'd been invited to a friends wedding, the reception being held in the hotel.
Reasonably priced hotel. The service was excellent from the receptionists to bar and waiting staff. The wedding celebration was a hoot, dancing and people chatting loudly. However when in our room, very near to the disco area, there was little or no noise, the fire doors suppressed all noise, and more to the point they shut quietly. No banging of corridor doors in the small hours, unlike so many other hotels we've stayed in. What a treat.
The room was newly refurbished, clean and well appointed. We asked for more tea etc (English tea drinkers !!) and it was all was delivered in 5 minutes. This in a hotel with some 150 wedding guests and hotel residents. Impressive. Definitely recommend this place as a quiet stopover while exploring Sussex

Very comfortable. We stayed at the hotel following a wedding held there. Check in was smooth, the room was comfortable, the bathroom clean and well equipped. The room was a good temperature and a good size. Plenty of hanging space. Staff very efficient and helpful. Breakfast was very good with a really nice buffet. Would definitely stay again

Virgin Experience voucher. Stayed 1 night on a Virgin voucher for bed breakfast and evening meal. Unlike other reviewers I was not expecting gourmet food or 5 star service, but I was very impressed with what we had. Yes the napkins were paper and there was no bread offered, but with a 3 course dinner I didn't need bread. 
The hotel was clean and well presented, with chocolates, water , robes and slippers in the room. The bathroom is a little dated with a shower curtain that attacks you as you shower, but it was clean and everything worked. 
We did have some problems with the heating, with nothing during the evening but it came on full blast during the night. I think this could be a changing season thing.
The staff were professional and pleasant. I thought the food was well presented and very tasty. The menu wasn't extensive, but there was enough choice. The waiting staff were attentive without being too fussy, ( there were only 5 tables occupied). 
Breakfast was a buffet with plenty of choice of hot food or cereals, croissants etc.
The location is good, 3 minutes off the A23, but no noise

Not the best value for money. We stayed one night while visiting friends nearby; we arrived in the middle of a wedding reception but our room was on the other side of the hotel and we couldn't hear the main party. All was good until some of the wedding guets decided to continue partying in the room beneath ours at 1.30. There was music blaring, loud drunken voices and lots of door banging. We called the night porter and by 2.30 they had quietened down enough for us to eventually get back to sleep. Obviously the hotel can't help having antisocial guests but not getting a good night's sleep puts us off having more weekends away. 
Seeing that another reviewer had a bathroom where the ventilation fan wouldn't switch off, we had one that wouldn't switch on.
On the plus side, the room was dark - no green or red LEDS glowing from smoke alarms or aircon machines, no minibar fridge to hum, no traffic noise, a fairly comfortable bed, spacious room and very friendly staff. 
We didn't get up for breakfast as we decided to sleep in after the bad night, so can't comment on the food

Friendly service and a good location. We needed somewhere in this area for a couple of nights and this hotel is close to a junction on the A23 giving good access north and south. We took the 'Autumn Break' deal. This gave full B&B plus an evening meal with fizz and canapés beforehand. Everyone was helpful and friendly. The room on the first floor was a good size with two comfortable chairs and a table as well as a desk and dressing table area. We would be happy to go back here.

All the food was great too!

Yummy afternoon tea. A friend and I enjoyed a delicious afternoon tea of sandwiches and cake, which I was given as a gift. There was more than we could eat but you even get a doggy bag with the leftovers! As much tea or coffee as you want too. A lovely treat. The service was friendly and efficient and we were able to take our time and have a god chat. I would recommend this as an indulgent treat or as a gift

buissiness trip. BE WARNED!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Do not stay in room 117 or 118. The interconnecting door has an old fashioned key hole with no key placed in it.....The concern here is that you can be seen by the occupant/s in the adjoining room. I was able to see a gentleman standing in his room using his mobile phone. My concern here was that if I could see him....then he could obviously see me! I complained and was moved to another room. I was also offered 5 pounds off my bill for my trouble.The manager, Mr Paul, informed me that in a six year period (since the rooms were revamped) they have never recieced any complaints of this nature...... (very concerning) as this poses the question why not? I demanded that these rooms were taking off sale until this issue had been addressed. Mr Paul confirmed that they would not sell the rooms (other than to families) and that work would commence immediately (I am not sure I believe this statement especially as according to Mr Paul he had recieved no previous complaints). Mr Paul and his team have a duty of care to protect and maintain their guests privacy and well being. Im my opinion they have totally failed to do so. This was my second visit to the hotel but after this incident I will never stay at this hotel again and I strongly recommend that before you do that you seek confirmation that this issue has been addressed. I feel my privacy has been totally violated. Also the bill was paid in full as I felt that Mr Paul and his team believed that I was only complaining because I was looking for a discount!!!! talk about add insult to injury

Average stay. We stayed as a part of a group booking. We paid £155.00 for two nights stay bed and breakfast. On the face of it, not bad. On the Saturday night we joined a group dinner meal that was £32.00 per head. The meal was not good value, with little or no choice and very average, so imagine how great a deal we thought we had, when today we meat a couple who had stayed for one night (Saturday) and paid £109 for room, dinner bed and breakfast. Considering we were quoted £89 as a 'special rate for the one night bed and breakfast, that means we paid £40.00 over the top for a pretty average dinner. That said the overall package was ok and I would say average. We stayed in room 202, when we arrived the room was cold, but a quick call to reception and the receptionist arrived with tools In hand to 'sort' the faulty thermostat out on the radiator, which she did, but you would have thought that this should have already been checked. The door slammed shut each time and we noticed that the door damper was dripping oil onto the carpet and had been for some time! This was reported. The rooms and the hotel are a little tired and the view from the rear of the hotel (our room) looks out onto a dump of a yard! Not a room with a view! So ask for a room facing the front of the hotel if booking.  Staff were very friendly and willing to help, so no issues there. There is an unpleasant smell in the general reception area.  Good location for visiting the area and loads of parking and it is a out of the way and quiet, except the fire alarm went off at 1.0am this morning for what reason we don't know.
Breakfast, Buffet style. Why? Why not freshly cooked, the hotel should be able to cope. I just don't like dried out and stewed food,  and uncooked mushrooms, let alone dried black pudding and bacon. Well Saturday's breakfast was not as good as Sunday's breakfast. Definitely down to the chef! Sunday's was much better.  My advise to the Hotel is to do away with the buffet breakfast, at least the cooked breakfast. Part cook the bacon and sausages and finish cooking to order, would be so much better.
Beds are hard. Bathroom was ok and shower good, but the maintenance guys need to take more attention to detail. When replacing screws on the shower handle, don't use plain steel, the rust makes down the tiles make the bath tiled area look old and tired. Not a good impression.
Would I stay here again? Probably not if I was on a personal weekend away, but for a group booking, it was probably ok. would be a reasonable wedding venue

***6 STAR SERVICE***. 8 of us stayed here recently, occupying 4 rooms, prior to and following a family funeral in Brighton. The Hickstead is by all accounts a fairly average hotel. It is clean and well appointed, but the rooms are just starting to look a little used. All of the usual ameneties are available for a property of this type, there is tea and coffee in the room, a bar and a reasonable resturant.

What really sets the Hickstead head and shoulders above other similar hotels are the staff. Every single member of staff from the maids to the general manager were just perfect in every respect. Our party included a 2 year old and an 80 year old who both demanded special care and attention, the staff were always happy to help and do almost anything to enable us to enjoy what was actually quite a sad affair.

To cap it all, I managed to leave a suit in my room and the hotel staff posted it back to me in Nottingham. I can't imagine a Travelodge doing that?

I would reccomment the Hickstead to anyone stopping in the Burgess Hill area

Delighted. We spent one night here (room 211) and found it to be excellent, despite one or two critical reports on Tripadvisor regarding The Hickstead, I went ahead and booked. The hotel staff were charming and helpful, the building was pristine, the bed the most comfortable hotel bed we have slept in this year, nice bath, a decent sized flat screen TV -  and the full English breakfast was plentiful and freshly cooked. We would definately book in here again

Delighted. We spent one night at the Hickstead Hotel and have to report it`s excellence. I was slightly concerned regarding some of the critical comments of The Hickstead on Trip Advisor but went ahead and booked anyway. Delightful staff, pristine throughout, probably the most comfortable bed of all hotels we have stayed in for a year, plus a first class freshly cooked full English breakfast. The hotel was in surrounding countryside so no disturbance from traffic noises. We would most definately book this hotel again

Couple staying the night.. My husband and myself were staying for just the one night to visit our son. Our room was fine, beds were comfortable enough as well as clean. 
We were greeted kindly by all members of staff, although it did seem a little mechanical- aside from one of the young waitresses at breakfast (I believe her name was Emily) sincerely asked us how we were and where we had travelled from; she also went out of her way to serve us specific items due to allergies etc. The young man working alongside her was also very attentative, and Jenny who served us in the evening was also lovely, with a smile on her face. 
The food was lovely and desert was particularly tasteful and presented very well! (We ordered the banana split and the treacle tart). For breakfast there was a hot Full English buffet topped up regularly with fresh food. A variety of juices were also topped up regularly, and there was also a large selection of cereals and fruits. (I would however have liked there to have been Mango juice on offer, rather than two jugs of orange.)
Location is fine, although it would be reccommended to bring your car along, as there isn't very good access to public transport, and there isn't much of anything within walking distance.
Would definitely recommend this hotel to others, but don't expect too much from it as it is only advertising for 3 stars

The best place for celebration. Thank you to Giles and his team for helping me arrange my husbands birthday celebration anabsolutely fantastic lunch. I know we can relax, enjoy and be looked after. Thank you so much for making another day so marvelous. The food was beyond delicious

Christmas celebration. We were with a party of 50+ for a Christmas celebration dinner and booked in for one night so we could drink without having to worry about driving. The staff were extremely friendly and helpful and the food was some of the best we've had at similarly large dinners. The bed was very comfortable although I personally found the room rather too warm and the atmosphere very dry. From a critical point of view, an overhead light would have been helpful in the bedroom as well as the provision of bathrobes and the towels themselves were rather on the rough side. However, these slight niggles would not stop us booking at The Hickstead or any of the group's hotels in the future. Overall it was a very pleasant experience. Thank you

Good stay for the holidays. Very friendly and helpful staff combined with e good rate over the holidays made this stay just shy of four stars.  They just need to renew the beds, which were squeaky and not so comfortable and update the bathroom, which in need of sone TLC

New Year's Eve celebration. Our party of 7 had a very enjoyable and memorable celebration with five course meal and excellent waiter service throughout our stay.  The resident DJ was very personable and gelled well with the guests.  The quiz played at the end of dinner and prior to the dancing starting was pitched well, and his delivery of it was fun for all.  Food was excellent and presented well and speedily.  We found all the staff really pleasant and more than willing to help.  We would definitely recommend this venue for any such celebration.  Found breakfast a little chaotic due to lack of seating as all guests had decided to descend at the same time, but again, the staff  dealt with this situation professionally.  Our bill on departure was wrong with items charged that we hadn't had, however reception rectified without question

a ggod experience. my wife and I really enjoyed an overnight stay
although it was rather quiet

we had a good meal but were the only poeple in the dining room
the service was excellent

the breakfast was also good

a shame more people didn't find it!

Fantastic cream tea with excellent service. We went back for another cream tea this week and loved it. The portions are very generous - a substantially sized scone, cream eclair, meringue, cookie, slab of lemon drizzle each plus cream, jam, sandwiches and tea or coffee. They were happy to provide vegetarian sandwiches and kindly checked which fillings I would like before making them.
My companion was very happy as they made him some extra sarnies and coffee served with a smile as he liked them so much.
Had to take leftovers home to enjoy later.
A real hidden gem - go and try it

Friendly and Comfortable!. Came on a Valentine's night special. Had a great Four course meal. Excellent food and excellent service just like you get at reception  or at the bar. Rooms are very comfortable and quiet an excellent retreat after a good evening's meal! This is the second time we have come here so it must be good!

Hungry. We arrived at the hotel at around 8 15pm on valentines day for a romantic night away. We were called at mid day to be told there were no tables left in their bistro, but when we asked were told room service was available still (perfect)! We settled down in our room had a look at the menu and decided we would have a quick bath then order some food. The bathroom was okay but their is rust on the wall and the soap holders which is pretty grim also chips in the bath. 

We got out the bath and called room service to order, we were at this point told we couldn't order any hot food as it was past 9pm.. I looked at my watch 9.02pm. 

As someone paying for a service I would expect better than this, especially as the restaurant was full and there are no other options to dine out at this time. 

The only other thing that slightly annoyed us was that the bed was on wheels. Not great for a "romantic" night away, but this actually became quite amusing

Valentines night special. We arrived to be welcomed very warmly, our room was very comfortable and very clean.  We went down to start our stay with a lovely gin and tonic in the lounge very nice, then relaxed changed for dinner we had lovely canapes and a glass of champagne we were then showed to our table in the very nicely set up dining room, we were asked on sitting down if we wanted water, but no wine list offered I think that is one thing that might be addressed as our starter arrived and we had to ask for the wine list also other tables had been offered bread but we had to ask, just little things but could easily be addressed the food and the staff were excellent and we thoroughly enjoyed.  We then had a few drinks in the lounge, then off to bed.  Good nights sleep very comfortable bed.  Breakfast the next morning was excellent and overall a very nice stay

Fantastic friendly service.  We stayed for one night on the valentine package. Although the hotel is dated the food and service more than made up for this. Fantastic hot shower to. Thanks for the lovely biscuits which were a nice touch. Staff seemed to genuinely want to give a good service. Breakfast was well cooked and had all you would want

A pleasant experience. This is a pleasant and peaceful hotel set in its own extensive grounds well away from road noise but excellently positioned for quick access to Brighton etc via the fast A27. Parking was free and abundant and the site was very well lit on our late night return. The hotel is basically in two parts; the original house and the more modern and corporate looking accommodation building which is more reminiscent of a Travellodge. However, that’s where the similarity ends as this hotel is very well run by smart, professional, polite and sociable front-of-house staff who are a credit to the management. 

Whilst some of the public (landing and corridor) areas in the newer building are rather tired and in need of a little refurbishment, our room was fine and on a par with any other ‘chain’ hotel. The  'house' is very smart and tastefully presented throughout, with a spacious and sunny dining/breakfast room. Service was impeccable throughout our stay and the full English breakfast was exceptionally good. At the risk of sounding controversial, I have to say it was a refreshing pleasure to be served by smiling and communicative British staff with no language or cultural issues. Our two night stay at the Hickstead was peaceful and enjoyable and we’d have no hesitation in recommending it to others and there’s a good chance we will return here during our next visit to West Sussex. All in all vey satisfied

Afternoon tea. Came to the Hickstead Hotel for afternoon tea. Food and service was excellent. One of the members of our party got lost as the sat nav sent her to the wrong location, and the waitress who was just going off duty offered to drive to her so that she could follow her back to the hotel. I was so impressed with this. Very friendly and helpful staff, thank you!

Return Visit. A return visit on the occasion of my wife's' birthday, and another very pleasant stay.  We booked - as before - upon receiving the winter break leaflet in the post.  This was for Dinner, bed and breakfast with a complimentary bottle of prosecco.
We were made aware when booking that there would be a wedding taking place on our chosen date.  We were allocated room 118 - which I note has been referred to in a previous review.  This room, along with room 117, have an interconnecting door.  I note from the comments of the previous reviewer that it could be possible to 'peep through' the keyhole, and that the reviewer did just that!  Interesting that one would want to do that, but each to their own I guess, not quite sure the reviewers motive here?  Still - I note that the keyhole has a cover over it.
Very similar to other rooms we have stayed in at this hotel - my only one gripe would be that the interconnecting door does mean you can hear the occupants of the other room - other rooms we have had in this hotel are very well insulated from noise.
As usual, the food was to a high standard, and it was nice again to be recognised and welcomed by Jenny in the restaurant.  It is this attentiveness that sees us returning - a real credit to the hotel.  With the majority of guests at the hotel attending the wedding, there were  just three tables in use in the restaurant when we dined - nice, quiet and intimate!
We did not get disturbed at all by the wedding that was in progress, although the occupants of room 117 decided upon their return from the wedding to have a chat at 3am using some wonderful colorful metaphors!  Still, judging from the way they spoke, I would not have classed them as overly intelligent, and courtesy to others may not be their priority.....
Due to the large onslaught of wedding guests expected for breakfast, there was a buffet laid on as opposed to breakfast being cooked upon request.  This was no issue, and the food was all very nice, piping hot and a great selection for all tastes.  We headed to the restaurant quite early, so it was very quiet.
So all in all another pleasant stay, and I really do not see the issue with the keyhole in room 118.
We are mindful that we were introduced to this hotel as part of a wedding party, so have the benefit of seeing both sides of the story when it comes to the individual guest/wedding guest debate!
I expect we will be back again in 2015

Our Third Visit. This was Robert and my third visit to the Hickstead.  Arriving the weekend of the 22 March.  It is always great to come back to somewhere where they remember you and are so pleased to see you back.
We had been given an afternoon tea gift from a member of the family and we decided to make a weekend of it and stay at the Hickstead.
Robert my partner booked us in for Saturday night and when we went down to dinner we were met by Jenny, the Restaurant supervisior like an old friend! Her professionalism and personality is really infectious and she really does make you feel so very special.
I must compliment the Chef on an excellent dinner saturday night.  The food was fantastic and I was offered extra veg as I don't eat potatoes. The lemon possett was to die for! We returned on Sunday afternoon after a trip into Brighton to have our afternoon tea as there was a wedding on during Saturday, but we didint mind and the afternoon tea was delicious!  Thank goodness we didnt have lunch and had walked a fair distance before we arrived to sample a fantastic afternoon tea and to see Jenny again.
We will be back and thank you for providing such fantastic customer service.  The gentleman at Breakfast was also very professional and very attentive and the buffet breakfast is of a very high standard and plenty of it!   Jenny's daughter on reception remembered us too.
See you very soon and thank you for a very enjoyable weekend.  Mark

Great food and staff in quiet surroundings. Stayed here for just 1 night on a package which included dinner, bed and breakfast plus a bottle of Prosecco.  We'd spent the day in Brighton and it's only about a 20-25 minute drive to the Hickstead Hotel.  Very quiet even with a wedding reception in full swing - they didn't disturb us.  The room was slightly dated but very clean and comfortable.  Dinner was great - food gorgeous and hot and the service from Jenny was excellent.  Breakfast next morning also great - when several young male members of the wedding party came down the restaurant manager brought out a jug of water for them as they were feeling the effects of the night before!  Nice touch I thought.  All the staff were helpful and are an asset to this hotel - would certainly recommend it and stay there again if in the area

Excellent Afternoon Tea. We went to this hotel for afternoon tea for mothers day, it was brilliant and the staff were very helpful and nice. The grounds are lovely and the hotel is very easy to find. We will look to go back there again

A little tired but very comfortable. Very comfortable rooms, if a little tired. The staff were very friendly. I stayed here weekdays for over 5 months and never had reason for complaint. The grounds are well kept and the surrounding areas had nice walks and good cycling roads

Exceeded our expectations. I can honestly say that from the moment we arrived at The Hickstead Hotel, we were very impressed. Our room was spacious, comfortable and provided everything we could possibly want. It was spotlessly clean as were all public areas of the hotel and the provision of mineral water and fresh fruit in the room was for us 'that extra mile'. The food consisting of afternoon tea and a three course dinner, was fresh and well presented, the cooking was above average for the price. The staff were friendly, polite and extremely helpful, especially in the restaurant. If this all sounds too good to be true - it isn't. If other hotels in the group are as good as this, we are keen to try one or two

Most enjoyable stay. From arrival the staff were most helpful and friendly. Parking was easy - always a bonus! The room was spaceous, clean and well presented. A central main light would have been an advantage, as the 'romantic' lighting was too dim for reading.  Breakfast provided plenty of choice and the dining room looked welcoming in the early morning sunlight

way beyond what we expected. Set in lovely grounds which were well cared for. Despite arriving in the middle of a wedding we were booked in very efficiently and made to feel welcome . We were taken to our roomto make it easier for us to get through the guests the staff couldn't have been more helpful . Our room was spacious and contained everything we needed for a relaxing and strees free 4 nights . The hotel was spotlessly clean every where. Breakfast was cooked well and a good choice .waiting staff were so helpful and very pleasent .always a good morning and have you slept well .we ate every night in the hotel . The chef needs congratulating for his superb cooking . The food was top notch and service was superb the team all worked well together .I would like to give a special mention to laura , deborah.
