And especially jenny they made our stay really enjoyable.we would definitely krecommend it to others and we would definitely stay again if in the area

A Beautiful Event Venue. We stayed overnight after attending a birthday party. The party was well organised and everyone enjoyed it. We had a beautiful quiet and comfortable room with a view up to the South Downs. After a leisurely breakfast, we went out walking in the spectacular countryside. All the staff were very friendly and helpful

Very nice stay and excellent food!. Had a really nice stay at the Hickstead Hotel - very good food in the restaurant for dinner, a relaxed and friendly atmosphere and a very comfortable room.  The surroundings are really peaceful and that makes a good night's sleep possible.  Will certainly stay again

Welcoming. Very friendly polite staff and accommodating for my 15 month year old. Rooms where clean and nice and breakfast was great with plenty of choice! Good value for money. Lovely settings and surrounding area of countryside would def visit again

Excellent service, rooms were dated.. Stayed in this hotel for one night whilst visiting family and friends. The service was very good and all the staff very friendly. We had an evening meal in the hotel which was also very good with a wide selection of choice. The room was clean but seemed a bit dated. Our room was in the wing off the main building which seemed very "1960's" and in my view would benefit from some updating. That said, it was very comfortable and good value. Also an excellent location with good access to major roads

Nice enough!. Booked up 1 night for our 18th wedding anniversary and did mention this on the booking form, but no offer for special requests(wine/champagne in room/chocolates/flowers etc)even though I did ask. Very friendly staff and the restaurant were very obliging when I found my dinner wasn't quite up to scratch(veg cold), but breakfast was not such a great experience. Ordered toast, tea and coffee and had to wait a considerable time for it to arrive and people ordering after us seemed to get served before!! Did find a nasty place in the dining room floor where my foot went down, think that could do with being repaired. 
Room was clean but could do with a bit of updating, bathroom wasn't the cleanest I have seen, I really do hate hairs in bath and the fan didn't work properly. The bed was fine, clean and very comfy but the heat of the room was unbearable overnight, even with the window open! Suggest maybe lighter duvets.
The area for getting into Brighton was ideal not too far but far enough out to think you are in the countryside

A wonderful stay. I came here with my boyfriend as we were celebrating for our anniversary in Brighton and read reviews on this hotel. We caught the bus on the way in (as didn't drive) and as we are 18 normally our service at hotels have been a little distant, awkward and rude. However I can happily say that hickstead hotel made our weekend a fantastic one. The staff were so lovely to us and to all other visitors. Hannah in particular at the desk was lovely and gave us a map on brighton and helped us out with where to go. Room was clean and tidy, toiletries and coffee and tea making avaliable, some reviews have said it looks dated, well from a younger point of view I didn't see that at all! The bed was ridiculously comfy and that bath was wonderful as it filled up very quickly with a good depth. There was a useful book on our desk answering any questions and a large tv was a great addition. The atmosphere of the hotel was great from the wonderful staff and I would like to thank hickstead hotel for a lovely weekend. Will definitely be coming back when I can drive!

Another lovely stay. We stay at this hotel regularly while visiting family in Brighton. It is always comfortable, convenient and friendly. The prices are average for Brighton, and there is plenty of parking, unlike in the city centre. The food in the restaurant is good, and service is attentive and efficient

A happy medium.. This hotel is in good proximity for Burgess Hill & Haywards Heath. Not a lot to say about it really, nothing to write home about & nothing to moan about either. Staff are friendly enough, not quite as clean & shinny as a Major Chain (Marriott or Holiday Inn). We got the room for £85 through booking.com. So was very good value, buffet breakfast included was good too

Another lovely stay! . My wife and I stayed last night to celebrate our 2nd anniversary, as we had last year for our 1st. We absolutely love the hotel. It very much is what it is, a middle ranking hotel, with friendly, helpful staff, good food and a reasonably priced bar. And a lovely full English next morning. For our anniversary we didn't want over pretentious 5* (if we had, we'd have gone to one!) - we just wanted a pleasant country environment and good food! Really really worth a stay!

Pleasant stay. My wife and I use Classic Lodges because they are reasonably priced in good locations
I would class this as a good average Hotel...Staff very helpful in all departments  no complaints with room very tidy  but stairs and corridor looking very tired and in need of a repaint
Restaurant was nice and comfortable   the food OK bit more choice would have better also lack of choice in the wine department  no new world wines  and the quality average taste but nothing to complain about  we enjoyed our stay

Pleasant Stay. Stayed at this hotel for a couple of nights. Staff were very helpful and the breakfasts were very good. Like other comments which have been made the Hotel is looking very tired and needs painting. The room was comfortable enough and we had tea making facilities and we had clean sheets and towels on both nights. I am sure they would get more five star ratings if they were to refurbish the corridors and couple that with a coat of paint. We did enjoy our stay and would recommend it as it is in a beautiful area and convenient for Brighton

Grim. Stayed one night whilst on business.  Room 'tired' looking and smelled stale, needed a good dust.  Shower curtain inadequate so bathroom floor dangerous after using shower.  
Room service food inedible.  Breakfast adequate.
Staff pleasant and apologetic, and insisted I not pay for food, although I would have been happy to pay for breakfast.
Adequate as an hotel but in no way does it live up to its advertisments.  Better to just convert to a care home and be done with it....  
Travelodge down the road better value and cleaner

Nice hotel with stunning views. Driving up the long driveway to a large carpark, a very warm and friendly welcome from the receptionist. Told me anything I need to know. 
The room was nice and spacious, clean.  A very large and comfortable bed, slept well in it. 
Shower was good, powerful and hot 
Wifi was good though out the hotel. Good breakfast lot's to offer, very friendly staff. 
Just a couple of bad points, 1st you can hear every room doorbeing shut, late in the evening and early morning and 2nd the evening meal was nice don't get me wrong but a bit on the pricey side for what you get. 
But all in all a superb hotel would stay again

A sussex gem. we stayed here very much by chance , we were going to stay near by but told it was full when got there . But what a superb place .We stayed on a special offer of room evening dinner with glass of wine and breakfast . The receptionist was so friendly as too were the other staff, room was spotless very attractive sunny position . Food was fantastic 3 course dinner brilliant and the breakfast was a true full english . WELL DONE to everyone involved

Lovely Hotel. Stayed  with my daughter in a very large twin bedded room (room215). We stayed here because the location is only half a mile from Hickstead show ground. The beds were comfortable, the room spotless and very quiet being at the end of the building.  The staff were friendly and helpful. We had an evening meal at the hotel.  The main course was excellent, but the dessert did not come up to the same standard.  This is my only criticism of this otherwise lovely hotel.  Would most definitely stay again

Good hotel in excellent location. GJK

Our stay of three nights at Hickstead was comfortable.
Room was spacious and everything worked - only constructive criticism was that the lighting was not really adequate for the whole room.
Restaurant was above usual chain hotel standard, and the food was well prepared with a varied menu, which had different specials every day.
All the staff were helpful, cheerful and welcoming.
It is well situated for a wide variety of leisure trips in this area of Sussex.
We will recommend this hotel to our friends and family, and we will certainly stay here again

Wonderful venue for our wedding - great staff made the day. We celebrated our wedding here on 17th May. From the initial booking through to the day everyone who worked at the hotel was helpful, paid attention to detail and could not do enough for us and our guests to make our special day even more perfect. A number of us stayed the night before, and had a very enjoyable meal in the restaurant. Impeccable service, good standard of cuisine all of which continued through to breakfast the next day. The rooms we all had were of a good standard, well furnished and clean. An added bonus was the free WIFI available throughout the hotel. The wedding ceremony, and the meal after went without a hitch - the food was served promptly to all tables, was hot, and of good standard - many of our guests commented positively on this. The Hickstead Hotel was a perfect choice for our wedding, the grounds are also very pleasant, and the location is great - close to the A23 and near to Burgess Hill for train statins

Pleasant stay. Stayed at the Hickstead for two nights last week. Good location for visiting Brighton and other places of interest in west and east Sussex, like Lewes Castle, well worth seeing. Our room was large, well appointed, bed was comfortable, bathroom fresh and clean with a fantastic shower.  The full breakfast is cooked to order and very generous in proportion, so it is best to stipulate what and how much you want on your plate depending on your appetite at that time of day.  The evening meal was good, also appeared freshly cooked rather than just micro-waved. 

The hotel seems to derive much of its business from conferences, meetings and functions which in no way detracted from the service given to the leisure traveller.  All staff members were   friendly, very helpful and cheerful and the general ambience was reminiscent of gentler times and generally restful.  I consider the Hickstead to be at the top of its class

great location and great value for money. We stayed one night on a special half board deal.not expecting much but great location for our trip. I could not belive this was a great place a large private car park staff very helpful and well worth the visit,A large bedroom with table and sitting area good free Wi-Fi tea coffee etc in the room very nice.The bathroom could do with a little up dating but quite happy.The major surprise was the set priced evening meal good choice and the quality was first class the lamb was the best I have ever tasted and was cooked to perfection well presented this chef knows what the customer wants.Breakfast was also good cooked to order and great service we will be back here again just to go back for the food

Lovely relaxing break in the countryside. We had a good  deal for half board under the Discover Sussex package and were not dissapointed. The room was spacious and clean with a great shower, staff were all friendly and efficient. The food was of a high quality  with a good choice on menu.The other customers were polite and courteous. In all, very good for a 3 star rated thotel

Was Dinner worth the waiting for. I booked a room for a one night stay at the Hicksted Hotel. 
As I walked in reception told me before I put my bags down that the restaurant was vary busy tonight and there would not be a table before 8pm. No problem I said as it was 7.15pm at that time.
Well the rooms are ok and clean, the public areas are a little tired and a bit late 80s and the grounds not loved,but for £89.00 B&B that's ok in this part of the world.

When I came down into the bar at 8.15 I herd the restaurant manger tell the barmen not to let any more diners in as they can not cope tonight.
The restaurant had only 24 covers/diners in it, considering it has 50 covers in it so only just over half full I can't image what the service would be like if they had all the tables full.

So I decide to have a beer and come back at 9.pm When I came back I was met with we are vary busy and it will be vary slow service tonight from the manger.
Well in the end I got a vary over cooked piece of pork and the bread served as I finished my main dish and the water never came.
The manger then offered the tables all round me free Port to keep the peace as the service had been a vary poor but forgot to offer any to my table who had had to wait since 8.15 for dinner.
 
Was it worth the waiting I think not and we still have the fun of  breakfast to look forward to

Fantastic Food. The room was a little old fashioned and the bed a bit on the hard side but this was more than compensated by the wonderful meal that we had in the restaurant and the lovely staff throughout the hotel. £99 for dinner, bed and breakfast I would have to say was very good value

Lovely couple of days away. We have just had a lovely short break visiting our favourite town Brighton.  We couldn't get into one of the seafront hotels so took a gamble and booked the Hickstead which is a few miles away from the bright lights of Brighton but I am gad we did because the hotel is very nice and the staff are all really friendly and welcoming.  The breakfasts on both mornings were excellent.  I do agree with a previous reviewer that the bed was a little hard but other than that a very enjoyable stay and would stay at the Hickstead again!

Don't judge a book by its cover. We arrived here after bring very disappointed by somewhere we'd booked in advance which was a real let down. It was getting late so looked online and this place came up. We got to the hotel, sat in the car park, could hear traffic noise, worried about the "it's tired. , beds hard " review. We were tired and hungry and this place is cheaper thAn Central Brighton . Thank goodness we stayed. - very friendly, very clean. Yes a little old fashioned and in some areas the paint work needs touching up. In the room, there's no noise from road or the big wedding party having their celebrations down the corridor! The room is spacious and clean. And well..... The restaurant 3 course meal included in the £109 price (for yes 2 people..... Yes for two -£54.50 each for night, dinner and Brekky) was amazing - the chef did not falter. I don't eat wheat and I could have most of the menu. Siobhan the waitress was lovely - polite and attentive

Comfortable. Not much to report really. Comfortable room. Nice staff. Bar was a little slow but weather was so nice didn't matter too much. Dull view from room. Wasn't expecting herds of Wildebeeste but looking out of window was a bit flat. Anyway staff are nice .. it is most incredibly convenient for a Brighton and Gatwick run and best of all it is not in a town or city ..just down a little country lane.

Happy to stay again. 

No problems

Pleasant stay. This hotel is in a quiet location. There is a main road nearby but you don't hear it in the rooms and not that noticable outside really.
Hotel was very clean and the staff friendly.
Stayed here 2 nights from 14/8/14 booked at last minute.
Food is typical hotel price but very good quality.
Ate in the restaurant on first night, told not a table available for over an hour but when we went in later the restaurant was only half full - so maybe they only cater for so many heads at a time instead of filling out the room.
Breakfast on the Friday morning was plentiful and cooked to order, on the Saturday it was buffet style but food was still excellent.
Would definitely stay in this hotel again if visiting the area

Nothing to rave about. Friendly staff on arrival. Standard basic room with some shabby fittings. View was diabolical. Pictures posted. 
Time spent in the bar was comfortable but my wingback chair was falling apart. 
Had a steak meal which was nice but a bit fatty.
People in the next room had children that screamed from 7.30am but that's not the hotels fault. 
An ok stay but I'm sure there are nicer places

My hopes were high..... I was advised by office colleagues to try this place. Looked good - sounded good, so hey ho. ,

Odd seeing deck chairs out front of Reception. Perhaps that's a local custom. Reception itself is understated and the immediate impression is one of a hotel which looks a little tired. The rooms - well my room - was ok but that's all - nothing to write home about. The usual coffee facilities and trouser press (nice touch).

The bar is barely that - it's squeezed in between the reception and the dining area. You wouldn't want to hang around it for long. The dining experience was fine. The menu is small but perfectly formed and my choice of pan-fried sea bass was really nicely done with some panache. Dining staff both evening and the following morning were exemplary.

All in all - not too bad but could do with a bit of investment

The perfect wedding experience.. My wife and I got married at The Hickstead on the 16th August.  I can't recommend this location highly enough.  In short I would say it's a very reasonable price for exceptional quality.  Dawn, our designated wedding planner, was incredibly helpful, knowledgeable and on top of things.  The staff generally were brilliant, they made the day run like clockwork.  The setting is gorgeous, with some lovely areas for wedding photos.  The food, both at the wedding and breakfast the following day, was superb.  The room was spacious and very comfortable.  I really have nothing but positives about this location.  I have also attended a wedding here previously and was similarly impressed.  A lovely relaxing setting for a getaway too

Good stopover for local business. We stayed here for one night on a short trip to England. Reception staff were efficient and the room was comfortable albeit dated. Breakfast was very good and the restaurant staff were also excellent. It was quiet but there were no functions ongoing. Free WIFI was a bonus and worked well

Great Restaurant, didn't stay the night as live locally.. I had a Virgin Experience for the restaurant. We had 3 courses accompanied by a lovely bottle of house white, which was better than expected. The food could not be faulted. Perfectly cooked lamb rump for main with a delicious (and rather rich) dark chocolate delice for desert. Really impressed with the food here. Food is on par with our previous Virgin Experience at Marco Pierre White's

Cream Tea. We recently took my Mum for a cream tea on bank holiday Monday to this hotel and it was just the best cream tea you could imagine with beautiful homemade sandwiches, scones and cakes
The staff were excellent from the moment we arrived and the lounge was comfortable and well appointed. If you are looking to use a gift voucher or just out for a cream tea this one was far superior to the one we had in a major London Hotel and less than half the cost

Afternoon tea. The 30th August was our 45th Wedding Anniversary and we were treated by our children to afternoon tea at The Hickstead.
It was a wonderful experience and we had a lovely time there.
The sandwiches was fresh and beautifully presented, the scones warm and served with jam and lovely thick cream.  There were 3 cakes each and a piece of shortbread which melted in your mouth.  We were told that all the cakes are made inhouse and they were all superb.  Too much for us to eat and we had to ask for a doggy bag to take some home.
Polite and attentive staff and lovely surroundings.  Don't change a thing you have it just right

Wedding Reception at The Hickstead Hotel. From the very first day that we went in to meet with Dawn to discuss our wedding plans we knew we had found the right place. Not only is it a beautiful venue, but Dawn made everything so easy and our day went off without a single glitch, even the sun shone! They are exceptionally organised and nothing was too difficult. Dawn helped us to plan every detail and Giles ran the day. They were both great and we couldn't have asked for more. It truly was the greatest day of our lives. Perfect. A number of our guests stayed at the hotel and they all loved it. The food was incredible and everything was reasonably priced

Family lunch. I attended the hickstead hotel for my parents 60th wedding celebration. The hotel was beautifully situated in wonderful countryside we were made to
Feel very welcome by all the staff. The food was very good and portions generous whilst the service  was very attentive. I would recommend this venue for any function

Mealtime Lottery. Stayed here for business. The room is big, bed comfortable and does what a hotel bedroom should do.
I had dinner last night, and chose Chicken Caesar salad. The waitress asked if I would like the anchovies, and I thankfully said no. I hate anchovies.
The Caesar salad was brought out with anchovies. Clearly chef believes that a Caesar salad should have anchovies. Anyway, I picked them out of the salad, and finished my meal.
Next morning, came down to breakfast.
Ordered my cooked breakfast, eggs, bacon, sausage, mushrooms, beans and black pudding. Only when it was brought out to me, chef had obviously decided that a cooked breakfast wasn't complete without fried bread, and I couldn't have black pudding.
I asked to swap the crispy fried bread for a piece of black pudding and was served a slice of barely warm, and suspiciously undercooked black pudding. If only it had been of the same crispiness as the fried bread..
So mealtimes at the Hickstead are a bit of a lottery

Friday Overnight break. We arrived at The Hickstead and were greeted by a smiley friendly and helpful receptionist. The room was big enough and the bed comfy, the bathroom was spotlessly clean and the shower was quite powerful which was great and just the way we like it. Our room was downstairs near the reception which was fine although if we had been staying longer than one night we may have asked to be moved as there was very little natural light. We went for dinner and were greeted by a Polish waiter who looked after us very well, he was very polite, friendly and the service was excellent. The food was presented beautifully and was very tasty, compliments to the chef (we had the lamb and it was so tender). Breakfast next morning was also very tasty and grease free although could have been a little hotter, there was a great choice both hot and cold. All in all an excellent stay and we would definitely go back. Couple from London visiting Brighton

Perfect Location For A Stopover. This hotel is everything a business hotel should be, but catering for leisure as well.  We booked the Garden Break with tickets to the Borde Hill gardens which due to time restraints we were not able to use, so the friendly receptionist immediately changed this to a complimentary drink from the bar which was most welcome.  The hotel is located a few minutes off the A23 but is sufficiently far away not to be bothered by traffic noise and made a perfect stop off point travelling between the west country and Kent, enabling us to arrive in Canterbury at lunchtime.  Our room was very large but next to reception and was very dark requiring the lights to be on all the time when in the room.  (Staying longer than one night I would have asked to be moved).  Eating in the restaurant was most pleasant, but be warned the cooked breakfast next morning was absolutely huge.  Forget any thoughts of lunch that day

Lovely stay with friendly staff. Had a lovely stay at the Hickstead hotel for a couple of nights and would definitely go back! It was a nice atmosphere and offered good choice of food on the menu. The rooms were clean and looked good

Good value stay in posh area. Just one night here in the heart of Sussex. Plenty parking in countryside location, good sized room, good shower, good selection of teas coffees in room.  Lighting too dim, TV small and bed hard.  But overall a good room.  The breakfast service and quality was excellent,  attentive without being intrusive.  A very competitive price.  We will definitely consider this hotel next time we are in the area

Unimpressed . When I arrived,  there was no recored of my booking as it had ben cancelled by the Central reservations ten minutes after me making it, for some reason. No matter, they had a room spare so I took that.  The room was separated from the next with a door, and the people in the twin room next to me were talking loudly and animatedly and watching TV, perhaps with other people present (I know it was a twin as the receptionist insisted there were only two people in there 'because it was a twin').

Again no matter, I moved to another room which was was separated by an actual wall from the neighbouring rooms. 

I could smell smoke in the bathrooms of both rooms, not just a trace, as if someone was actually in there smoking.  At first I thought it was people in the next room, but the receptionist assured me it came from outside. I realised it must come in through some kind of ventilation system right into the bathrooms from people standing outside,  which to my mind is more of a problem than one rogue guest smoking in their room.

Dinner was overpriced but not awful, I thought for a £13 salad there would be some substance and/or style, but they were stingy with items that were not lettuce and it was rather generic and insubstantial. 

I filled in the breakfast card to order room service and one of the items I ticked was 'fresh fruit platter' - this turned out to be a bowl of tinned mandarin and grapefruit segments, and prunes. I don't think this can claim to be fresh fruit platter by even the most generous definition. 

The worst thing, and probably what drives me to leave the review in the first place, and mention all the other things, is the fact that even rooms separated from their neighbours with real floors and walls are poorly soundproofed. I was kept awake by one of my neighbours snoring, I can hear a phone vibrating in the next room, I can hear every footstep on the room above me.

I don't understand why hotels claiming to hold themselves to a high standard, such as this one, can put such little emphasis on quality of the primary service they are supposed to provide - somewhere to sleep. Subjecting the customer to all the intimate nocturnal snorting and groaning of their fellow guests, and at the same time depriving them of their own privacy by separating then with such thin walls, makes the experience no better than staying in a cheap hostel. For all its airs and graces, I think this hotel has its priorities wrong.

The reception staff are pleasant and polite, which is important,  so I give it two stars

Wonderful hotel. Fantastic experience! The hotel accommodated me as a vegan by making a  delicious meal and supplying soya milk. Lovely friendly staff. Recommend it for a lovely getaway. I'm planning on going back as the whole experience was so good. Well rested

Staff very helpful. Visited for a family celebration and staff were very kind when my uncle was taken I'll after the meal.   The room itself was OK but nothing special and the corridor outside had a strange smell like stale cigarette smoke. The room was a good size and the bed was comfortable. Background noise from road in the distance.
Breakfast self serve and average. Very kind staff

Fair to Middling. We booked a double room here as we were visiting relatives and then going into London for the day the following day, so it was handy and a good price.  There was a private function going on when we arrived, which rang a few alarm bells but we'd booked so we took the chance.  We couldn't get to the bar as although the private party had their own function room, they had taken possession of the bar area too, and although you were paying guests, they made you feel as if you were intruding.  The hotel staff did nothing to try and accommodate us even though they could see we were a put put out.  If we had wanted to eat in the dining room, we could have been accommodated but we didn't want to, we just wanted a drink, so we went without.  The receptionist who booked us in was quite ungracious to start with as we had to summon her via phone from elsewhere, but she managed to crack a tepid smile and asked a few scripted questions.  The room when we got up to it was fine.  It was a good size with a table and two hard arm chairs, but the lighting was so gloomy it was untrue.  There were tea and coffee making facilities but no bottled water.  We could have ordered some drinks from room service but objected to paying £2.50 extra just because we couldn't actually get to the bar!  The room looked clean, but you couldn't tell as it was so dark!  The window didn't open fully as it had a safety device fitted and we couldn't be bothered to try and summon help from reception from little miss helpful.  The bathroom was tired.  Grout in floor tiles missing, the chrome fittings all rusty and the shower curtain skimmed the edge of the bath.  The shower was efficient but you constantly had a large rusty chrome hand rail poking in your side.  The water also ran a bit hot and cold when the room next door turned their shower on.  The bed was clean but very hard, but extra pillows were stashed in storage bags in the wardrobe.  Towels were the usual grey, skinny, hard offerings and only one large one and a hand towel.  Good job we didn't need the complimentary toiletries as they weren't of an exceptionally high standard and had been there a while judging by the covering of dust.  The walls are paper thin and you could hear every word said in the adjacent rooms even though other guests weren't talking particularly loudly.  About 9.30 we were disturbed by the children belonging to the private party using the corridors as a race track.  There was a group of them thundering up and down the corridors screaming and shouting and banging the fire escape stair doors.  No one was supervising them or seemed to care.  We rang reception, no one answered, so my husband went out and asked them to be quiet, and they scarpered.  We could hear the music from the private function and to be fair the guests were not awfully considerate when returning to their rooms in the early hours, but the hotel can't be held responsible for that.   We went down to breakfast and the ladies in the the dining room were very helpful and pleasant.  We were shown to a table for two and helped ourselves to the plentiful buffet.  One minor query though, cooked breakfast without fried eggs, not even an offer to cook them fresh, just scrambled.  The rest was very nice though.  Then the private function group arrived from the night before and they proceeded to seat them all around us!  Once again we were made to feel we were intruding  so made a hasty exit.  It was fine for one night, but I certainly wouldn't return or recommend it to anyone who wanted anything more than just somewhere to lay their heads for the night.  And then only if they had ear plugs

Excellent hotel. We stayed here for one night and were very impressed.The room was quite large and had everything we needed, shoe polish, hairdryer and along the corridor an ironing board and iron.
We didn't need dinner but had an amazing buffet breakfast which included a full English breakfast, toast, croissants, cereals, lots of fruit juices and nice fresh coffee.  The staff were very pleasant and helpful and we would stay there again if we were in the vicinity

Afternoon Tea at The Hickstead. We had a Taster for Two gift and were having difficulty using it, when Hickstead came to the rescue offering Gluten Free afternoon tea.
We were really impressed with the originality, quantity and quality!  The smoked salmon, ham, egg sandwiches were generously filled and went well with the GF bread.  There was a good selection of GF cakes - the bitter chocolate worked perfectly in the muffin for example - covering all trace of that funny GF after-taste!  We took our time, with so much to eat, and enjoyed the plentiful supplies of tea and un-rushed atmosphere.  Have already recommended to others

We loved the warm welcome!. The overwhelming comment for this hotel is a very big thank you to the staff for their overwhelming welcome and their friendliness.  It certainly makes this hotel special and I hope the management take time to praise their staff for this essential ingredient.  The bedroom was clean and spacious and the bed was extremely comfortable (and big).  The evening meal was fantastic and the quality extremely high so complements to the chefs... We had seabass, mackerel pate, goats cheese and the fish and chips... Brillilliant!  Breakfast was standard but very nicely served and cooked.  Often, it is the little things which make a difference and The Hickstead understands these and includes them.  I would very much recommend a stay at this delightful establishment.  Thank you Hickstead

Very good food and service. Rooms well appointed - lovely to have tea/coffee tray that actually had a teapot. Food very good. Autumn deal very good value. Building itself rather functional - dining room would be improved with tablecloths

Memorable Tea.. We were given a present of a voucher for Afternoon Tea at this hotel so duly phoned and booked a date.
Never having been to this hotel before we were pleasantly surprised to discover it in fine open countryside in spite of being so near to Gatwick airport. The main building is a lovely old country house that has been carefully brought up to date with good taste and quality fittings.
The Tea was served in the dining room that has excellent views of the gardens surrounding the hotel.
The Tea itself was more like a meal with an excellent selection of sandwiches, beautiful warm homemade scones and a mouth watering selection of delicious cakes; the size of the tea was too much for our modest appetites so we asked the waiter if we could take the surplus home and they willingly wrapped the items and put them in a bag for us.
The service was very efficient and the staff were all friendly and helpful and made sure we had an enjoyable experience at The Hickstead.
We shall certainly return and we also know what to give special people as a present - a Tea Voucher !

Could do better. We stayed at the Hickstead on a Saturday night. Plenty of room in the car park. I was not impressed by reception, the welcome was not warm, the girl was flustered by the arrival of guests to a wedding. Our room was not ready until late in the afternoon. The room was tired, the bed and the pillows were very hard. There was plenty of hot water in the bath taps but it was not reaching the shower which we could not get to heat up. The cleanliness of our bathroom (room 105) was not up to my standards!  On the other hand, there were good tea & coffee-making facilities. 

The main problem was noise. There was a wedding and reception at the hotel on the day we stayed, with many children. A group of these were running up and down the corridors on the ground floor and the floor above, banging doors and screaming until late evening. It was difficult to sleep with this noise. I did complain to the receptionist, but action taken, if there was any, was not effective. Because of the number of people moving around there was a lot of rubbish and debris along the corridors that gave a messy appearance to the hotel lobby, bad and corridors.

Breakfast was good although the only eggs on offer were scrambled. Diet or sugar-free lemonade/Sprite or 7up was not available in the bar. The orders for the main course and dessert served at the wedding reception was all mixed up with many people complaining that they got the wrong food.  I feel the hotel could have done better all round

Afternoon tea. This was a gift for our wedding anniversary, the staff and the food were very nice with  small selection of sandwiches and cakes ,the tea was hot. We were the first to arrive and there wasn't much of an atmosphere and the room was a little chilly. We had finished in 45mins ,much the same as others who had arrived

Our wedding. We held our wedding at the hickstead hotel and it was beautiful,  the staff are fantastic. I can't fault the staff or hotel as they have all been very helpful in making our wedding day as special as it was. Unfortunately I did hear that orders got mixed up at the wedding dinner, and the beds were hard. Apart from that a lovely place to stay. Would definitely be visiting again :-)

Emma & Lee's Wedding. My son had his wedding here 5/12/14  All of the staff were extremely friendly & helpful. The wedding breakfast was tasty, hot & large 😄  every one commented about how well the hotel coped & how the wedding was a huge success. Thank you to all the staff from the cleaners through to the receptionist & Dawn the wedding co ordinator & of course the chef. The breakfasts were great too

Needs lots of TLC. Stayed Friday and Saturday night here for my sons wedding. I booked this some 16 months ago.I had planned to arrive mid afternoon and eat in the restaurant on the Friday evening. Just a few days before my arrival I was informed that I could no longer use the restaurant as they had a wedding and a Christmas party that evening.I did ask whether we could have room service and was advised that they may be able to do sandwiches but we would have to order as we booked in but it could take hours! I opted to go to a local restaurant first, which delayed our arrival.On arrival we booked in, was given a key but no offer of any help to the room ( I am a disabled person). The room, 105 was reasonable in size but really smelt so first thing was to open the window. When it came to closing it at midnight it became problematic. The window would not close. Eventually the night porter managed to close it, but we were unable to open it for the rest of the stay.The en-suite bathroom is very tired, (as is all the hotel). The toilet seat was so loose, just hanging on. Someone else's hair on the toilet and in the bath!! The tiles around the toilet were loose & broken and the grout non existent! The bedding was clean but bed hard and the noise from the corridor went on most the night. Looking forward to a shower turned out to be a disappointment. There are no disabled rooms in the hotel and all the baths have a shower over, which is great if you are able bodied. Having been lifted into the bath I then found that the shower had only lukewarm water that quickly turned to cold! We reported this to the reception and was told they would sort it for us. Breakfast was a good choice, a buffet help yourself and the staff attentive but limited for vegetarians. The wedding breakfast was lovely and the room was made to look amazing.So up Sunday morning looking forward to a hot shower, once again having to be lifted into the bath to find we had just freezing cold water!! NOT impressed. I spoke with the manager Giles, who informed that the thermostat had gone and was down to the age. Couldn't get it fixed till Monday. Yet no one had bothered to let us know or offer us another room to shower in.I have seen on TA that the week before someone else had stayed in the same room,105 and had experienced the same problem with the shower. Whilst they reflected the problems in my bill, only paid for one night, I still feel cheated and feel I wasted my money. A final word....about time this hotel put in at least one room with a shower in it to be disable friendly. Would I stay here again....NO

My Niece's wedding. Attended a Family wedding and stayed 2 nights. As I have travelled a lot and stayed at 5 star hotels was not expecting to much. HOW WRONG WAS I. The staff were wonderful everything for the wedding went like clockwork and the food was amazing. Every member of staff greeted you with a smile and nothing was to much trouble. I would recommend any one to arrange a function here.  I honestly can not find anything that was not good about this stay. And I will be back for sure

Emma and Lee's wedding. My daughter and her husband had their wedding on the 5th of December, what an exceptional wedding it was. The staff were friendly helpful and made the day perfect

The venue was brilliant, and incredibly pretty, made only better by how smooth and hassle free the whole day was. I really couldn't of hoped for a better result.

The food was wonderful and the chef even came out to a big cheer from all the guests.

Dawn the co-ordinator was fantastic as was everyone that was a part of it.

Thank you so much for making my daughters day so special, would recommend this to anyone thinking about having their wedding here.

Thank you!

Kathy Berry

A simply magical day

Sack the DJ. Went here for a Christmas party at work last week and found parts of the stay very disappointing! The hotel looked tired and in need of repair, the bar area was small and not very festive.

The rooms were very cold and could not get the heating to work!

The food for the Christmas party was excellent, and service was good!
 
The room where the event was being held was cold and had the air conditioning on in December!

The DJ was out of date, rude, and he made inappropriate comments that were racist, and sexist, sack the DJ, put the heating on and some Christmas cheer and things would be much improved

Christmas Meal. I took my four employees, helpers and partners for a Christmas Dinner.  This is the second year we have done this and I found it as usual excellent.  This time they put out all sorts of hats and goodies and made us have a wonderful fun time.  The food was absolutely excellent, lots of it and not at all expensive.  the staff were extremely attentive and we tipped them well for their efforts

Excellent Food.. We stayed here in December 2014, the second time this year. On both occasions we found the food to be excellent and well above the rating of the hotel and plenty of it. The staff were always on hand if they were needed. The rooms were clean and tidy a bit cold when we arrived. The staff immediately put the heating on. Worth a visit for the food alone

60th birthday party. Only stayed for one night but room was very spacious and surprisingly quiet considering a DJ was playing down below. Queen sized bed was very comfortable. Breakfast was pretty standard although the sausages looked undercooked and I sent mine back to be cooked a bit more.
All in all was good value for the money and would not hesitate to stay again

Let there be light. Please.. Excellent, helpful staff, great food, comfortable beds. When we arrived (December 21) the heating was off in both our adjoining rooms, and there was no travel cot, but the heating was turned on immediately we pointed it out, and a travel cot found (actually, two, as the first could not be erected properly). Our only complaint is that the rooms (at least both of ours) are too dark. There is no overhead illumination, only wall uplighters. This meant the only way we could find stuff in our suitcase was by using our mobile phones as torches!

New year celebration. Six of us attended the 2015 new year celebrations and all ejoyed it very much.
The evening meal, disco, entertaining, DJ  waiting / bar staff and breakfast the following morning were all excelent. It was also great value. 
The only reason it does not get excellent was because the room, although comfortable  was  in need of a bit tlc. 
Would certainly consider going again next new year

Can't cook poached eggs!. I stay here regularly and the chefs seem to be completely incapable of cooking poached eggs. Even when I specifically ask for them to be soft, they come out with the yolks almost completely solid. One of the easiest things in the world to cook, but they can't seem to get it right. I want runny yolks!

Burns Night Dinner: An excellent evening. This is an annual event for my association with about 50 members attending our private Burns Night Dinner.  My wife and I have attended for the past six years.  Once again, it was a very pleasant and relaxed occasion.  On arrival the staff were very welcoming.  The dining room was extremely comfortable and well set out.  As always, the serving staff were very efficient, polite and keen to please.  The five course meal was excellent - the main course of roast beef was particularly enjoyable for its quality and quantity.  A great evening.  Many thanks to management and staff

Fourth visit with Mr Wonderful. We were looking to get away for the night and chose the Hickstead as we have been several times before. On arrival we were welcomed by the head receptionist who recognised us. We booked dinner for 7.45 as we had been advised there was a large party booked in for 8. The food and service were excellent as always but we noticed a few new faces and had been informed Jenny had left. Still a very warm welcome by the newbys and we had a very enjoyable evening. Next morning no buffet breakfast but cooked to order which was a vast improvement and delicious. Compliments to the chef and to all the team who made us feel so welcome. We will be back in the spring for sure

4th stay. We decided to stay for our 4th visit to The Hickstead, as it's a short drive from Brighton, which we love, but far enough away as it's so peaceful!  Special offer package, which was dinner, bed and breakfast and a bottle of prosecco!.  We cannot fault the food in The Oak Tree restaurant, and the lamb was absolutely gorgeous!  This is a really comfortable 3 star we came upon by chance as we wanted to stay near to Brighton at short notice 4 years ago.  Staff excellent as always, although we were a little disappointed to hear that Jenny, the restaurant manager had left, as she was brilliant, however it was another great stay.  Spacious room, spotlessly clean as always!  No complaints!  See you again soon.  Well done to all at the Hickstead as usual

Bad Smell of Sewerage in Reception!. I have stayed here many times, on several occasions there has been a smell of drains/sewerage in the reception area. This time the smell hit you as you walked in and could be smelt in communal areas. One staff member shrugged their shoulders, the other said they would "spray the air". Some serious drainage redial work required. I will be looking to stay elsewhere until this is sorted

Excellent as always. We try to go to The Hickstead at least twice a year and have been doing so for a few years now. Once again we were there this wekend with our usual partners in crime (good friends) and thoroughly enjoyed our stay.  Food as usual was excellent and all the staff go beyond the call of duty to assist you.  Rooms are spotlessly clean and the only small comment we had was that the rooms could do with a little extra lighting as quite dim but hey certainly not worth complaining about!
Can't wait for our next visit!

Kind regards

Christine Glover

Nice day for a white wedding. Stayed here for a wedding. Not the kind of place I'd fly solo and I tend to prefer places that have  a lot of character to them. The rooms weren't feats of grand design but they were comfortable, spacious and good value for money. Nothing special, but nothing wrong with them either. The staff however were a delight, they made the wedding and the stay absolutely fantastic, the food was good and the service was flexible and unfailing kind always going the extra mile. Definitely recommend them for functions parties and weddings

Well run hotel with good customer service. We stayed for 1 night in order to visit Brighton which was a 20 minute drive away. The girl on reception was very welcoming and check in quick and easy. Our superior room was warm and well decorated . We had a bottle of Prosecco chilling in bucket and bottles of water and fruit bowl on the table . We had a very good meal in the restaurant with excellent bottle of Rioja . Service was efficient and friendly . Breakfast again was very good with a good choice of cereals fruit youghurts and fresh croissants plus a full English breakfast. An enjoyable stay . Would recommend

Weekend Break. Having stayed many times at The Hickstead i'm afraid to say that the Wedding that was taking place this weekend unfortunately seemed to take over the Hotel.
Wedding guests were everywhere!!!! We had a lovely meal as usual and hoping for a drink in the bar afterwards, but  had to retreat to our room (4 guests) at 9.15pm as there was literally nowhere to sit. Sorry to say, spoilt our stay and we felt that non Wedding guests were not too important. Have stayed before when a Wedding has been taking place with no problem.
Saying that The Hickstead is still a super little Hotel with great offers

Pleasant but average. Friends room had defective heating ,it took an hour to get another room even though he hotel was not full. Dinner was just average. Staff very pleasant and attempted to be helpful.
 This was the 5th Classic lodge we have stayed at and ranks 4/5!

Tired and in Middle of nowhere. Start with the positives, staff are friendly, courteous and give good service; rooms are fairly spacious and clean, nice breakfast. Negatives, whilst clean and kept as nice as can be expected, it is old a bit tired with no spa facilities (some arrangement to use facility down road at cost). It seems to be off away from Haywards Heath in middle of nowhere

Hotel and wedding venue. Attended a wedding here, what a great service they do. Very polished and professional.
I could not fault the food, the tomato soup was the best I have had, the breakfast had everything we wanted. 
The check in and out process was easy. 
The staff were helpful and pleasant.
My only niggle was the noise at the back of the hotel when the DJ was leaving, but that only lasted around 15mins.
Beds have a very hard mattress

Nice atmosphere, but in need of refurbishment. I stayed one night, having read other reviews n this hotel prior to my stay. I agree with others, that the hotel badly needs upgrading. The whole place is about 10 years out of date and has not been well maintained. I observed rotten window frames, poor decorations, broken tiles in bathroom, etc. it is a shame because the hotel is in a good location. I found the staff to be very friendly and efficient and it looked like they had a number of regular guests staying who were looked after well by the staff. I had a meal in the bar and was disapponted with the food - nothing special. Breakfast was ok. Compared with other hotels I have stayed at in the area, this does not provide the best value for money. I would only stay there again if my usual hotels were not available, but would consider it again if refurbished/upgraded

Great alternative to expensive Brighton hotels!. Nice, comfortable hotel. Extremely polite and helpful staff. Although there was a small hiccup with our breakfast (the orders got mixed up, and we ended waiting for ages), the waitress was very apologetic, and we ended up getting our food at 50% of the price. Plus  food was delicious (both cooked breakfast and an evening meal). Free and very quick wifi. And all this for a very reasonable price. Great base for exploring Brighton and the South Downs without breaking a bank

Beautiful surroundings;comfortable room. Stayed on the Saturday night before the Brighton marathon(my husband was running).Pleased with room and hotel itself.Easy journey into Brighton. Very helpful staff before stay when asking questions regarding breakfast availability due to checking out early.
Would love to stay again

Welcoming receptionist and good breakfast. The receptionist was always pleasant and even managed to get me some green tea for our room. The beds were comfy, perhaps the room was a little too warm at times. Facilities were ok but nicely clean. Breakfast was nicely done. Little things like qood quality cooking of the scrambled eggs, tasty mushrooms and grilled tomatoes. The croissants were delicious and crisp. I enjoyed a good size pot of green tea, from which there was a selection of tea-bags. The gentleman serving tried his best to check that everything was good. All in all, very welcoming

Friendly with great service as always. We had the pleasure of an extended visit of over a week while work was done on our house. As usual we were made to feel very welcome and greeted like old friends. As we had an extended stay we had an opportunity to sample the food at the restaurant. We knew it was good but it seems to have got even better! The quality is superb and we relished every meal we had here. A big Thank You to everyone - Reception, Night Porters, Cleaners, Chefs, Restaurant staff and of course Hassan! Thank you Giles for a brilliant Hotel which we can always rely upon!

Fantastic afternoon tea !!!. Must say how never had such a scrumptious tea ! This quaint ,cosy welcoming hotel is a gem !! A 3 tier cake stand full of homemade cakes all my favourites and so fresh !! Tasty sandwiches with a large variety of fillings with crusts cut off to perfection ! Then scones all homemade with endless cream !! What made it so special was the warm welcoming and friendly staff  especially our waitress (ellie) couldn't do enough for us ! They don't rush you and even have board games available to play while enjoying the lovely country views !! Definitely recommend this hotel !! So enjoyable booked for Mother's Day !!!

Lovely location and very welcoming. We had a lovely relaxing stay last weekend, the staff without exception were friendly and welcoming. Our room was lovely, fresh and clean, the bed linen was spotless and very comfortable. The breakfasts are very good and again the service is excellent. We had some very helpful advice on planning our trip to Brighton on the Saturday and even maps were provided. Good location, we visited Brighton and Hever Castle

Afternoon tea booking. I tried to book afternoon tea but was told "we're full as we don't have enough staff".  Well here's an idea - get some more so you can actually provide a service!  This was on a Sunday morning for that afternoon, so not exactly last minute

The Hickstead Hotel. I arrived just before 9 pm. this hotel is in the middle of nowhere. The restaurant was closed and it was only touch n go whether I would be fed. Luckily Chef agreed and his food is excellent as was Head Chefs . Not good though if you are a late arrival.
I found the hotel to have deteriorated visually since my last visit. The bedroom had a strange smell/ window had to be manhandled to close/ bath tap dripped/ room needed renovation/ no heating and thin bedding. Price per night too expensive for what is on offer.
No lift and poor ladies had to manhandle my luggage upstairs- most willingly I may add.
However some redeeming points-
The staff are lovely. The food is superb.  The outside gardens/ views are lovely too. 
chef/ pleasant staff/ Giles is a nice manager
Classic Hotels you need to spend some money here for clients to continue staying

Afternoon tea. My wife and I spent a voucher on an afternoon tea (coffee!!) in The Hickstead today, to celebrate an anniversary.  Marvellous coffee and food, good service, - more than made up for the weather.
It didn't help our waistlines though - best saved for a special treat!
 ROBERT DEWICK

Long service award trip. We chose this hotel because of its proximity to the South Downs and Brighton. The hotel is in a quiet location near the A23.  The overall decor is tired and dated although all the staff were very friendly and helpful

Great Staff, Building starting to feel tired. I have just returned from a wedding held at The Hickstead. Frankly they were great, attentive chatty and polite and made sure the day went well for the guests and happy couple. The food both the three course meal and breakfast were of a good standard.

The only drawback for me is that the accommodation is starting to show its age and is probably at the stage it needs an overhaul

get married here. On the 30th of may 2015 we got married at this hotel. I must say the staff are amazing, they are very polite and helpful. The food is absolutely amazing. You will be made to feel like a friend, more than just a customer. I think this hotel went well beyond what we was expecting. Thank you so much for the best day of my life

Value Added Experience. We had the opportunity to spend 1 night at the Hickstead on the 19th June 2015. We were pleasantly greeted from the moment we stepped into the reception. The room was clean and quiet. The Hickstead is located in the quiet countryside and the grounds are well maintained. We had a three course dinner and found the food to be outstanding and appropriately presented. The service was prompt and attentive. I will definitely stay in this hotel should I be in the area again due to the first class service received and the close proximity to nearby attractions

Faithful family friend!. This hotel is like an old family friend as we have stayed here many times over the past 10 years or so when visiting our relatives.
 It's always clean & comfortable with every type of breakfast provided in a pleasant dining room overlooking the surrounding fields.
The bedrooms are quiet with super soft white sheets & extra pillows & towels are available in the wardrobe- which I often forget about so a note on the wardrobe door would be handy!
No mini bar or fridge or air con so it does get warm in summer & windows upstairs only open 6".
Close to Hickstead Showground

Very Friendly. We stayed at the Hickstead to celebrate the marriage of Luke and Katherine. We found all the staff very helpful and friendly. This is a small hotel not far from the A23 but you will not hear any road noise as it is surrounded by fields in a lovely setting. We were very lucky with the weather, so were able to spend  a lot of time in the hotel grounds
which are very pleasant. The room we stayed in was very nice, the food was good and we ready enjoyed the breakfast. On the whole we had a great time at this Hotel

Going to a wedding. We recently stayed here after attending a friends wedding that was also held at the hotel.  The staff were incredible.  Nothing was too much for them and they bent over backwards to help us all out.  The room was decorated to a really good standard for the wedding and looked great.  The bedrooms were a really good size, nicely set out and really well equipped.  The food we had at all times was of a surprisingly good standard.  To top it all off the hotel is in the very pleasant surroundings of the Sussex countryside.  Overall I can not rate the experience we had there highly enough

Champagne afternoon tea. Lovely afternoon tea, lots of cake and sandwiches however, did not get our champagne! Guess I should have asked at time but was enjoying the fun experience I forgot. Shame as this was a gift. Staff were very nice

Afternoon Tea. Lovely friendly staff from moment of arrival. Delicious sandwiches with fresh, soft bread,  fruit scone with plenty of cream and jam. Also shortbread  biscuit,  carrot cake, strawberry meringue and chocolate eclair.  All full sized so watch your waistlines. More than we could eat but staff were more than happy to provide a doggy bag. Coffee offered as an option for those non tea drinkers

Wedding reception . The Hickstead Hotel was wonderful the staff and service were fantastic the rooms lovely clean and comfortable the food was lovely we loved our stay here and will definitely go back and recommend to everyone. Please check it out wonderfully out of the way but close to Brighton in a car

Disappointing . Stayed here for one night for work, I opted for this hotel thinking it would be a step up from the travelodge. I was wrong, overall the hotel is very tired, chairs in the restaurant were grubby, the water in the sink took ages to drain, the mattress was old and the scrambled egg I ordered was shocking. I fed this back to the hotel. I would not recommend

Great weekend. Just returned from a lovely two night stay at The Hickstead. The room  was clean, comfortable and spacious. The food at breakfast and dinner was plentiful, hot and beautifully cooked and presented. The staff were very friendly and all gave excellent service, nothing was too much trouble. A special mention goes to Ellie, who has such a bubbly personality, and gave that bit extra to ensure our stay was enjoyable. And thank you to Patricia, the welcoming face on reception, who gave us some great tips on local walks and places to visit

Slightly disappointing. We stayed here for 2 nights as part of a long weekend break. The room itself was nice and had everything we expected, but the bed was very hard, and the bathroom definitely needed some attention – some tiles at the end of the bath were loose, the rail and tray over the bath were rusting, the grouting was going black, and a couple of floor tiles were also loose. The receptionists were welcoming, as was the young lady in the bar and restaurant. We had a very nice meal in the restaurant on Sunday evening, and the breakfasts were also very good with a reasonable, although not extensive, choice. The breakfast staff on Monday morning were great, but the Sunday morning staff left a lot to be desired – there was a lot of wandering about and no clearing of plates until tables had been vacated – I had to physically call one over and hand up my dirty plate in order to get it off the table so that I could enjoy my last coffee without looking at leftovers.  The lounge area had some comfortable chairs but it wasn’t a welcoming area – and it was all very . . . brown. I think the problem is the steep rate that was charged – it raises your expectations of the quality of accommodation that you’re going to get, and my expectations just weren’t met. We spent the following night in a hotel on the outskirts of Ipswich for half the price, in a former manor house that was well looked after, well run, beautifully furnished and had a sense of luxury about it, and also had a fitness centre and pool. 
On a plus, this hotel is well placed for touring the South Downs and visiting other attractions, and the Royal Spice restaurant in nearby Burgess Hill does a most excellent curry

Average but nothing special. We were given an afternoon tea voucher for this hotel so as it was our anniversary too decided to book for dinner, bed and breakfast as well. 
We arrived and were shown to our room by a very helpful lady from reception who then spent time helping us decide what to do in the area. 
Later I went to reception again to ask a question and another lady was there then who really gave the impression she couldn't be bothered.
Went into dinner in the evening and was quite frankly unimpressed with the selection on the menu. When the meal came is was nicely cooked and presented (must have decent chefs) but we felt the quality of the ingredients could be better.
The mattress on the bed felt hard and a bit lumpy and even with the window open the room felt hot. There was only a heavy quilt on the bed so sweltered all night which kept us awake, so not a good night! 
Breakfast was good and plenty of choice. We went out for the morning and arrived back for our afternoon tea which was very disappointing for the money. There were a few finger sandwiches, one scone with cream and jam and three other cream cakes. It would of been nice to have some plain cakes on offer instead of so many rich creamy ones. We have had several afternoon teas in various places and this was by far the worst. 
One other comment is the fact the car park has only one disabled space, which was occupied. The car park was very full so I had to park quite a distance away from the main entrance which made it difficult for my husband. When I asked about this at reception I was told that they had only ever had one space, as if that made it ok!
Overall we were not very happy with our stay and certainly would not recommend it to others

One night recently. Stayed one night while attending an equestrian competition at the All England Show Jumping Ground which is within a stone's throw of this hotel.  Very close to the A23 to Brighton but it is tucked away down a lane which makes the site quiet and secluded.  The rooms are well appointed, clean and comfortable and the staff helpful and friendly.  The restaurant is large and light and the evening meal and breakfast offered a good choice of well prepared food.  An enjoyable relaxing stay in the middle of two busy working days of travel and competition

Reasonable afternoon tea. We were given a voucher for afternoon tea; most of the venues were miles away so we plumped for the nearest which was The Hickstead. On arrival the staff were very welcoming and showed us straight to our table. Tea was set up in the hotel restaurant; it was quite a large, bright function room filled with tables set up for tea/dinner. There were some other people having tea but there was something lacking; not much atmosphere. 
The food was good, a selection of sandwiches, scones, cake and pastries plus as much tea as you could drink. It was OK just missing the X factor that makes a good tea great

Weekend break. This charming little hotel is conveniently placed just off the A23 which was perfect for us as we were visiting family on the coast. Although still quite some way from the coast the price for our stay was fair and availability wasn't an issue with last minute booking.

We ate in the restaurant once, and were both pleasantly surprised and delighted with both the service a food. Bar prices were also very good for a hotel. If we ever need to stay at a hotel just off the Gatwick estate or from Brighton will we most likely return

Lovely hotel for Gatwick & Brigton. We booked this hotel for an overnight stay whilst dropping our daughter off for university in Brighton. We hadn't stayed here before but wow what a good find. It's really conveniently located just off the A23 between Gatwick Airport and Brighton yet was so peaceful and quiet. We booked two rooms but I had accidentally booked two doubles win I needed a double and a twin. No problem, rooms changed by staff who even showed us to our rooms. Perfectly located adjacent rooms. Rooms were a good size and tidy with all the comforts needed. We ate in the restaurant that evening with a good choice on the menu. We'd also booked the b & b option and enjoyed a very generous breakfast the following morning. All of the staff were very friendly and noticing we had a child with special needs went out of their way to make our stay really comfortable. We'll definitely be booking again for overnight stays when we travel to Brighton or before flying from Gatwick

Excellent value in comfortable hotel near to Brighton. 2 night all-in stay with my wife. Excellent value as 3 course dinner inclusive option was very little more in price than B&B option. 

Clean and comfortable hotel. Welcoming and helpful staff.  

Excellent evening meal and huge breakfast. Comfortable and clean room and quite spacious. Quite countryside setting. Free wifi including in-room was ok but not very strong away from the communal areas. 

Good base for touring the South Downs and / or the south coast area around Brighton. Very easy access just off the A23 some 12 miles from the coast. Quite convenient for Gatwick also

Fantastic, friendly, Hotel. Stayed here for a friends wedding, the rooms were nice and clean. The staff were lovely and very friendly, special thanks to Ellie, who was just brilliant! - The food was delicous, we had dinner on our first evening, service was fantastic and compliments to the chef, as food was first class. Lovely gardens

Excellent friendly hotel. We very much enjoyed our short stay at the Hickstead Hotel.  Our room was lovely and the bed was very comfortable.  The staff were friendly and helpful throughout, and breakfast was magnificent!  We only wished we could have stayed longer

Excellent hotel, excellent service.. Stayed for 2 nights for my mums wedding, great food, Giles organised  everything to perfection & a big THANKS to Ellie for being so welcoming, for providing a EXCELLENT bar service & my 2 boys absolutely adored her, Ellie made the weekend. Definitely coming back. Thank you.👍

Beautiful Setting and excellent service. We stayed for 2 nights as an anniversary weekend away.Lovely friendly and helpful staff, great location easy to get to nearby towns and villages. The resturant food was delicious and the breakfast was more than enough to keep you going all day. The rooms were clean and comfortable. We would stay there again

Fantastic hotel. Stayed here on business for four days.  Room was excellent and all staff very friendly and efficient.  Plenty of parking as well.  Restaurant served a very good breakfast and dinner menu although reasonably limited was very nice as well.  Recommended

A Great Stopover. Everything was as promised from my initial booking for a Friday night offer.

Nothing was too much trouble and the dining was excellent on the offer.  Rooms were good and very clean.  We shall definitely return to this chain of Hotels,

Afternoon Tea for 2. We were really looking forward to it. Have to be honest, was a little disappointing. Firstly, we were sat in front of patio doors looking out on a car park. These doors were covered in the biggest spider webs I've seen and huge spiders, which was difficult for my partner to relax and enjoy herself, as she he has a real phobia of spiders.  We were offered tea or coffee, no choice of which type of tea or coffee. The sandwiches were nice enough, but it would have been nice to have been asked what our preference was for fillings. The scones were lovely and warm and the selection of cakes were good. Overall, it was the little things that were missing, no table cloth, tea served in a metal teapot, not china. Not being asked where we would like to sit. It was a gloriously sunny day and would have been nice to have sat outside. It was very nice but not the tea for two experience I was expecting

Excellent Hotel. This was a last minute booking and was so glad I chose this hotel to stay in. Brilliant location, clean, modern and all the staff from the initial booking phone call to check out were friendly, professional and happy! I stayed in a standard double which was very clean, lots of space and plenty of hot water. There is loads of free parking which is especially good when you have a horse box and the hotel is literally 2 minutes drive from Hickstead showground

Nice... but dated. Stayed for a couple of nights for a nearby wedding. The hotel had a very dated feel to it but the room was spacious, had plenty of hot water and the bed was comfy. Staff were friendly and helpful and the food was nice, but nothing special. Although they had wifi it didn't really work very well and we struggled to pick up a signal from our room. Overall it was nice enough for a short stay

More tea? That would have been nice.. Second tea we've had here, both using. Virgin Vouchers. Really glad we didn't pay. The dining room is like an office canteen, lacking decor or atmosphere. So this time we asked if there was a garden so that we could have tea outside with the dog on a beautiful sunny Sunday afternoon. The garden was actually the car park outside the main entrance with tables on the Tarmac. Apart from the lady in reception the five minutes of service we got was noticeable for the lack of smile or talking. Champagne tea for two is half a bottle which gives you a good couple of glasses but the rest was a left down. One and a half rounds of sandwiches (egg, ham and salmon) a scone, a meragine complete with blonde hair, a sponge neither of us fancied and a soft shortcake biscuit. We were totally ignored once the tea had been dumped, no refills or usual "how is everything?" visit. We gave up after an hour of cold tea and walked the ten foot to our car. One advantage of tea on the Tarmac!  There are so many quaint places for afternoon tea around this part of Sussex. This ain't quaint and won't be getting a further visit

Nice place for a wedding package.. We stayed there for one Friday night after a wedding event. Parking is easy with plenty of it, it is also in a rural location which is nice. The staff were very helpful and Giles the manager was very helpful with all the wedding guests needs. Our room 226 was on the first floor close to the stairs so during the night people were heard on the wooden stairs. The room it's self was comfortable and had nice little touches like fruit and a paper. In all a good place to stay. Right now comes the yes or no bit...
Would we recommend the hotel.... Yes we would.
Would we stay again..... Yes we would....

Tip room 226 is quite noisy  due to the wooden stairs

Quiet hotel away from the bustle of Brighton. Four of us stayed here last night for a one night break. We were looking for accommodation in Brighton but were put off by the expensive prices! We came across this lovely hotel hidden 12 miles away in the countryside. The rooms were a generous size with a double bed and tea & coffee facilities. We had a three course meal which was beautiful, every meal was made fresh and was really tasty. We also had a free glass of wine within the offer. The breakfast was really nice with a large selection of cereals, cooked breakfast, fruit juices, yoghurt, fruit and croissants. We were also given a pot of tea and coffee each as well as a round of toast. Would definitely recommend this hotel if on a break in Brighton but don't want to pay top end prices for one night

Nice peaceful hotel. Stayed here for a family wedding. Plentiful parking, spacious bar and rooms massive compared to other small hotels.
Food ok.
Room was cold during the night.
Breakfast was nice although a few more veggie options would've been nice.
Mushrooms and beans for me!
Had a great stay in all

Amazing Wedding!. My sister and (new!) brother in law got married at this hotel on a Friday. Everything was perfect! Giles was, and is, amazing! He is not afraid to get stuck in and help out, and nothing was too much trouble. 
The hotel is in a perfect location, the food was brilliant and the rooms perfect. Dinner was lovely, leak & potato soup to start, chicken with rosti and veg for main and profiteroles for desert, breakfast was also lovely, both hot and cold options available. After the wedding a few of us sat in the bar and they did not shut the bar until we were finished! Think we had the poor bar maid up until 2am!! 
Thank you Giles and the Hickstead Hotel for a wonderful wedding package!!

Afternoon tea . Lovely afternoon tea for my friends birthday. Really welcoming and friendly. Had an extra pot of tea and spare cakes in a doggy bag. We were there for a couple of hours and may have overstayed our welcome though they never said. Cakes were nice and so were the sandwiches. Would recommend this place for an afternoon tea

Friendly welcome. Always made to feel at home. Breakfast is filling and so tasty. Ample and secure parking. Privilege Club benefits include slippers, fresh fruit, newspaper and bottled water. Friendly bar and evening meal add to a pampered feeling

Overnight stay. My partner and i have been visiting this hotel for over 4 years, and this was around our 6th stay.  This was the first hotel we stayed in after we met in 2011, and it's so lovely, and has such special memories, we try to visit at least once or twice a year.  It's a great location for us as we are frequent visitors to Brighton, and The Hickstead is approx a 20 minute drive away, and although not far from the motorway, is in a peaceful rural area.

The rooms are tastefully decorated, and comfortable, the staff are always curteous and polite, and the food in the Oak Tree restaurant inside the hotel is excellent.  Compliments to the chef again.  I chose a tomato soup, double lamb cutlet followed by dark chocolate mousse with shortbread biscuits!  Absolutely delicious as the food always is here, including a quality full English the next morning too.  Thank you again to everyone at The Hickstead - See you soon!

Afternoon tea. We were given a voucher for afternoon tea. After several attempts at booking a convenient date and dealing with a unfriendly receptionist we finally arrived to have our tea. The receptionist was not very attentive an carried on talking to her colleague making us feel unimportant. She then led us to a table in a stark dining room. We were seated very close to two other couples having the same afternoon tea. Although the food itself was plentiful and looked great the whole experience could have been made better. First of all the tables could have been spread out a little so that you could have a romantic time with your other half without the people on the next table listening to your every word. We did ask to be moved but was told we couldn't because we had to sit where it had been laid for tea... a cup and saucer and a plate! I had visions of a nice white table cloth and a pot of tea with water etc. but the table was bare and cold and although we did get a pot of tea it was too strong to drink as I like weak black tea. The music was also awful. There was pop music playing loudly in the restaurant.  The other couples were offered more drinks but we were left until we had finished before we were asked. The waiters and waitresses were professional but only popped in a couple of times, otherwise we felt abandoned. Whilst the chef had done a good job with the cakes and sandwiches it was let down by the organisation in the restaurant. Would we go again? No

Night away - October 2015. My Partner booked a special treat for us to have a night away at the Hickstead Hotel.  This is about our fifth visit to the hotel and we always feel at home here.
We had booked a Dinner, Bed and Breakfast package inc a large glass of wine package directly with the hotel.  On arrival we were greeted by the receptionist who recognised us from our last visit and although we were early our room was ready.  We spent the afternoon in Brighton before heading back for our dinner booked for 7.30pm.  Giles the General Manager was on hand to show us to our table and again recognised us from the last time.  Our three course meal was excellent and the menus enabled us to pick and choose courses all of excellent quality and very well presented.  As a three star hotel other establishments can learn a great deal from this hotel.  In the morning we plumped for a cooked breakfast which was cooked to order and there were a great selection of cereals, croissants and fruit.  Emily our server was very attentive and an asset to the team.  Thank you Giles and the team for another excellent stay we will be back very soon

Lovely afternoon tea... Our children had bought us a voucher for an afternoon tea and we chose the Hickstead.
It does look a little bit like a retirement home, but we were warmly welcomed and seated straight away. The staff were very friendly and helpful and we had an excellent tea. Fresh cream cakes and nice sandwiches. My wife had a soft drink instead of tea and I had a second pot but we were not charged for either of these...Didn't see much of the hotel but it looks very pleasant. All in all a very pleasant afternoon. Thank you
