afternoon tea. We were given a voucher  for afternoon tea for two as a present from our son and daughter in law who had their wedding reception at the Hickstead so we chose to spend our voucher there and we wasn't disappointed. We brought some of the food home as we couldn't eat it all

Overnight stay . We were a family with children so it is a very very good place to stay after a long day in Brighton .
The hotel is sooo clean and comfortable the staff are very friendly and helpful ............there is a little bar down stairs it is nice to have   Some beer ..........
Don't miss the break fast ( smoked salmon and fried eggs)

Visit to Brighton & Eastbourne.. We booked on a deal for two nights with a dinner included. On arrival we were told that there was a wedding at the hotel on the Saturday and were given a quiet room far from the main facilities. This was spacious, very quiet (apart from self closing room doors) with well lined curtains. We had an uninterrupted sleep on both nights 
Breakfast and dinner were excellent, especially the service, but the a la carte dinner is a bit expensive.
We used the hotel, which is only a short drive from Brighton, to visit there and then see friends in Eastbourne

Very pleasant stay. Stayed here for four nights on a business trip. Hotel is comfortable and in a quiet location. The staff are extremely welcoming and service is excellent. They went out of their way to accommodate me and were friendly but not over familiar. 
The food was excellent. Really good choice of well cooked breakfasts - perfect poached eggs nice and runny yolks. Good choice of evening meal and exceedingly well cooked.
Perhaps could do with  a daily 'special' menus as I do not eat meat I would have welcomed more choice over four days. Otherwise excellent

Very comfortable stay. Stayed here for one night whilst attending a nearby function.  Had a lovely afternoon tea at the hotel.  Room was very nice and bed comfortable.  Buffet style breakfast was good with plenty of choice.  Plenty of car parking in nice grounds and very peaceful surroundings.  No complaints at all and would stay here again

Nice quiet weekend away!. Booked two rooms (other was for brother and sister in law) on a package for bed, breakfast and evening meal.  Hotel reception was smooth and room was well appointed with few extra touches like slippers, fruit, water etc.  Cosy little bar for a few pre dinner drinks and a very good evening meal with plenty of choice and attentive waiting staff.  All in all well recommended

Wouldn't stay here again. Nearly all of the staff (cleaners and waiters) were very friendly and the breakfast was the best part of the stay, the full English was really good! The first thing we noticed was the overwhelming strong odour in the passage and room when we went up the stairs. The air smelled like an industrial strength cleaner or some kind of highly scented air spray, a very stale incredibly strong odour which permeated everything. (I really don't know what caused it) We could barely breathe so we slept with the window wide open as the whole room needed a serious airing. The room was a good size but was a bit run down overall, a loose tile on the bathroom floor in front of the sink, stains on the bed and carpet. Could do with general updating. There was NO hot water so it was like doing the "ice bucket challenge" in the shower in the morning. We would have accepted this at a small B&B but at a hotel we expected a hot shower for our money. The pillows were flat like pancakes, like resting your head on a bag of cement powder. Between the smell and the uncomfortable pillow, I had a splitting headache the next morning. When we mentioned the cold shower and pillow to the receptionist her manner and reply left us feeling slightly fobbed off. All anyone want's is a good night's rest, a good breakfast and a hot shower. We got 1 out of 3. We were relieved that we had only booked for 1 night

Wedding delight. My son and his fiancée settled on The Hickstead for a Christmas wedding. As it was between Christmas and New Year, we more or less had the place to ourselves.

A number of us stayed the night before the wedding and they provided an excellent buffet supper for about 30 guests.

Huge car park available - shouldn't be an issue if hotel is fully booked. No vending machines for late night snacks, but there is service station about 5 / 10 minutes walk away.

The rooms were comfortable ( we lucky enough to be on the ground floor very close to the reception) and spacious. Huge bathroom and large towels (often lacking!). Only down side for our room was that the laundry lorry came early one morning and parked outside our window. I notice the Sussex Search and Rescue team seemed to be based here as well.

Members of the party who had family rooms were given inter connecting rooms rather than cramming lots of beds into one room. Nice touch.

Free  WiFi available which didn't seem to suffer with loads of people logging on to the system.

Breakfast was buffet style and plenty of choice from continental style to full English. 

The wedding had been meticulously planned by our new daughter-in-law and overseen by Giles from the hotel. There never seemed anything that fazed him and the level of service from him and the staff was second to none. Full marks all round.

The wedding 'breakfast' (never understood that term) was first class and good alternative veggie and childrens' meals provided. Top notch DJ for the evening entertainment. Evening buffet provided for the guests and a huge pile of sandwiches provided for the late night revellers.

The two best men (we don't anything by half) needed a projector and screen for their speech and this was readily supplied and put in place when needed by the hotel team. The fab Giles again.

Breakfast the next day was again brilliant and no pressure to get us out of the rooms  by the correct time. The team were again helpful with regard to storing presents and dishing out wedding cake to the departing guests.

Cannot recommend strongly enough and will be returning in a few months when I seem to be acting as best man at another ceremony. 

I would say this is neither a budget hotel or luxury hotel but middle of the road, good old fashioned service from a caring and attentive staff. Did I mention Giles?

We had a slight hiccough when an elderly aunt fell over outside but again hotel staff helpful and caring. Thanks goodness for a couple of paramedics in the bridal party. 

(Sorry I can't provide any photos - they seem to be mostly wedding pictures)

Overpriced and a bit dated. This hotel is slightly dated and the room is not that comfortable. I would say it is very overpriced for what you get. The room had the bare essentials but nothing more. The best part of it was the breakfast and the staff were very friendly. Will not be visiting again

Lovely Little Gem. I stayed here for the weekend with my boyfriend after we got a good deal on Groupon for a couple of nights. The grounds are really lovely, and there is plenty of free parking. The room was also nice, spacious, clean and the bed was really comfy. 

The restaurant was nice, a little pricey but the food was really tasty. There wasn't a lot to choose from but we were only there for 2 nights so it wasn't a problem.

The absolute best thing about the hotel was the staff. I didn't meet one person that wasn't friendly and helpful and and each person helped to make our stay a very pleasurable one

Good food and excellent service. Enjoyed a Burn's Night supper here with a group of friends, and have been to the same event over several years.  The hotel atmosphere is very welcoming, the staff are charming and helpful, and the food was excellent.  I have never had a meal here other than the organised Burn's Night, but based on the standard of that, am sure their normal catering would also be very good

Good function,room not so good. Location was great. Room a bit dark,need more lights.Radiator was behind heavy curtains which made it difficult to get changed in a warm room as no heat got into the room
Staff very good and help full

A wonderful gathering of friends. We planned our club meeting of friends at this hotel for a number reasons, easy access to main through route (A23) and ample parking. But get inside, a great reception from the management and time to relax with a drink before lunch. We had planned a simple two course lunch which was impeccably served by staff, the quality of the food was excellent. A great place that we will use in the future

Yummy Cakes and Relaxing Afternoon Tea. The staff were very welcoming and friendly. We booked to have an afternoon tea at the Hotel and it was served in a quiet and relaxing room with comfortable chairs, great atmosphere for a nice tea. We were offered three choices of sandwiches salmon, egg mayonnaise and ham and mustard which my partner really enjoyed however I am not really a fish or egg fan but the ham was lovely. We were then offered a selection of home made cakes and a scone with jam and cream. All the cakes were absolutely delicious and their was a nice selection. We were  offered an endless supply of tea and coffee. We also chose to stay the night in the Hotel too. The room was very spacious, clean and met our expectations. The room was very comfortable to stay in for the night and breakfast was included in the price. The only thing I would say is that at breakfast time it was a little busy and some guests had to wait for the buffet to be replenished however it did not take long for the food to come out. The breakfast was your typical full English and the food was lovely but maybe a little bit more choice of hot food would be nice. There was also a cold breakfast option of cereal, fruit, yoghurts and pastries available. The pastry I did have was very yummy but again there was only two left for me to chose from. All in all our Hotel experience was very good and I would defiantly be recommending it to my friends and we will also be staying here again too

in quite country. It was a nice quite hotel and a comfortable room. We stayed for valentines and was very disappointed with the event. No special decoration or acknowledgement in the dining room that it was a valentines special weekend. Not worth the money

Business trip. Stayed here for just one night while on a business trip. when I stayed the hotel was just getting a little tired, still alright but just starting to show wear. The room was a ample size for one night and breakfast was quite nice. There was a conference being held and parking was a little tight

Don't stay here if you want a good nights sleep. There is so much wrong with this hotel, that I’ll start with the good stuff as that’s the easy part.

The wifi is free and there is no onerous logging on or remembering passwords, it’s open and it’s free.
The room itself was clean and a good size
There is plenty of parking
The breakfast was nice.

Now here comes the bad…

The walls are extremely thin.  I was in room 112 which had the option to be an adjoining room to the room next door.  The door was obviously locked as this option was not taken.  The guests next door arrived back at gone midnight and I could hear every part of their conversation and every word on the television programme they were watching.  They were not being particularly loud, just having a normal conversation.  I was shocked and rather annoyed having been woken up.

There is insufficient lighting – 4 wall lights and that is it.  Fine for the evening wind down but I was trying to work having got back from my meeting earlier than expected and the light was fading, meaning I was struggling to see.

The mattress was rock hard, and I like a firm mattress but this was beyond firm and like sleeping on a plank of wood.  Several times I woke up in pain where my normal sleeping position was impossible due to how hard the mattress was.

The room was also incredibly hot, even though the radiator was off.  I overheated several times.  Opening the window was an option, however it is near a busy road which could be heard.

The bathroom was not as clean as I would have liked.  Small hairs were still present in the sink and the grouting was in need of a good scrub.  There was also mould on the ceiling above the shower.

When  I was checking out, the room had been prepaid but I had to tell the receptionist that who was trying to get me to pay.  She said ‘what makes you thin it was prepaid?’ as if I was trying it on.  I  had to forward my hotels.com booking details to her to prove it – after everything this was quite ridiculous.  I also wasn't asked if my stay was ok, meaning I couldn't provide this feedback to enable them to make improvements.

It was a business trip, and I didn’t get to choose the hotel but will be feeding back to avoid it in the future

Fair but with room for improvement. I tried the hickstead while in town for business more than once. Nice view all around, almost always cheerful staff.
One thing one has to consider is the level of noise when inside a room. The walls are so thin that anything that happens is going to get to your hears.
One other thing is internet speed. That is really annoying, most of the time even checking the mails gets difficult

Best Value and Service. A beautiful location, just 15 mins outsidge Brighton (if you need to go there), with lovely walks and countryside. Staff and management work hard to see you right, and this is perhaps the best recommendation. Our room was really warm and spacious. We had a really quiet night, as they situtaed us away from a party which was taking place that night.   We had a DBB package for £99 - absolutely fantastic value for money. The meal was beautifully prepared and everything on the a la carte menu was included 3 courses, (steak - £5.50 supplement - thats all).  I had the Tuna which was cooked perefctly

Don't knock it!. As an employee of HPC compressed air systems based in Burgess Hill, this hotel is always prebooked and ready to slip into after I've driven the 5 hrs  (after dealing with the southern drivers ) from the laid back West Country- Plymouth.
As a regular that has to forfeit his time on the road dealing with customers, my time spent here is what I need after a hard day in the classroom undertaking training. 
The staff are always welcoming, and for the morning breakfast stint, there is one member of staff who knows me by my first name...
For those who have given negative reports on this place, don't knock it. It's a 20 min drive to the nearest international airport; so close for those who need an overnight stopover to recharge their batteries, or those who just need to wind down after an intense absorption of air compressor components. 😉

A Beautiful Wedding. We stayed at the Hickstead to celebrate the wedding of our dear friends and from their feedback to us, we know that you went over and above the call of duty to make their day as perfect as possible. 

Our two day stay was comfortable and pleasant. The food was great and all staff members were helpful and polite. 

The room where the wedding reception was held looked stunning and it was clear that you went to a lot of trouble to make things look as lovely as they did. So, as you can see, the above is all positive.  

I hope the management do actually take these reviews on board as they say they do, as there is a big BUT here......

What possessed you, (on a day when there was a gale so ferocious that is was given a name - Katie) to think it was ok for the room where the wedding ceremony was held to have a broken window that could not be closed? 

It was clear that the metal in the frame was broken. We did try to close it but couldn't get it past about a 3 inch gap. This meant that a) the temperature in the room was freezing (no wonder the bride was shaking) and b) those sat in the vicinity of the window (us), were  absolutely frozen and subject to rainy blasts coming through. 

This seems such a shame. Quite clearly you would have known about it as the people setting the room up would have seen and felt it. Plus, this room was used twice during the day, so the problem could  have been sorted in the meantime. 

To be honest, gaffer tape may not have looked pretty but your guests would have been far more comfortable. 

So  well done for laying on a superb wedding for our friends, and 
FIX THE WINDOW!

wedding coordination and venue excellent. A warm friendly welcome started our stay off on a good footing.We were welcomed as wedding guests as were the rest of the party.Excellent location and plenty of amenities nearby.The wedding co ordinate did an excellent job Well Done.Bedroom lighting really made my stay under par as unable to see clothes in wardrobe and dressing and make up without mirror light very testing.Bedside lighting also inadequate for reading.Hopefully this can be rectified.Thanks for a Happy Wedding

Shocking. Room is fine apart from hearing the chap in the next roomi vomiting and swearing! Food well ....... ordered steak and after 45 mins waiting apparently they had none left, nor lamb, nor scallops so ordered linguini decided not to eat the shellfish as it looked emmmm pale won't stay here again

Very average afternoon tea in bland surroundings. This would be two and a half and not three if possible.
We won 'Afternoon Tea for two' at our annual Fun Day last July and have finally found a gap in the Hickstead hotel's diary that suits us and them.  I did look on here before we went but always take mixed reviews with a pinch of salt as not everything suits everybody.  However, I have to agree with all those who say the room is bland - brown and tired - bare tables with no tablecloths.  There was no choice of tea and pots brought out with no hot water for top ups, although pot refills were offered.  The food, well, the sandwiches were presented without a choice or garnish, and considering there were two of us, there were an uneven numbers.

The cakes - where do I start!  The scones had been liberally dusted with icing sugar to cover up how brown they were.  I think they were trying to match the decor. No soggy bottoms here. More like crunchy.  I am not sure if anything was homemade. The lemon cake was so dry we came home and had some of the one I had made!  Although the size of the cakes was good, the quality was poor.

The whole experience is what you make it. Perhaps it has been a busy weekend and they have fallen back on freezer/cupboard standbys?  Perhaps they are short of staff?
Whatever the answer I feel the other people in the room who had paid for their tea were badly let down this afternoon and the hotel did itself no good on the 'word of mouth' front

Wedding. My wife and I had our wedding here in February. 

I've read some reviews saying the hotel is awful. We had no complaints at all in fact we had a lovely day. 

The staff are very friendly and couldn't do enough to for us. 

The wedding day itself was lovely and everyone who attended said how thoughtful the staff were. Attention to detail was exceptional. 

The food was lovely, hot and no one had to wait, again some people wanted different choices and this was no trouble at all. 

Giles looked after us from the minute we went to look at the venue right the way through to check out. So thank you very much Giles, you and the team made us feel very special. 

Ok so the hotel isn't 5 star but your not paying 5 star prices. The rooms are £80 per night and this includes a monster full English. 

The rooms are clean and comfortable. 

Put it this way on our return from honeymoon we stayed 1 night at Cisswood house in Horsham expecting something very nice, wrong!! £85 for a night and didn't include breakfast this was another £18 joke and it was over cooked and not much at all. 

The rooms were dirty and dated in need of some attention. 

So bottom line, we had a great time hear and no 1 had a complaint about anything and yes I would stay here again. 

Thank you to all the staff who made our wedding day so special

Wi-fi slower than dial up used to be. The hotel looked good on the website, however it did not match up to its claim. The wi-fi was terrible, taking minutes to load simple pages, over 30 minutes just to download the paper. Ridiculous in 21st century. The room was slightly tired, a cracked sink.

The staff were welcoming and polite. The breakfast was fine and had good service. There was a good selection of cooked items which could be ordered

Given voucher for meal and decided to stay overnight. Room really nice as able to take dogs so did wonder if would be ok but was really nice. On ground floor near car park which did seem to be populated by loud people who either had no home to go to or didn't want to go in to the building ! Not too good if you have young children as language was rather colourful shall we say ! Not fault of hotel ! Food in evening was really good quality - well presented and served by nicely dressed helpful staff. Breakfast was same - good choices, well presented by very helpful staff

Something Missing. I stayed in the hotel for 2 nights, I had booked 4, but a funeral and an early finish of work meant I only needed 2. The hotel offered no compromise or refund of the nights not required. I do understand hotel policy, but customer satisfaction and retention must be important too, there seemed such desperation to obtain my payment for all nights. 

The hotel itself is in need of attention, it reminded me of hotels I stayed in when I was a child in the 70's. The smell of paint was very strong in the corridor to my room, which was clean and big, the bed however, was far too hard and the pillows awful. There was no heating so the room was freezing.

Unexpectedly good restaurant. Stayed for two nights for business. This is a hotel that seems a little dated but the informative, if unusual, letter I was handed at check-in told me the carpets were being replaced and suggests the proprietors know that the buildings needs a bit of sprucing up. 

The front desk staff were friendly but unable to help provide the toiletries that should be available according to the guest handbook - apparently they haven't had toothpaste for some time now, though the garage I was apologetically directed to had everything I needed. 

The lobby smells a bit like cabbage and I wondered if that was a foreshadowing of dinner. Happily it was not and the restaurant, as mentioned above, is surprisingly good. Food was a little slow to arrive but once it did it was obvious that time had been taken to prepare an excellent standard of cuisine. It is very reasonably priced, too, given the quality. 

The bar manager was particularly engaging, quite rightly berating my colleague for not finishing her dessert while being the kind of barman who knows not to keep all of his bottles of ale in the fridge. 

If I lived nearby, I'd be back to that restaurant often

Great place for a long stay. The property is located in a a quite neighbourhood. the staff is very helpful and meets all your needs. The breakfast and dinner is sumptuous and with variety. Transportation is an issue though the hotel arranges a taxi. Better to use your own car

Exceptional value for money. £109 for two people, with afternoon tea, 3 course dinner, and full ( excellent) English breakfast, is value for money that can't be beaten. Forget economy hotels - Premier Inn etc - this is the way to go. Yes, the décor is a little tired , but we had a pleasant room, in a very peaceful location, and the manager could not have been more customer focused and caring. You won't be wowed by the place, but you will get good food, gracious service, and a good night's sleep. Can't be beaten at the price

Excellent base for a stay of any length.. We arrived on Friday 6th May having read awful reviews, we did not have a high expectation.  However, the only smell was the wonderful fresh fragrance of new carpet !  There was absolutely no sign of work being done, and the staff told me that the work had been completed ahead of schedule. They also told me that areas undergoing renovation had been cordoned off accordingly. And that disputes notified whilst the parties are still on-site are ALWAYS addressed immediately. I have absolutely no reason to question this.

 We found the staff to be helpful and courteous at all times. I can only assume that people complain AFTER they leave . . . Mmmmm.

Our room (205) was entirely satisfactory, comfortable and well-supplied. And allowed the pure joy of being able to open a hotel window to allow air in !!! It was very quiet, despite there being a wedding party staying and all rooms fully booked. This meant one morning with a buffet breakfast (obviously, to cope with the numbers involved and no delays), but the food was fresh and frequently replenished. The other mornings we had table service with food cooked to order.

The grounds and environs are very pleasing, especially if you are lucky enough to have good weather.  It is a central location for visiting much of the South Coast area, with good road links.

My one criticism would be that I considered the restaurant dinner menu to be very limited in choice, and unimaginative in content. However we did have two dinners there that were okay, but not inspiring or memorable. They did the job, but that was all.

I would certainly recommend this hotel

wedding. We stayed for a wedding held at the hotel. 
With regard to the wedding, the staff were extremmely helpful, accomodating all needs. As guest and the official photographers, staff went out of their way to help my husband and I. The bride & groom and the brides family were very complymemtary on all aspects of the wedding and planning of...
With regard to the hotel stay.  Our room was ready for us well before check in time. Rooms were very cleans & well presented. A slightly larger bed would have been an asset. Tea & coffee provide. 
Breakfast we very good.
this hotel is well situated near the A23 yet feels as if you are in the middle of the countryside.  Just 20mins, to Brighton with the South Down closer, I would recomend this hotel for a short break. Insidently just 20 mins drive due south of Gatwick Airport

Andrews/Parker Wedding 21st May 2016. I would like to say a BIG, BIG thankyou to all the Hickstead team.  From the very 1st visit to Hickstead when we were just looking for a venue for my daughter's wedding to the actual Wedding day everything was just perfect. Giles & his team were so helpful, & nothing was too much trouble for any of the team. They were all very organised & did everything for us. We were fortunate to be able to set up the reception room the day before,[which they did for us] we had a practice run through with music & walking down the isle with the bridal party. I would most definitely recommend The Hickstead Hotel as a Wedding venue.My daughter & 3 of her bridesmaids stayed the night before at the hotel [which was included in the package]. Everything led to a calm bride-to-be when getting ready  the next day. All our guests enjoyed themselves, & the food was great too. So thank you again for making my daughters wedding day so special. A very happy mother of the bride

Tired. The Hickstead is mediocre at best, the internet did not work the first 2 days, then it was very slow the rest of my stay. Resturant staff were friendly. Breakfast was good. The rooms are spaciousness but getting tired. Hotel due a revamp. The bathrooms in the bedrooms could do without a bath. Should be replaced with a standalone shower

Family reunion. Service was excellent & staff very helpful, our rooms were all close together on the ground floor & same corridor, pleasantly appointed. It was easily located in a rural setting with plenty of parking. Our only problem occurred when the central telephone network for the group of hotels did not connect my relatives to The Hickstead which had the provisional booking for rooms made with the very helpful  Events manager there; on contacting her the problem was resolved. My relatives were impressed with the help &. service & food we received. Ideally located to Brighton & many other local attractions

Lovely remote setting and fantastic service!. I stayed here last weekend with my partner. We were attending a wedding nearby and I arrived at around noon to leave my car in the car park before heading off. I popped into reception knowing full well our room would not yet be ready for check in because I was so early but wanted to notify them that my car was in the car park. The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful and managed to move us to a room that was vacant so that I could check in early - at no extra cost! The room was much bigger than expected and the breakfast was lovely. I went for the continental (yoghurt, fruit, cereal, pastries) but there is also a hot buffet with eggs, bacon, sausages, beans etc. We each got our own pot of coffee that could have lasted us all day. Its a shame the hotel doesnt have a swimming pool but the surrounding countryside is great for an early morning jog. I thoroughly recommend staying here

Convenient hotel on way to Brighton. Welcoming on our arrival, room nice and clean, usual tea making facilities, ensuite bath/shower with toiletries provided.  Delicious dinner in restaurant , quiet rural location for a peaceful night, breakfast was more than we could manage!

Our wedding day. My husband and I would like to thank Giles and his team for making our wedding day so perfect. We fell in love with the hickstead hotel from our first visit. Everything ran so well thanks to Giles who was with us through the whole day, and to all his team who helped with all the setting up and making our wedding room so beautiful. We can't thank you all enough for all your hard work and making our wedding so special.  Thank you Nicola and Adam Parker

Nice venue for a family gathering. We had a family weekend and dinner at the The Hickstead.  Many of the group stayed at The Hickstead with others staying in other nearby hotels. Set a couple of miles from Burgess Hill in open countryside. The dinner was excellent with good service and food. The bar is very small for the number of guests, only space for 1 barman and hence slow service. Nice place. Would visit again.

Afternoon Tea with a smile:-). We enjoyed our afternoon teat here, the staff were extremely attentive, their service was good and the tea, sandwiches and cakes were excellent.  Our cake stand made us smile as it was very wobbly. I thought the ratio of sweet to savory was not quite right and maybe one should be given the choice of less sweet and more savory, as I thought the sandwiches were lovely.  All in all a very pleasant experience - thank you staff

Not that impressed. We had a pleasant evening, for a meal that had been bought for us. I could not make my mind up about this hotel. It is pleasant enough for a quiet drink, it has 2 menus one for more up market the other catered for chips and burgers

In the middle of nowhere but a nice hotel. Not a lot to say really except the sat nav told me to go left instead of right just before I got there. The service and breakfast was fine. Wi fi not great. The room was comfortable and large enough. There is no lift

A very pleasant weekend. We stayed in a superior room which was spacious, well-equipped and immaculate. The bathroom was well designed and had both bath and shower.  The television was easy to use (not like so many in large hotels which are complicated). A bowl of fruit and two large bottles of water were provided.
This room was better thought-out than a number of 5 star hotel rooms we have recently experienced.
The food was excellent and the efficient and cheerful staff made the visit a pleasure

Lovely hotel and great staff.. What a little treasure this place is, a lovely hotel for a 2 night quiet break and only 25 minutes from Brighton. Stayed for 2 nights (Friday & Saturday) had a lovely peaceful break which the staff helped make even more peaceful, nothing is too much trouble.

Food was lovely breakfast was plentiful and service was excellent, very polite and attentive. The only thing i would improve is the sausages, although they were very tasty they could do with browning off a little, to others it may look undercooked.

Dinner was fantastic the food was amazing so was the service, we had a pre planned and pre paid meal including wine, due to us now not wanting the wine, they allowed us to change this for drinks from behind the bar which was a nice touch.

i would definitely return as this is lovely and quiet and only a few miles from the busy Brighton

10th -11th July 2016. Lovely hotel in a lovely, quiet location. Definitely need a car to get around. The historic Bluebells Railway is nearby. Haywards Heath is the nearest town which had a good array of restaurants but not much else. Brighton is approximately 30 minutes by car. The hotel restaurant service was slow but the meal was hot and very filling. The menu was varied,as was the Lighter Bite's menu.The buffet style Breakfast was good with a wide array of cereals and cooked breakfast choices.Staff were lovely and approachable. Car Park had ample spaces. Only complaint was the room was very  warm and the window was very hard to open. 

Only spent one night as we were visiting friends in the nearby village of Boney but would happily stay for longer as there is much to see in the surrounding area

Joint 70th Birthday Meal. This was a visit for a Virgin Experience joint 70th Birthday present.
Staff very efficient and friendly, food to suit all tastes, staff helpful to cater for my dairy free diet, certainly a place to return to.
Set in nice open country we had a table with view of South Downs on a pleasant summer evening

Birthday night away! . This has always been a special place and it was my partner Robert's birthday and a night away seemed the perfect gift! I booked the summer special Pimms and Canapés followed by a three course meal which went down a treat but the menu was limited and not the usual bigger selection we have had in the past! Anyway a comfortable stay an excellent breakfast and always nice to be welcomed back by staff who remembered our last visit! We will be back

We were Married here.. We had our Wedding here, which included our taster menu in March. From the first day we contacted the hotel we were very impressed by their attention to detail, nothing we asked was declined. We invited over 70 guests to the ceremony and wedding breakfast. And 30 evening guests. The food was excellent and all dietary choices were taken care of, vegetarians, vegans and pescatarian no problem. Everybody who stayed loved their rooms, and enjoyed their breakfasts. The staff were superb and the Manager was brilliant, he was with us from start to finish and constantly checked with us that all was well. We cannot fault the day, the evening DJ Jon was excellent, one friend of ours was an entertainment manager and told us Jon was one of the best DJs he had ever heard, and he has been in entertainment all his working life! I could go on but we certainly would highly recommend this hotel to everyone

Fantastic Hotel. We could not fault, staff were lovely, food was spot on,evening meal and breakfast was perfect. Room was nice,but was really hot,there was no a/c but when i asked for a fan, they brought one up to our room within 5 min :) 
It was my partners 60th birthday, when i mentioned it to the girl on reception, i thought nothing of it,but that evening we ate a lovely meal, the waiter brought out from the kitchen a cake/Pudding with candle in, thought that was really special...Thank you again :) . Would stay again for sure

Wedding. My daughters wedding the hotel was excellent. The staff friendly and very helpful
the food excellent and the planning so good every thing went without a hitch!
Most of us stayed two nights and the room was clean and comfortable.
I cannot give enough praise it was a truly memorable weekend

My Birthday Treat. This was the first hotel my partner & I stayed in after we met, so it's a special place for us and we have stayed many times.  It was a Birthday treat for me and we enjoyed a lovely dinner in the Oaktree Bistro - excellent as usual, except we were given the lighter bites menu rather than the full menu, so choice was a bit limited.

All in all a great little 3 star hotel, and all staff were friendly and helpful as usual.  We will return

2 nights and breakfast. very good. Our double room was very comfortable. The bathroom and shower just as required.
Very good breakfast with great choice of cereal and cooked breakfast. So much that I didn't need any lunch!
The staff are very helpful

Pleasantly surprised. We were pleasantly surprised by our stay here; the rooms were an excellent size and very clean if a little dated in style/decor.  The first floor room was very quiet and we had no problems with noise despite the hotel being full and a wedding taking place on the second night of our stay. Noise was more of an issue in the ground floor room but nothing beyond what would be expected.

The only disappointment was the poor outdoor facilities, a complete lack of garden space, just a few chairs on the car park.  As the weather was so lovely we would have liked to sit outside for a few drinks but opted to go elsewhere

Afternoon tea. My husband and I went here for an afternoon tea for two. We paid £17.50 each and anticipated that it was the correct price for an afternoon tea in a modest hotel. 
Well ! The surroundings are fab,with good views of the downs etc. The Hotel itself was a little sad in places and could do with updating. For example the dining room chairs looked if they were from the 1980s and the dining tables were all bare "mock "wood with no cloths and décor really not so pretty...minor points I know but they all add to the ambience. Tea consisted of a pot of tea or coffee (very good tea) each which could be replenished again and again; A plate of 9 finger sandwiches was bought to the table. Rather unsophisticated in that they were very large , not the freshest and each one had just one crust left on ! At the same time as the sandwiches, a tiered cake plate was added to the table. This was the most hilarious thing as it was so wonky and wobbly and chipped around the edges, we had to prop it up on the table using a serviette! On the tiered plate there were 2 fruit scones some jam and cream. the scones were very nice and homemade too...by far the best part of the tea.There was a choice of cakes on the bottom layer. Consisting of 2 bitter chocolate mini eclairs,2 cream meringues and 2 massive "builder like " portions of the weirdest cake ever. It looked and tasted somewhat like carrot cake mixed with ginger cake! On top of each slice was the most disgusting "frosting" and I use that term very loosly...it was slimy, oily and taste free. It almost slipped off the cake top...yuk. Service from a solitary Maître'd was just that...solitary. Don't think that this was my best experience of an afternoon tea, cant imagine they have many returners. In fact as we left we were going to voice our comments to the Maître'd but he vanished into thin air. COULD DO SO SO MUCH BETTER

Tired. The reception staff were all very pleasant, but there is no disguising the fact that this hotel is past its prime. The décor is tired and in need of a facelift, but the place itself is clean. We had a room on the ground floor, so opening the window was an issue. I would guess that the bed is rather old, and not very comfortable, but a good size. The lights in the room are not very bright so you have to go to the bathroom for applying makeup. The bathroom is clean but the grouting in the shower (above the bath) could do with a go seeing to with lots of bleach. The shower itself is powerful (but clearly quite old) and I'm not sure I really like the use of a shower curtain rather than a screen - pretty sure it's not terrifically hygienic! We didn't use the bar or restaurant facilities, so can't comment on them. Plenty of on-site parking and the grounds look well-kept

Overnight stay in transit. We only stayed the one night but from the short stay the positives were: ample parking, good bed, decent size and firmness; powerful shower, good size room for two persons. Negatives were: the doors were heavy and slammed on closing. In a long corridor every closing door would reverberate throughout and as guest were returning to their rooms particularly around 1 - 2am sleeping took a while. The building construction appeared light so not only the doors made disproportionate noise but so would guests who were just chatting as they walked down the corridor. This is obviously a difficult one for any hotel. The room was a bit stuffy but opening the window obviously helped but as it was a ground floor room, leaving it open all night was not really an option since the window opening restriction was just a sprung loaded hook. So in conclusion the hotel in my opinion is average but would I choose this over the Travelodge around the corner? And the answer would be yes

Pleasant stay every time.. Set in the countryside beside a small lake with plenty of parking space. The building is a bit tired but is vey clean so no real cause for complaint. The restaurant is good and the bar although small is fully stocked. Functions are well catered for and popular for weddings

nothing remarkable at this hotel. Stayed for a weekend while visiting friends locally. Room was average, clean but nothing special, the shower was good! Breakfast was more like a 2 star hotel than what I expected from this hotel. An average buffet usual cereals and stuff. Hot buffet was ok but nothing special. We did not eat in the restaurant but had room service which was ok. 
The service was indifferent, reception was ok but definitely not friendly. Restaurant processes us through breakfast

cost to much a advert hype promises lot more then deliver. room ok
stains on carpet from while ago
loo didn't flush properly all u could hear running water trying to fill
beds hard uncomfortable cheep flat duvets hard think spare blankets
keys to open door took some getting use to.
food poor rushed expensive
cheap tea one tea bag in teapot
cheap coffee no decent coffee
boring expensive menu
greasy
fish haddock rubber
toast only one side.
pricing bill error.
big time
really check bills
unimpressed sad look forward to sumptuous stay
 felt was having a break relaxing
instead uncomfortable
over priced 
rushed
not enough staff
bar very very expensive 
did not drink alcohol
soda lime and  lemonade ice nearly £5.00
wish we had never gone
unimpressed

Dated. The Hickstead feels like a hotel that was really nice 20 years ago but hasn't had a penny spent on it since then. 

Positives: It's a nice location, quiet enough, the staff are pleasant and parking easy. 

Negatives: The room is extremely hot with no way of cooling it, the window can't be opened fully as its on a restraining lead despite being on the ground floor. The food is plain pub style food and the drink selection limited. The bed isn't the most comfortable, and the furnishings generally have seen better days. The wifi is free, but slow to the point of being unable to load an email let alone stream media. 

Overall, a reasonable place to overnight in, especially in winter, but not for a longer stay and not for the avid internet user

Pleasant stay and excellent value. Booked a two night special package which was dinner, bed and breakfast while using the hotel as a base for visiting Brighton. Booked a family room over the phone and checked in by friendly staff who were great for the duration of our stay.

Our package gave us a three course meal from the main menu and this was good value and of a good standard. We had to pay for our son's food but this still meant good value. 

The restaurant staff looked after us very well - nothing too much trouble.

The room was comfortable for what we needed. Wifi slow but free. The corridors are very echoey and any conversation in the corridors do find their ways into the adjoining rooms - but this is more due to the guests than the hotel.

Breakfast was fine - on Saturday we gave our orders for hot breakfast but on sunday it was buffet but it was much busier.

We were a family of three - most of the other guests were a bit older than us shall we say ! However, the staff were very welcoming to our 10 year old !

Brighton was only a 15 minute drive away.

Great stay - most enjoyable

Lovely hotel.. Stayed with my husband for 2 nights, room very clean and staff friendly. Breakfast was delicious and plentiful. Free wifi included. Only slight downside, not much to do around the area without travelling a distance

Perfectly ok average three star hotel. The staff are helpful, the decor is fine, it is perfectly good for an overnight stay. There are fields all around - the outlook is not beautiful but it is nice to be surrounded by green. It is a bit of a drive from any sort of destination. We had been to a wedding in the vicinity and needed somewhere to crash out afterwards. I suppose that does raise a small niggle, that after a late night it is a real pain that breakfast finishes so early - at 10.00 at a weekend. It is worth noting that there is nowhere else to go for breakfast nearby - we had to drive into Burgess Hill which is about fifteen or twenty minutes to drive and park. The hotel does have room service but that also finishes at 10.00

Strawberry meringue to die for!. My daughter and I went to the Hotel for a delicious afternoon tea, a birthday gift from her boyfriend. What a treat! The hotel is in quiet surroundings with views to the South Downs, plenty of car parking space and with helpful and attentive staff. I had a pot of coffee and my daughter a pot of tea. Plenty of fresh milk on the table and we were offered refills if required. The finger sandwiches were lovely. Fresh bread - not too thin and not too thick. But the scones and cakes were superb! The scones were of a robust proportion, warm, and accompanied by an ample helping of clotted cream and jam. There were two eclairs, two dainty macaroons, two generous pieces of lemon cake, two scrummy shortbreads and the two creations of Meringue (light as a feather) topped with delicious slices of strawberry and drizzled with chocolate. Absolute yum factor! We were almost beaten....and couldn't manage a single morsel more for the rest of the day in spite of having a 2.30 sitting. Really nice. Thank you

Ideal location for our family meet up. The whole family booked in to stay here, as we had a family wedding locally, and we had stayed here 10 years ago.
We had all checked in Friday evening, and met up for a family meal in the restaurant which the hotel had pre-arranged for us. Then being a lovely warm summers night, we sat outside drinking and talking.
Breakfast is indeed plentiful with pretty much all you could want, cereal's pastries or a full English....my only complaint is that the only eggs are scrambled which if you're not down first thing become dried out. 
The day after the wedding was a nice quiet day, sitting out in the sun again, before meeting up with the newly weds for a Sunday lunch locally.
Check in and out was quite quick and worry free.
Staff are very helpful and friendly, and my request for an iron and ironing board.....plus an ice bucket...we're delivered promptly.
A good base to explore the local area if you don't know it....about  15 mins drive down to Brighton, or a quick taxi to Burgess Hill and get the train

Afternoon tea. My friend and I had afternoon tea yesterday and were rather disappointed.  Firstly the tea I had was an excellent pot and my friends coffee was equally good. We started off our afternoon tea with sandwiches which were ok but not exciting. We were given 9 fingers of sandwiches, an odd number for 2 people, which consisted of 3 each of egg mayonnaise, ham and mustard and smoked salmon. We both felt that the sandwiches lacked sufficient filling, except the egg mayonnaise. The smoked salmon was very plain and would have been better with some cress or cucumber added. Also there was too much mayonnaise in the egg sandwich.

Onto the cake section - we had two warm scones, mine was hollow which was strange but the jam and clotted cream were nice.  We then had a really delicious small meringue with cream and fresh strawberries followed by an eclair which was rather large.  We did look at the piece of cake on the tiered plate but it was large and had some thick topping which looked very unappetising and neither of us ate any of it, and a large shortcake biscuit, again we didn't eat it.  I did take the shortcake home and my husband ate part of one of them and said that it was so sweet he couldn't finish it.  There were a number of other people having tea and lots of the cake was left.  In our opinion the cake and shortbread were much too large.

Quiet a lot of the cake selection was based on cream so if you don't want a cream overload then it would be worth mentioning before booking afternoon tea here. We didn't feel it was good value for money or a very enjoyable experience.

We ordered more tea and the waiter promptly bought more tea and coffee, took our milk jug away for refilling and forgot to bring it back, not a big deal but attention to detail is important

Comfy like an old pair of slippers.... Stayed here at this nice quiet hotel many times and always had an almost identical and satisfactory experience.  Plenty of car parking.  Nice staff led by highly experienced manager, who always tries his best to make every guest happy and has an ability to choose some excellent staff for his team...every one of them seems to be nice.  The bar is often staffed by the friendly, helpful Bar Manager, Hasan, who has a great sense of humour and can entertain you with some interesting stories.  It is worth ordering a pint of Guinness, just to watch the whizzy machine making it!  Rooms are clean and well equipped with decent TV...no air-con,  so can be hot in the summer.  Beds seem a bit too firm, but lots of people like that.  Some bathrooms are a bit dated, but who cares, they are nice and clean and the showers work really well.  Dinner can be a bit "hit and miss", but the restaurant specials are normally good.  Breakfast is cooked to order and very tasty

Quiet location. We had to attend a funeral in Brighton, and on the advice of a cousin booked here.

It is located off the busy A23 but sufficiently far away not to be affected by traffic noise.

The reception and restaurant are located in what looked like the old building with rooms in a newer block built on.

The rooms are modern with all the usual facilities with table and chairs. 

We had booked breakfast which on our first morning was self-service but was waitress service on the second. It depends on how many people they have staying we were told.

After our main reason for staying, we returned for an evening meal. This was reasonably priced and offered a good choice of menu.

There is also a service area off the access road to the A23 with a Little Chef in case you don't want to use the hotel's facilities

I would say this is a working hotel,. The reason.............. you would only be here because your on the way to or from somewhere else. The facilities are OK but as has been remarked it is in need of some refurbishment in the rooms.  The dinning room is fine and serviceable.
The meals were ok for the traveller but not a place to experience a culinary adventure. Staff were very good and there was plenty of service. Parking very good.
On the two night we were there they had functions and while I did not see the function room there was little or no noise from that area of the hotel.
As a base to explore the east Sussex coast or a place to stay prior to accessing Gatwick it is in a good position

Felt compelled to review straight away. Visited today for an afternoon tea that was bought for us as a gift earlier this year.

Front of house staff very friendly but hotel/restaurant looked quite tired. 

Tea and food very average: no choice of tea (and we'd expect proper tea not tea bags), no clotted cream (this should be a given, right?!) although scone freshly cooked and very tasty. Sandwiches were a bit lack lustre with fairly tasteless fillings and slightly stale bread. Macaroon seemed stale too. The meringue with strawberries and cream were very nice.

We bagged the rest of the cakes to take home.

Perhaps we have been spoilt by some 'top end' afternoon tea experiences in the past but when you think you could do better at home you know there's an issue

Countryside charm?. You feel you are in the middle of nowhere when you go to this countryside hotel.

Very nice to sit outside and have a drink surrounded by beautiful fields. A walk before dinner is very nice.

Going inside it the interior decoration is what you would expect and the rooms are spacy. 

But is it clean? The seats of the chairs in the social areas look dirty (maybe they cannot be more clean than this).  The carpet in my room made me wish I had brought my flip-flops

Deluxe Afternoon Tea . We visited today for a Deluxe Afternoon Tea brought for us as a wedding gift, my husband read the reviews before we choose this hotel, having been to a lot of these type of things unfortunately the look of the hotel is slightly for me disappointing although please bear me mind as I say we have been to lots, the interior inside the dining area needs updating and is looking tired, I think it needs brightening and modernising which I can see is in the plan. We could not however fault the food in terms of the actual cakes and scones they were lovely and scones were warm, the cakes delicious! My husband felt the sandwiches could be a little smaller and a bit more choice and maybe presented a little better but again the bread was lovely and fresh and the fillings were nice. It could all maybe be improved a bit with nicer cups and saucers and a nice teapot but again if the hotel are not specialising in this sort of thing, would it be worthwhile for them. I would definitely say it was average with the food scoring high. Staff were lovely and helpful also

Hotel Manager worth his weight in gold!. We attended a wedding at the Hickstead and arrived midday (2 hours prior to check in) reception staff very accommodating and gave us rooms that had been cleaned so we could get luggage away prior to service.
This is a 3* hotel and you get no more/less than expect, rooms are tired but comfortable - two things which would improve immensely are a fan in room I know weird request as we are in the UK! but as all hotel rooms only allow less than a fag size gap when opened I do think you can always add the spare blanket but not able to cool down easily. second thing is windowless bathrooms are the norm but extractor fan is essential to clear steam/odours!
anyway wedding went so smoothly this is thanks to I think his name was 'Giles' (apologies if not) the best manager I have ever met, hope the hotel chain read this as this man needs a payrise - you would go under without him as any minor hotel faults are compensated by the attentive but not grovelly service given by the manager and he leads all staff behind this 10/10 service.
wedding food which would not expect much when mass catering is involved, especially in 3* restaurant but no every course was SO good, extra gravy on tables, vegetables plenty - cooked & hot! my partner is the worst food critic and he said best food he had had so praise indeed from the man who has never praised my cooking in 25 years!
beautiful grounds and lovely outside area off the function room, which is a walled garden so very sheltered. anyway to sum up this is an excellent venue for a function or to eat and with a couple tweaks to rooms not a bad place to stay either.
Well done and many thanks to The Hickstead and staff

great location. close to Gatwick (so an option for early flights), close to Brighton and A23, so very convenient for travelling around the area. Of course you need your own car.
Hotel itself was very clean, good sized rooms, we only ate breakfast here but that was very good and a reasonable price. Room price was also very good. Staff very helpful, we checked in really early so they looked after our bags and even took them up to our room when it was ready

Just fabulous. My husband and I went for a relaxing 2 days break to The Hickstead Hotel. When we arrived we were very welcomed and remembered from previous stays. The hotel room was lovely - it was large and clean. 
The food - we were welcomed by Debbie each morning who looked after us so well and like all the staff couldn't do enough for us. Breakfast- you can have continental, cereals, fruit, yogurts and a cooked breakfast. 
Evening meals - plenty of food and absolutely delicious - very very good value for money.
There isn't a lot to do on site which is why we went there but you are very close to the downs, to the seafront, airports and shopping centres. 
We chose the Discover Sussex deal - B&B, 3 course meal and a glass of wine for £98 for both of us !!! 
I will definitely recommend The Hickstead Hotel to all my friends

Afternoon Tea. Quite a remote hotel but worth finding.  Visited on a Sunday afternoon for tea which was delicious.  The sandwiches, scones and cakes were all freshly made.   Staff are very pleasant and friendly.  Endless pots of coffee or tea.  Would thoroughly recommend.  Ideal venue for special occasions

One Night Stay. We had an executive room but not sure what makes it an executive room!  Perhaps it was the robe and slippers.  The bathroom was tiny with a horrid shower curtain.  The bed wasn't comfy, neither were the pillows.  Honestly a Premier Inn provides more!  The room looked basic/standard I'm sorry to say.  The only positive was it was clean

Afternoon Tea. The hotel is well signposted from the main A23 and easy to find. The welcome was warm and genuine from the front desk as soon as we arrived. Excellent organisation meant we were passed on to waiting staff and a swift service continued with the delivery of food and refreshment in short order. The food itself was gorgeous and we could not have wanted for more. A pleasant, quiet setting gave a relaxing time which is just want we wanted.

Quiet tasteful music played in the background and helped us enjoy our time. Although only a short visit it is a place we will gladly return to in order to have a longer stay

Quiet hotel with good food. We stayed at this hotel for a weekend in October on the Discover Sussex dinner, bed and breakfast offer.  It was excellent value for money. The room was clean and spacious but the bed was a little hard, although some people may like this.

The food was excellent. there was plenty of choice for dinner and breakfast.  Service was outstanding on our first day, although there were only a few tables taken.  On our second night (Saturday) the service wasn't quite so good as the restaurant was nearly full at dinner and again for breakfast. However, the quality of the food made up for this. The house wine was drinkable but could have been better.

We got the train into Brighton for the day and the receptionist was very helpful in giving us directions to the train station. It made it an easy journey and well worth a visit.

I would recommend this hotel for a quiet weekend getaway and would stay here again

Over priced. We travelled straight from work and arrived at the hotel at 9:05pm, five minutes after the restaurant stopped serving food. This is fair enough but I expressed a wish for a snack with room service, to be told that wasn't possible. I then asked if I could have takeaway, this was permissible but unfortunately didn't arrive until 11:00pm - beware Papa John's. After this less than splendid start to our "relaxing stay" the room was hotter than a recent stay in the tropics! After eating a rather late meal of pizza we finally managed to go to bed ( my side the springs had gone and had to sleep in a dip) people came crashing, laughing, shouting along the hall 1:30am. 2:30am the room shook as a body fell upstairs, now unable to fall back to sleep we then realised how thin the walls were, it felt like the chap next door was snoring next to me. Sadly the decor is tired, chipped away and worn. Breakfast was cold with only a couple of options for vegetarians. When you enter the reception you are hit by a blast of (sewage - toilet air) £244 is an extortionate price for two nights here. Very disappointed!

Lovely stay, very helpful staff. We stayed for a wedding and were greeted by friendly and welcoming staff.

Our room was large, modern and clean and was ready early so that we could check in.    

Breakfast was very nice, with a wide selection.

With the friendly staff and the hotel not being big it had a very peaceful, friendly atmosphere.

The hotel is situated in a isolated position near to the A23

Sons wedding not at this hotel. It was the smell as soon as I entered...I did not except first room so took second...mould on window and celing in the bay...work had been carried out on the celing above bed  but not finished paper peeling ...shower the shelve to hold shower gel was rusty toilet seat fixing dirty...when i dropped my earing trying to look for it  I found more than a earing...dust under the dresser once stall removed and a old toe nail ...i could not wait to leave how they could call this deluxe room.....breakfast the mushrooms were hard.   I tried to find some where else but at short notice as I was going to my Sons wedding I needed to get ready there.....
Lynn Surrey

Great food, but sadly appearance and cleanliness really let's it down. Just returning home from an overnight stay. On arrival we were a bit disappointed with how run down the outside appearance is, a nice looking building but all the woodwork needs attention. Reception staff friendly enough, our room sadly didn't live up to its superior title, again it's all a bit tired looking, our toilet seat was broken, extractor fan didn't work and there were lots of cobwebs.
On a better note, our meal was excellent in the hotel restaurant, the staff were very attentive and friendly and breakfast was also really nice, if the rating was on food alone it would be five stars! 
We expressed our concerns to reception at check out so hopefully they will rectify the issues we had. Just a little sad that our celebration was tainted by a lack of attention to detail

Lovely stay. We spent Saturday night here on a bed, breakfast and evening meal package. The staff were very hard working and incredibly friendly. The food was terrific, so much better than I was expecting from this type of fixed package. The breakfast whilst buffet style was of an exceptionally high standard.The whole evening was relaxing and I would highly recommend a stay. The room was clean and comfortable and reception were always available if you had any additional needs.

The only downside is the lack of soundproofing in the rooms - not a particular problem for us as the only noise was next door's snoring and I'm used that! 

All in all, I thought it was great value and look forward to staying again

Nice hotel but let down by some clientele. We stayed here as a result of a deal sent to my wife as we had stayed there before for an Afternoon tea and Overnight stay with breakfast about 4 years ago. We really enjoyed that stay and so were looking forward to the Cream Tea, 3 course meal and breakfast.
As we have a dog, getting away for  weekend is often difficult and were pleased that dogs were welcome here.
One of the first things I noticed was the 3 parking spaces closest to the main door. The 2 outside bay were disabled bays. However, they were all normal sized and both had plant pots close to the front (one to keep a fire exit route clear!) which made it awkward to park.
We did arrive a tad early and there was no-one on the reception desk to greet us. However a receptionist was soon found and we were booked in.
We knew that by having a dog friendly room, we would not be getting the best rooms. Ours was tidy but tired and on the ground floor. However, I did notice that there were a couple of tains on the carpet and that there was some blackening of the sealant around the bath and the fittings.
Obviously the dog could not be left in the room whilst we were eating and so after putting him in the car, enjoyed our cream tea in the lounge. That was very nice fresh scones and hot tea/coffee. One scone each - which was ample, bearing in mind we also had a three course dinner later.
The grounds at the front are plentiful and ample to exercise our dog. Load of grass and plenty of trees (which obviously is helpful if you have a dog!).  My only issue here is that there are no bins for dog waste and as a result you have to give the bagged waste to the receptionist for disposal.
When we went down for our evening meal, this was when we had our problems. There was a group of 8 guests that met here for a meal and overnight stay. They had previously met on holiday and had a re-union there. This group were all extremely drunk, foul mouthed and abusive to not only the waiting staff but also to us. We had our starters before they came to their table which was next to ours. The starter was very nice. Before our main meal was served, this group sat down and were very loud and shouting, "F...ing this abbd F... that etc etc". One particular "lady" was very rude towards us and as a result our waiter - Artur - asked if we would like to move tables. We declined as it would look obvious as to why we moved.
The main course was served, which again was very nice, but sadly we felt we had to rush our food - just to get away from this group!
We opted to have our Desserts in the lounge due to thei behaviour, but even this was questioned by this "Lady"
We would have liked to stay and have another drink, but again because of their behaviour we felt disinclined to do so and therefore walked the dog and returned to our room.
There was another party going on in the function room, and although there were numerous guests there, we did not hear them at all.
In the Room, my wife had a shower which she found flowed hot and cold with no consistency.
Personally, I found that the bed was extremely hard and as I am a larger build person, found it uncomfortable as I did not seem to make a dent and I ended up with painful hips. Previously the bed was very comfortable.
All was reasonably quiet and I managed to fall asleep, but then at about 1am, I woke up and then I heard the noise, which was only in the background in the first instance. However, this got louder and louder and I could clearly hear the "Lady" shouting the group singing at the tops of their voices. I don't know where their rooms were but at 2am onwards it became worse and worse. So much so - at 2.45am I telephoned the Mick in reception to complain. The noise subsided at about 3.30am. My dog normally sleeps through anything but this unsettled him!
Both my wife and myself both felt sorry for the waiting staff that evening - Artur and Lauren who were really working hard and attentive, but were both subject to constant abuse from this group! 
All in all, this group spoilt our nice romantic weekend away and now I will seriously reconsider about returning to the hotel. 
This is a nice hotel, but I do feel that it is tired!

A nice hotel it's a shame the bar closed so early.. A nice hotel and good value. We didn't check in until 10pm on Friday and where amazed that the bar was closed so early. We were told that as there were no customers for the bars so the guy went home. I would have thought that the receptionist could have managed to pour a couple of drinks but didn't offer.( Friday 2nd Dec if the management are bothered )
Now the breakfast. I have been fortunate enough to have stayed in some of the best five star hotels and the cooked breakfast served at the Hickstead would be a hard act to follower by any hotel. It was great.
The hotel is in lovely surroundings, open country with horses in an adjacent field.
My wife and I were in the area for a funeral but will be back on a happier occasion for sure

Ok but a little tired. One night stay as we were in the area for a party. Pleasant enough room but the bathroom was a bit of a let down - odd choice of floor covering (paving??) that makes it look like a dirty exterior floor.
Breakfast was ok but we waited ages for teas and coffees - the staff were very nice and the restaurant was busy but we did wait a long time.
All in all it was ok value and perfectly satisfactory for a one night stay and had no pretensions to be anything more than it is

Convenient off the A23. Don't be discouraged by my 3-star rating. It actually is about 2 1/2 stars but perfectly clean and nice. The room was spacious, clean and welcoming. The ubiquitous "full English breakfast" was ample and good and the staff very friendly and accommodating our every wish. There is no elevator to the 1st (Am. 2nd) floor, but they kindly helped us lug our bags up. The setting in the English countryside is lovely and tranquil and access to the nearby dormitory town of Burgess Hill very convenient indeed. We shall stay here again, as we have family in BH and formerly stayed elsewhere.
Our only criticism is that the walls are thin and conversations of adjacent rooms can be overheard quite plainly.
Access to the hotel off the main London/Brighton road (A23/M23) very convenient - only 1 1/2 minutes! Access for Crawley and Gatwick Airport easy. Despite busy holiday traffic, we buzzed around via M25 to Heathrow without a hitch.
In short, don't overlook this place. It's not meant to be fancy but it is clean, warm and welcoming

The Hickstead. Beware the paper thin walls in the modern bedroom block, particularly if you have a connecting door to the adjoining bedroom! Our neighbour spent much of the night on his mobile, he might just as well have been in our room. Otherwise, the bedroom was comfortably furnished and bathrooms fairly good; the public areas are a bit dated. The saving grace was the friendly and helpful staff. Quite pricey for the quality. The breakfast was ok

Tired. It's always good to be asked how your stay was when checking out. This didn't happen when I left The Hickstead. Maybe they didn't care? If asked I would have pointed out that my room was cold and I couldn't adjust the temperature and the bed felt old, lumpy and the mattress at least in need of replacing. Just not good enough at £75/night. The hotel is generally tired, run down and soulless.  An extra £10 for breakfast confirmed for me that on this occasion, I'd made a big mistake choosing this hotel. To it's credit though to be fair, the breakfast was very good!

Overnight stay. This is the third year we have been.  the staff were excellent nothing to much trouble for them. As a party of 14 the  food and service excellent. Very good breakfast selection. Bedrooms clean and tidy

Simply delightful . I often stay at the Hickstead Hotel for work. The location is great, the staff are amazingly friendly and it feels like home from home. The rooms are very comfortable and they have baths too!! The breakfast is great and the bar offers a good range of beers

Smashing Weekend. We first came here a few years ago as a present from our children and really enjoyed the stay. Last year, my wife found an offer for the hotel and as we enjoyed our first stay so much, we booked again. Sadly last time, due to the behaviour of a group of disruptive and rude guests, our stay was spoilt. 
We wanted to get rid of that memory and therefore booked again.
We went down last weekend (25-26/2/17), with our dog as well! We were greeted by staff and went to our allocated room. Our room was, as expected, very clean and comfortable with everything we could wish for. Tea and coffee, TV, fully en-suite with soap/shampoo etc. supplied along with towels. Extra pillows in the wardrobe and a Trouser press - fantastic.
We booked for dinner and when we walked into the restaurant, we were recognised by the waiter - Artur and welcomed with a handshake and a smile and a genuine, "So pleased to see you again!"
We were seated at our table and we decided what to eat. Once ordered, we were then given a taster appetiser that the chef was trying out (Gnocchi with black cabbage and a sauce). It was very nice and neither of us had tried that before and both really enjoyed it.
For starters, I had the Buffalo Mozzarella, whilst my wife had Chicken Satay. For mains, I had supreme of chicken and my wife had lamb shank and we both had a peanut slice with ice cream and brandy snap for dessert,
The food was very tasty, cooked just right and just the right amount. Full compliments to the chef and the kitchen staff!
Throughout the meal the waiting staff were very attentive to our requests and needs, always there when we wanted them but never in your face or pestering us. This is how I like staff to be when I am out with my wife.
The room we had was very quiet and peaceful and we both had a very restful night.
Breakfast in the morning, was again very filling and lovely. It set me up for the day and the journey back home to Essex.
The hotel was very comfortable and clean. The staff very professional, attentive and courteous. All in all we had a fantastic weekend.
The hotel, staff and food allowed us to have a peaceful weekend and 'recharge our batteries'. This is what any hotel should be like!
Would we return to the hotel again?  We discussed this on the way home and yes - we will!
Thank you

Afternoon Tea. We were given a voucher as a gift for Afternoon Tea, not being afternoon Tea people we were not sure what to expect. What a lovely surprise it turned out to be. The Tea was very large and really good, as we could not manage all our cakes a member of staff kindly packed them for us to take home. Would recommend the Hickstead for great Tea and very comfortable surroundings

Tiptop Tea!. My husband and I were given a Red Letter Day voucher for Afternoon Tea as an anniversary present and chose Hickstead as the venue as it was the nearest place to our home. We had never been to the hotel before and were delighted to find the hotel just off the A23 set in the most delightful surroundings. A plethora of daffodils greeted us as we drove up the drive and parked the car in a car park overlooking the countryside without an inkling the major road to Brighton was just half a mile away.
We were greeted by a lovely receptionist who showed us to our table by the window overlooking the car park and grounds. From here we could see rabbits playing in the field and watch the horses playing in the field.
The waitress served us our tea and we were delighted with the selection of sandwiches provided and an excellent array of cakes for us to enjoy. All of the food had been made on the premises and it was superb. In particular, the presentation of the sandwiches and the scones were excellent. The staff ensured we had everything we needed and we had an anniversary to remember.
We have dined at some fine places including the Ritz, but we believe you will not get a better experience than Afternoon Tea at the Hickstead hotel. We will return!

Good value one night stay with friends. We had booked the Hickstead due to its close proximity to the Bolney Estate vineyard which we were visiting for a pre-booked tour.
Apart from a ridiculously expensive taxi from the vineyard to the hotel, our weekend stay was excellent and such good value for money.
Our stay included a 3 course dinner with wine, bed and breakfast for £109 for two people. 
Often these type of deals offer a limited set menu and lower grade room but at the Hickstead the choice was vast and the room was lovely. 
We had a brilliant weekend and thoroughly enjoyed being spoilt by the fab staff. The food was faultless and the candy floss served with my dessert was a real treat! 
Would highly recommend for a good value weekend break in the countryside

Business Breakfast Meeting . What a fantastic venue for a large business meeting. An amazing breakfast spread today (I will not eat for a week....) 
Plenty of parking, awesome views and friendly professional and helpful staff.  Highly recommend.  I'll be hosting all my meetings here in future.  Thank you

Afternoon tea . The hotel is in a lovely setting in the Sussex countryside with close access to the A23.
We went for afternoon tea which was a gift package. The food quality was excellent with freshly made delicious sandwiches, warm scones and cakes. Very friendly staff. The hotel is a little dated but the excellent food and friendly staff more than made up for this. Would recommend and would come for tea again

Wedding weekend . We have used this hotel for our wedding venue. Wedding preparation are stressful but the hotel and the team made the day and evening as painless as possible. They have accommodated all or request and changes ( many of them) and made it truly suited to our needs . The hotel location is ideal for commutes from Gatwick / london / Portsmouth where our families came from . The hotel facilities where perfect for our needs. Special thanks to the hotel team who  have truly help making this a memorable and very special day which will be in our memories for the rest of our life . I would recommend this hotel to anybody who wants to make their wedding day a success. the team could not have been more accommodating and welcoming to us and our families

Two ajoining rooms for a family - great value and lots of space. We booked adjoining rooms for a family party and have to say was delighted with the accomodation.  Rooms much bigger than "normal" family rooms and the benefit of two bathrooms was a real plus.  Service at reception was lovely and breakfast really good. Would come back for sure

Great place to stay. This is Great little hotel and a bargain at the price I paid. The hotel offered good amenities ie bar and separate restaurant. I had a ground floor room, the room was good size room and very clean.
Friendly staff who were happy to help. Good food, i just had the bar menu, the burger was a killer, and well worth the money

Quiet Hotel. A group of us stayed here for one night. The first impression is that you are iff the beaten track as you go down a ling drive to the hotel.  Then there is plenty of parking space clase to the hotel.  No lugging of suitcases from a distant car park. 
The welcone was odd as there was no one on reception but a call system brought a friendly face very quickly.
Checkin was easy and fast, followed by a drink in the bar with friends. Again friendly and more like a family lounge than a bar.
Thw evening meal was delicious and a lovely surprise for what I had taken as an 'average hotel'.
The room had everything needed and the breakfast was great. A Full English with all options including black pudding.
Highs & Lows: A very friendly and welcoming hotel but the bedroom was too hot.
I would recommend this hotel

Workmans hotel. Stayed here a few months ago, I would call this a workmans hotel as it was full of workmen in the bar in work clothes. I thought the rooms and the bar/lounge area in need of a good refurb, would not stay there again

A Pleasant Stay. Stayed here overnight as we were running Brighton marathon next day. Booked online a year ago. Requested a quiet room as had previous issues with loud guests in previous hotels before a big race (sleep is definitely needed)
Was pleased on arrival receptionist pointed out we had been allocated one of the quietest of the 52 rooms. We did not book dinner or breakfast here, went out locally for dinner and up too early and gone before breakfast.
