We did however use the bar and sit outside in the lovely sunshine on Saturday afternoon.
The room was comfortable, tidy and more than sufficient for a 1 night stop over, everything we needed. Soaps and bits provided in bathroom.  Bed was comfy, I slept well. If I was to say one negative thing it would be the bathroom. On my evening soak I looked more closely, someone else's stands of hair over tiles, and in the bath. The silicone around all the bath was quite mouldy, tiles a bit dated, tiling behind loo messy. Definitely bathroom needed some attention and a deeper daily clean, but on 1st glance and if I'd not laid in Bath probably wouldn't have noticed. 
Check in and out efficient, staff friendly. Would use again if in the area

Wedding venue. This hotel is quietly tucked away, but is great place for a wedding party. The staff are extremely helpful and are very accommodating. The wedding co-ordination and the manager were both very efficient with the wedding going off without a hitch.The venue itself was very pleasant, although i noticed that upstairs in the ceremony room a few of the windows double glazing had lost there seal so the view could not be appreciated. Also the curtains looked a bit shabby. I am sure that these issues must be on a refurb list of things to do, as the blown window in the reception room had been replaced.
The wedding breakfast was very tasty, and enjoyed by everyone, as was the breakfast the following morning

Exploring Sussex. The Hickstead is located in a nice part of Sussex close to the show ground Hickstead is known for.
It was not quite as expected for the status Hickstead has, but most things were without fault including the food and the staff.
A nice 3 night stay with good breakfasts and good food provided in the restaurant so overall it was a good stay.
We enjoyed listening to the presence of various birds and the geese wandering around the grounds

Very attentive staff. We stayed 28th April for a friend's wedding up the road. The staff were very friendly and welcoming both when we checked in and at midnight when we got back. The hotel was clean but quite dated. The water was on colder side of hot. 
Breakfast was good and fast and enough food. 
Overall for a stop over for an event it is perfect

Good hotel. We stayed for two nights with 3 course evening meals and breakfast Good hotel just a couple of niggles a little bit outdated but very clean and tidy staff very helpful and pleasant we had a down stairs room which was very hot we turned off the radiators but could not open the windows thy where sealed shut so room was a bit stuffy the room was nice though with bath and shower
when we had dinner I ask for a coke and was given a can and glass which was ok but when the coke is £1.95 a can and you see multipack can do not split on the can and your paying £1.95 for it I'm. No miser but you can buy 6 cans for that ! 
There's not a lot to do in the hotel apart from eat or drink maybe some light entertainment in the evening would be nice but apart from that not a bad hotel

Excellent staff and room service. I was on business and stayed one night in this hotel which lies in a very quiet rural location. I had failed to ask the hotel in advance for a ground floor room and did so when I arrived. Even though another room had been allocated, the staff could not have been more accommodating, helpful and pleasant and once they had checked that a ground floor room was ready, it was allocated.

I had a room service dinner which was excellent and arrived very speedily. I also had breakfast in my room which was delivered precisely on time. The accommodation is very good for the price and I enjoyed my stay enormously. There is a large amount of easily accessible car parking and free Wi-Fi which works well.

This is a very good hotel with the advantage of delightful staff which are much to the credit of the management. I would not hesitate to stay here again.

I recommend this hotel

Afternoon Tea. We had a gift voucher through Red Letter Days and decided to use it at The Hickstead. We were warmly greeted on arrival and shown to our table overlooking the grounds. The champagne tea was superb. The waiter was so attentive and really made the experience the success that it was. There was plenty of everything and can highly recommend. On departing we were also offered a 'doggy' bag but politely refused on the grounds of giving our waistbands a breather!

Surprisingly good. I hadn't expected much from this hotel as it had been booked as a deal by work.  I arrived slightly later than planned and was surprised to find that a three course meal was included with my accommodation.  It was even available via room service.

Comfortable bed, quiet location and good shower in the bathroom.

Staff were friendly and accommodating.  

Breakfast was good as was the evening meal in the restaurant the following evening. Free wifi as well

Excellent, would definatly stay again. Friendly staff, On site parking, very comfortable, and very clean, extra towels and pillows in Wardrobe, receptionist very helpful, Lakes to fish if you wanted to,  good rates, and excellent breakfast, only 10 mins from Brighton, would defiantly stay here on our next trip

Christening . Superb hotel. If this hotel was rated on the professionalism of the staff it would be 10 stars. All of the staff went beyond the call of duty to make our stay one of the most memorable weekend's of my life. From the manager to the cleaners. A special thanks Perri the bar man and also to the assistant manager keep up the good work guys not often you find a little gem like this and little did they know that the majority of the party have a hotel background and were unaware of the scrutiny they were under

Nice stop over. My husband and I stayed here for just one night whilst on a flying visit to the region.Our room was Clean,Modern and quiet (despite being on the ground floor).Breakfast was swiftly served in the morning with an abundance of options.Can recommend the Continental! The hotel is very well located for Quick and easy acces to the coast as well as Gatwick Airport

Wedding venue. From planning our wedding, the big day and the morning after made this a very smooth and happy event, not just for us, but also for our guests. We opted for their wedding package which included a taster evening, two rooms the night before, the wedding breakfast, with toastmaster, an evening reception, the bridal suite, and breakfast the next day. All the staff at the Hickstead excelled themselves, they knew exactly what to do and when to do it. The wedding breakfast turned out to be a fairly complicated menu which was delivered, beautifully cooked, to the correct guests. Without exception, the 50 guests thoroughly enjoyed the food and were full of praise of the staff, and our choice of venue. Overall, they made our "special day" very special

The only good thing here is the staff.... I had stayed in this hotel before (2014) and I was not looking forward to another stay. I was correct. This hotel is stuck in the 1980s, the rooms, the food (and some of the staff) have not changed for 30 years or so. Uncomfortable beds, small and very uncomfortable showers, no extra facilities such as plugs near your bed..wifi that hardly works (speeds that I last saw in the 1990s...), and the most miserable decor all got a tick.  But the staff smile and really try...which is why it gets two stars.  The food generally is just edible, although I did have the sea bream two days which was ok (the fish and chips was just passabl, but the tuna was really horrible). Bring earplugs if you want to sleep as it is really noisy, but better still, find somewhere else

Wedding stop over.. Stayed here after my sons wedding.  ( at hickstead showground ). Was quite disappointed on arrival,  check in was very small and not very welcoming. ( Although it was  midnight ). The guy gave us our key and pointed us in a direction of doors. The room was ok with tea/coffee making facilities.  Plenty of room. But the bed was really hard. Bathroom needs a bit of a makeover. They did keep the bar open for a while but was asked to keep the noise down. So after a poor nights sleep it was off to breakfast.  Very good selection of bacon,  eggs, beans etc. Even if you didn't want scrambled egg they would do eggs whatever way you wanted. Cereal limited as was the fruit. Tea already made in thermos tea pot. After a few minutes became stewed.  Toast already on table so it was cold. Was it value for money at £102. No

Superb. . My family and I spent a fantastic night at The Hickstead after enjoying Brighton beach recently. 

The hotel is in an odd area, kind of in the middle of nowhere, but that appealed to me. It means that you'll not be bothered by passers by. It's set in nice grounds, it's pretty, quaint, and only a short drive into Brighton centre. 

The staff are phenomenal, very welcoming and accommodating, even in the middle of the night. 

We only ate breakfast at the hotel, but it was very good quality. I think there could have been a slightly bigger selection of cereal and pastries, but I have no complaints at all. It was busy and sometimes a few items ran out but once staff were aware it was replaced very quickly. 

I will definitely be back soon

Not acceptable !!!. I have had issues with this hotel on recent stays but last nights topped it all. I arrived late and checked in and when I got to my room the problems are as follows :
The door wouldn't close properly, a rancid smell hits you immediately, it was an adjoining room which was occupied would not lock . There was a large ,still wet stain on carpet next to bed. I refused to stay in it so was given a " off status " room but bathroom was unusable and they suggested I slept in there and used bathroom in the previous smelly room. 
I absolutely refuse to stay here again

Not bad for the fee. Stayed in June on business, friendly professional staff, and for the £85 fee the room was adequate at a 3 star, and food good.  Nice quiet location as well.

Only one slightly negative point to note, the downstairs corridor leading to rooms smelt really bad, like a urinal.  Seriously in need of some ventilation and/or air fresheners.  I was worried the room at the end of the corridor (112) was also going to smell but it was fine

Afternoon tea. Went to The Hickstead for an afternoon tea experience - lots of yummy cake and sandwiches! Staff were lovely, especially when we couldn't eat all the cake and made up a doggy bag for us. We weren't ready to leave after our tea so we took our drinks to the lounge area and played scrabble for a while. The staff we quite happy for us to stay even though we weren't guests in the hotel. Overall a lovely experience and would defifintely go there again

Friendly staff and a wonderful breakfast make the difference. 15 (ish) minutes from Brighton The Hickstead is a great place to stay if you don't want the hassle of car unfriendly Brighton. The hotel boasts ample parking and very friendly and welcoming staff which makes up for the fact that the internal decor is due an overhaul and is looking a tad sad. The rooms are good but soundproofing is poor and if you are a light sleeper be prepared. The staff make a difference and the breakfast is one of the best I've had you don't make your own toast and drinks are not from a machine and its all cooked fresh. Breakfast is a must if you stay here

Don't waste your money. Paid £100 to stay one night here and not worth the money at all. Rooms are joined together by a door where you can then hear people talking next door...nice bit of privacy! Broken window so unable to open it. Get moved to an exact same room before finally getting moved to a "normal" room. This was very hot in here, luckily the window opened!
Nice location but a lot of money for what you get

Lovely Afternoon Tea. We arrived to a warm welcome and were seated immediately. The staff were very friendly and attentive throughout out visit, the sandwiches were freshly prepared and the cakes were homemade and delicious. We relaxed as we enjoyed our tea in the lovely surroundings and atmosphere. Altogether a lovely experience

Mr and Mrs White. We had our wedding here last weekend 8th July and I would highly recommend it. The venue and setting is just beautiful.
The staff are professional and nothing was too much trouble . We had an unexpected wedding guest attend and with only a couple of hours notice the manager had organised another table setting and she was served a three course vegan meal.
The food was delicious and plenty of it and the breakfast room was set up magnificently.
Many of our guests stayed the entire weekend with us and commented it was the "Best wedding ever!!!" We were asked why we chose a venue so far away and I would say it was worth it, it exceeded all of our expectations. My daughter was our bridesmaid and said when she marries she wants it there too!
I can honestly say I would not have changed a thing. We were guided through the whole process from the minute we were shown around the place last year and I'm so pleased we went with our instinct to book there and then.
A huge thank you to the manager Giles Paul and his entire team who created a magical wedding weekend for us !!

Nice afternoon out. We were given as a present an afternoon tea The staff were very friendly and welcoming 
The tea they served was first class and very nicely done and we even were given a doggy bag to take home what we hadn't  eaten so nothing went to waste

Afternoon Tea. Great experience, we took our grand daughter out for a cream tea at the Hotel.  It had been a gift to us from our children...  The cream tea was lovely and the staff served hot chocolate and jam sandwiches for our 5 year old, that was what she asked for.  I was quite prepared to pay for her drink and sandwich but that was waived away by the staff, a real kindness I thought.  Beautiful part of Sussex, lovely Hotel and wonderful staff.  Highly recommend the Hotel and staff

Afternoon tea gluten free?!. Disappointing!  3 of us went for afternoon tea for my daughter's birthday. I am gluten free  and so was pleased I could be accommodated. Booked and  Ordered in advance by phone. On arrival we were offered our tea( drink) when we asked what types were available we were begrudgingly brought the choices!  Which were extensive! . My gluten free tea came separately which was nice, but no scones as the others had! On asking I was told they didn't do glutenfree scones? So felt a bit cheated! Started on the sandwiches which where tasty until I got to the ham and English mustard. Any gluten free person would be sceptical about this condiment. When I asked and it was checked indeed my thoughts where correct! Mustard not gluten free!!  Having said all the above, remaining sandwiches nice, cakes delicious, but did worry a bit, but I've had no reprecussions so must assume rest of food was ok! And the issue was compensated!!

Ideal for overnight stay. I have used this hotel regular since 2013 for an overnight stay on business trips.
Ideal quiet location for a good nights sleep.
Rooms clean comfortable although in in need of refurbishment.
Staff polite friendly and very helpful

Pride Weekend. My partner and I have stayed at this hotel many times before during the past 6 years. We stayed for 2 nights last weekend and pleased to say that the hotel is up to its usual standard.  We love it here as its an almost countryside location, but less than 20 minutes to Brighton by car or taxi.  Comfortable rooms with all facilities and very pleased to see the return of the buffet breakfast again! We dined in the Oaktree Bistro on one of the evenings and again enjoyed great food.  Staff all lovely too.  Thanks to all at The Hickstead - we will be back again soon

Great afternoon tea. Afternoon teas can be a bit boring but this one had a very good variety of cakes to accompany the sandwiches and scones which were very tasty and fresh. Refills of tea and coffee no trouble. Doggy bag to take home the things we could not manage to eat

One night stay. Quite location, room clean with some toiletries products provided. Kettle with tea and coffee only one pack of biscuits for two people. Staff .The breakfast buffet was ample choice but as with most buffet the food was not piping hot

Two night stay , whilst attending a Wedding.. Stayed at the Hotel for two nights whilst attending a Wedding locally .The Room was clean and tidy,breakfast was good and staff very attentive , pleasant and helpful. Convenient for M23 to Brighton or to Gatwick

Extremely disappointing . Stayed here for one night as a couple. The hotel room was dirty. Dirty bath room, grout and silicone round the bath was mouldy and shower curtain yellow stained. Net curtain in the room again yellow stained. Carpet didn't look like it had seen a Hoover in a while and holes in the plaster board. We ate in the hotel restaurant. Decor is dated however staff very polite. The food was extremely overpriced for the quality. We had calamari which has so flavour. Like eating elastic bands. Steak was ordered medium but came well done. Lacked flavour and very chewy. Bed was uncomfortable to sleep on, need replacing. It was hot so we had the window open and were woken several times through the night by roudy guests through the night which the hotel so nothing about. Was then woken again by a member of staff watering plants with a hose at half 3 in the morning right outside our room which is ridiculous! 
Don't waste your money, for the amount we played we could have just stayed in the middle of Brighton!

Regular stays. Along with a number of work's colleagues we regularly stay at The Hickstead Hotel. The hotel is ideally located for our works and set in a decent grounds allowing you to enjoy the evening outside should you so wish.   

We have always been made welcome, and none of us have had cause to complain. 

The rooms are set over two floors, the first rooms facing over the car park and fields have the better views, but all are always clean and tidy. 

During a couple of the extremely hot nights, even with the windows open (how far is restricted by the security cable) it did get quite hot in the rooms, air conditioning would have been nice. 

We all have never been short of hot water for showers and baths, regardless of the time of day. 

As we are staying are often staying for a number of nights it would be good to have a small fridge

'Dog Friendly' not so Friendly. I booked this hotel on line as my wife and I were performing at Hickstead Showgrounds over a two day weekend period, and we would have our small Lhasa Apso dog, Max, with us. We have no qualms taking him anywhere because he is well-behaved and quiet and we love to have our pet with us. The Hotel web site stated it was dog friendly, but this turned out to not be the whole truth.  I had booked three nights B&B plus two evening meals. On arrival, the Hotel was impressive and the staff polite and cordial.  However, we were advised that our dog could not be with us when we ate, even in the Bar area (we didn't expect to be able to take him into the restaurant, but we are quite happy to eat in the bar).  This also meant unless we sat outside with him, we couldn't relax and socialise in the bar in the evening either, only in our room.  Their compromise was that we could have our meals delivered in our room, but at an additional cost of £2.50 'room service' each per meal. This is not 'dog friendly' as far as we are concerned.  We did have our breakfasts delivered to our room, but I cancelled our evening meals and we found several pubs in the area who were happy to have our custom with Max with us when we ate.
Our room was quite large and looked out over the large car park, but there was no air conditioning - and it turned out to be a rather warm weekend, so we had to make do with opening the large window, which, for security reasons was unable to be opened beyond a few inches. Bath, shower and toilet were very clean, and the towels changed daily. We liked the hotel and the staff, but feel they should amend their publicity regarding their dog policy, so people know in advance that their dog will not be allowed in the bar regardless of whether one chooses to eat or not. Had we known, we would probably have booked elsewhere

One Nighter. We found all the staff to be friendly and welcoming and the service we received was excellent. The evening meal was superb. Plenty of choice and extremely well cooked and presented. It's a shame that the quality of the bedroom is not up to the same standard. Our room felt dated and the bed extremely hard. The bathroom was tired as well but all the water pressure etc was fine. By the time you have tucked into the great cooked breakfast, you can forgive the room

Bed and Tea. I arrived at 9pm to a very full car park, apparently driver awareness course being done in the conference room which made me giggle as I'd not been able to find a decent space to park as loads had parked on the wonk over 2 spaces. 

Just time for a cup of tea, brewed in a pot cause it was there, along with some terrible oat biscuit, before sleep. 

Bed comfy, alright pillows, good shower and then I was gone.

Overpriced with uncomfortable beds and cold water. Stayed overnight for a friends wedding. The double bed was more akin to sleeping on a concrete slab it was so hard and uncomfortable. In the morning the bath water was tepid and the shower was freezing and difficult to operate with a strange red button which you appear to have had to press in and hold to generate any type of warmth, pretty pathetic really. £102 for this standard of room is a rip off. However staff did come across as quite friendly and the wedding was well organised and everyone enjoyed themselves. Just a shame about the room

Nice hotel. We attended our friends wedding this weekend and were very happy with the hotel. The staff were very nice and the food was lovely! Our only small issue was that in a room for 3 people there were only 2 cups and saucers, 2 sets of towels and 2 spare pillows. For one night it was ok. The shower was hot enough and easy to operate. All in all, no complaints, and we would stay there again

Did the job. This hotel was OK did the job wanted to spend a few days in Brighton before flying from Gatwick. The lady that checked us in was not over friendly and she did not think much to the views which was strange it was in a lovely setting down a drive well away from the road so peaceful. The menu for the bar was  not bad price but a drink in the bar was expensive.
The shower did not work and I had to go to another room to take a shower, they did  fix it while we were out. The booking included afternoon tea which was great really enjoyed afternoon tea there was plenty to eat. The grounds were very nice, and they give us a packed breakfast as we left before the restaurant opened for our flight. I would stay here again

Nice place. Spent here one night when came to West Sussex for a 2-day training. Had a ground floor room overlooking car park. It was clean and cozy - that's what you need to rest after all day in meeting room. :)
Small bar with pilsner (staropramen), ale (?) and Guinness (can). Some spirits as well
Food - great tasting mushroom and sherry soup, decent  portion of fish and chips however not fully satisfied with the pudding. I have asked for poached pear in red wine sauce. Pear was little bit too tender - maybe needed few extra minutes in the pan or should've used more ripe pear? On the other hand - my colleagues got very nice golden syrup sponge with custard (instead of ice cream) and they loved it :)
Generally nice hotel to spend a night. Staff was very friendly and helpful. Would use it again :)

So much better than expected.. I was looking for a room near to Brighton as our son was seriously ill in the Royal Sussex Hospital. I very nearly did not book this hotel based on some of the reviews. I’m glad I did.

The staff could not have been more helpful. They even helped me locate the local Tesco store to purchase items to take to the hospital. Despite a wedding they made us feel we were important.

Room 219 was clean & spacious. It had a view of the car park, which is better than it sounds!!

The room is classic and may appear dated to some, but we found it had everything we needed. It was well positioned away from the wedding party, giving us some much needed sleep after a 6+ hour drive.

Food was good, drinks were cold. Both we felt were reasonable (not that we were really tasting anything).

Breakfast was self service, plenty of choice in a nice restaurant area.

If I had to make some constructive criticisms they would be-

Double bed felt a little small (I guess most people have a King/Superking these days).
Toilet in room 219 took quite a while to fill once flushed.
We did feel as if we were intruding on the wedding party as we eat in the small bar area that evening, but did not want a restaurant meal.

But your staff on reception, in the bar and serving our meals made up for all that. I would stay again, and might indeed do so very soon

Good location with lovely staff. We stayed one night and it was very good, dinner, bed and breakfast special rate which was great value.  The reception staff and greeting we received when we arrived was exceptional. 
Nothing was too much trouble, and dog friendly too

Ok if you don’t want anything special. The positive is the location is great, just off the M23, close to Gatwick, close to Brighton and good location to travel around the area.

For me the hotel was very lacking in other areas considering the cost:
- Very hard mattress and hard pillows meant I hardly slept at all. If you like an old springy mattress you’re ok
- the bathroom, whilst clean, was again tired and in need of re-vamp
- General decor is tired and old needing a good facelift throughout 
- There seems to be a lingering smell around reception and the small bar area that was very off putting 

On a plus note, the breakfast (food and service) was very good - lacking a bit maybe in atmosphere. 

Wouldn’t stay here again, would book into the Horsham Travelodge or Premier Inn.

Afternoon tea. Went for afternoon tea with a friend on Monday 6th November. My 2nd visit but there won't be a 3rd! Both my friend and were very disappointed.  She likes Earl Grey tea which they had, I like Assam or Darjeeling which they didn't have, they could only offer fruit teas or English Breakfast!  For afternoon tea!  And it was all tea bags!  The dining room lacks any ambiance and one wilting flower detracted even further.  The milk was already on the table, which I thought unnecessary, milk should be brought with the tea. The plate of sandwiches was a strange number, 3 sets of 3, for 2 people if you divide equally you have to cut one in half.  The cakes were nice but the lemon drizzle was very dry. The scones were the best part, they were lovely.  The staff were friendly and polite but I feel that they are fighting a losing battle. Frankly I wouldn't like my wake to be held let alone a wedding! All in all a very disappointing afternoon.  They should take a look at the Ashdown Park Hotel, afternoon tea there is wonderful

Lovely stay with friendly and professional staff. Absolutely lovely hotel, the staff were very professional and kind. 
Slept really well and ate a good breakfast. Would definitively recommend this place. 
Location is lovely, makes for a really relaxing and enjoyable stay. 
Marie, Haywards Heath

Stayed Here Many Times - Not Exciting. Wish I could get excited. Rooms are perfectly good. Location is brilliant for A23. Just feel it lacks atmosphere. The bar feels more like a hall than a pub lounge. The beer is poor in selection. Not had a memorable night here

Awful hotel . I can’t believe the reviews for this hotel. It has to be one of the worse places I have ever stayed. 
The rooms are large but the beds are so uncomfortable. The walls are paper thin and you can hear everything the person in the room next door is doing. Along with all of their conversation.
Breakfast is very basic and the pastry were stale. 
I will never be staying here again and cannot understand how is Has the reviews it does.

Great value stay. Second time we've stayed here on the classic lodges package. Good room and a free upgrade (fruit bowl, newspapers and slippers) as I have their privilege card, substantial afternoon tea with good pastries. Inclusive dinner was Pub style grub (fish and chips, burgers, steak etc) but deserts were beautifully presented. Breakfast buffet was adequate and there was a good choice of cooked options. Mine, smoked haddock and poached eggs was a mixed bag; fish very badly dried out but eggs were perfect and wouldn't be out of place on Masterchef professionals. All this said it was a good value package and we probably would use the hotel again

Afternoon tea. Visited for afternoon Christmas tea. Excellent with a good choice of sandwiches and cakes together with attentive service in elegant surroundings and lovely views. Great value, particularly as I also used my Yeo Valley Yeokens to get two for one

Family Christmas gathering. I would like to thank all the very friendly helpful team working on the 28th Dec 2017. We really enjoyed spending our family Christmas with you all, many thanks for everything. Lovely welcome, hot dinner and excellent organisation from the hotel. Many thanks again and happy new year to all of you

Pre-wedding stay. We stayed here prior to and just after a nephew's wedding in Brighton.

We cannot find fault in the manner in which the team run the business. The reception personnel were extremely helpful and courteous, providing us with a room which was not within earshot of a wedding function that night. The manner in which Kevin conducts proceedings at breakfast in the restaurant warrants special mention. The food was spot on, tasty and prepared to order.

We note that  a few negative comments have been left by folk. Hmmm... they are obviously welcome to share their views. However, if abject  luxury is what they are looking for, then for heaven's sake, search for luxury hotels in the first place, dig deeper in their pockets and pay luxury money.

For our family members, all was well. Thank you for looking after us

Ammazing, relaxing and truely perfect.. First time here for new years eve, stayed here on three previous occasions.
What can we say about this Hotel. From the moment we got there till the time we left, the staff were were some of the best we ever had, nothing was to much trouble and considering the number of people staying that was a credit to the Manager and his team.
The food for New years eve was as always FIRST CLASS especially the number catered for.
We will be back during the year, also returning for New Year.
Thank you all for a very special night

Birthday Party. Check in was easy and fast. Our room on first floor was large, clean and very comfortable. Free wi-Fi was easy to use in room, and flat screen TVs had good choice of stations. Tea and coffee provided with extra bonus of trouser press.comfortable beds to sleep in and bathroom had all usual extras, shower gel etc.
Attended a Birthday Party in the Function Room, which was large and spacious. Only downside was there was no dance floor, and we had to dance to the band on carpet. Bar was set up on a table which was not a problem and prices are reasonable for a hotel.

Breakfast was a highlight with a great selection of hot and cold food buffet style. Four choices of fruit juices and everything was fresh. Tea and coffee on request

Needs updating. The staff were all very friendly but the bed mattress was really hard and uncomfortable and the bathroom was definitely in need of an update - both from a decorative and practical point of view.  The toilet took about an hour to refill so that it could be flushed again, and the handle looked and felt like it would break any minute.  Afternoon tea and breakfast were both good

Afternoon Tea. The waitresses and reception staff were lovely. All except for the chef who you could hear constantly moaning in the kitchen " they want it on brown bread!?! You said they wanted it on white!" needless to say made us feel very uncomfortable.
 The highchair was filthy, waitress was happy to clean it and did apologise. Also the cup my daughter was given was dirty. The table cloth on the table looked clean, although napkins smelt of smoke.
Afternoon tea was nice enough, although you do get slices of cake rather than individual ones. Waitress was happy to fill up my tea pot. Also there was pop music playing, would have preferred something a little more relaxing, but that is just my opinion

Brilliant wedding venue and top quality staff. From the moment we first viewed The Hickstead the staff were brilliant. Through the whole planning phases to the actual wedding day, the staff were friendly, helpful and accommodating. All the guests enjoyed the food and shared our views about the staff and venue. The numerous stresses of planning a wedding were definable made easier  with the help of Kellie and all the other staff members

Lovely hotel in a quiet area but near to plenty!. We stayed here for a two night break, visiting Brighton and Arundel. The hotel was easy to find, had ample parking and was pleasant on approach. The hotel was clean and comfortable, staff friendly and our room spacious and more than adequate. Nice toiletries and plenty of tea and coffee available, as well as some biscuits!! Bed was comfortable and we had two good nights sleep. There was a wedding on the Friday night, although we didn't hear any noise from this and even this morning, the wedding party was kept separate from the other guests. Only grumble would be the, although speedy, English breakfast, which for our taste was rather undercooked (prefer a little colour on both bacon and sausage and tomatoes were not softened at all). Saying this, we would stay again. Have posted reviews on sea life centre, Brighton pavilion, cafe Rocco and the Brighton museum (Brighton appx 15min drive, reccomend the park n ride) and Arundel castle (appx 45 min drive)

Awful. I review hotels frequently and don’t like to give bad reviews but I am always very honest and unfortunately this hotel was pretty dreadful! I cannot believe the good reviews and ratings it has....they sound like a different hotel! When you arrive at the hotel it looks nice from the outside and is off the main road so in a quiet place. When we arrived there was a wedding ongoing. We checked in and found the lady on reception to be very unwelcoming and unfriendly. We thought she must have been busy or stressed but she was still like that later on. Our room was average, it was clean however the net at the window had rust staining along the bottom. The bathroom tiles had been broken and looked like they had just been put back on with blu tack! The rooms were not sound proof at all. In the morning we awoke to the sound of someone having a wee and breaking wind in the room above us!! We went out to nearby Burgess Hill for the evening and returned around 11pm. We ordered sandwiches from the 24hr menu to be told that that wasn’t actually 24hrs as there was no chef!! After complaining we were eventually given sandwiches. The one positive thing about the hotel was the lady serving at breakfast - she was very friendly and talkative. Unfortunately the breakfast itself wasn’t great and one of my friends was served a very under cooked poached egg! All in all we won’t be returning - we paid £137 for a night and I don’t think it was worth even half of that unfortunately

Rather unloved. Stayed March 2018, one night. £90 room only

When you first approach this property, you have this feeling that you're arriving somewhere really rather special. The entrance and driveway are almost grand and beautifully laid out and then you reach the front door to discover everything else is a bit of a let-down.

I'm going to overlook the fact that the hotel separately tried to overcharge me, first for the pre-paid room on arrival and then for dinner the next day. Perhaps these things happen and they did remedy the bill without fuss.

What surprises me is that the published photos do seem to be quite genuine and yet do so little to convey the tired state of this place. They claim the rooms are decorated in a contemporary style but the brochure must have been written c. 1975. And there seems to have been very little updating or maintenance since. Therefore, and especially given other local hotels in which I've stayed for quite a bit less (and including breakfast,) I thought the accommodation really poor value overall. Worn and dirty carpeting, mouldy shower seals and decaying furniture are just not acceptable.

Not wishing to be totally negative, it is certainly quiet which is a blessing and I did sleep well. I also can't fault the breakfast, that was a very pleasant surprise - plentiful and well-priced (on its own.)

If the price point were rather lower, I suppose I'd consider staying here again. But with so many more appealing and cheaper options locally, I doubt it

A lovely setting and a warm welcome. . We were in the area as my wife was on a business course nearby.

The Hickstead was a good choice for a one night stay.

Set in a quiet rural area with plenty of free on site parking.

Spacious, well appointed room, which was Spotlessly clean. So a big thumb's up to the housekeeping staff for that.

Friendly staff at check in which was without delay.

We ordered evening meals from the room service menu and were very impressed with the food quality, quantity and the speed of service.  All faultless.

In the morning, breakfast was a relaxed affair with a buffet of hot cooked items and a range of juices and continental pastries, cereals etc.

Check out was set for 11am but as I wasn't feeling well I asked for a later checkout and 1 o'clock was agreed.  This was done with no extra charge, so a big thanks for that.

We will be back in the area in a few weeks time for another course for my wife and would not hesitate in choosing The Hickstead again.

The picture is an interior view of our room but the hotel does present itself well as you approach up the drive.

A good choice.  We'll be back

Lovely setting but in need of update. Firstly I have to say the hotel setting is lovely. Peacefully set  in the countryside with great surroundings. I don’t like to be negative but feel I have to comment. Firstly reception staff should be warm and welcoming. Didn’t feel this at all. 
The bathroom in our room smelt of urine , I went to mention it at reception but unfortunately another room wasn’t available , however we were offered a discount off our evening meal. I was surprised the lady in reception didn’t come to the room to smell for herself, it was as if she already knew!!
If you are vegetarian the menu for dinner only offers one dish. I’m afraid to say this tasted like a ready meal! The wine list could also do with updating. 
The staff at breakfast were lovely but appeared rather stressed. 
This hotel would be lovely if it were to be updated and customer care was a priority. The setting is fantastic. However I don’t feel enough is being done to maximise would could be a first class hotel

Sad demise of a "Classic".. We have stayed at the Hickstead two or three times a year for the last 5 years and have, unhappily, seen it go down and down.  It has never been top notch but it has so much potential that we just kept hoping that the owners would decide to invest in this property.  Clearly this isn't about to happen and it's unlikely we'll stay there again.

The hotel could be really lovely and to see the neglect is very sad.  The location is fabulous, the staff have always been excellent and very professional although, sadly, many have now left or are leaving.  The restaurant is, still, surprisingly good for an out of town 3* hotel.

However, the rooms!  They are in urgent need of renovation, especially the bathrooms where black mould is now the norm.  That said, they are clean, just very scruffy and unloved.

Yes, they apparently do a good trade in conferences etc. and this must be how they stay afloat but the hotel guests seem to be incidental and, allowing the bedrooms to become so run down, just shows disrespect for Classic Lodge's loyal clients, like us.

I'd love to read a glowing review this time next year to the effect that the place has been smartened up and looking great

Worth a vist. Stayed at The Hickstead for 4nights last week and the staff were very helpful and friendly without being overbearing. The food at dinner and breakfast was very good . The afternoon tea which was part of the deal was very well presented and was very acceptable.We have stayed at several Classic Lodges hotels and found they all offer value for money

A very disappointing experience. We have stayed before and personally I wasn't overly impressed then but it was passable, we booked again because it was convenient for what we wanted.

On this occasion we had a ground floor room but when I opened the curtains!!!! (I will attach a photo)   The room above us had creaky floorboard which meant I was awake at 7am - not too early but I was on holiday.  In the evening/night the walking around was at 1am.

I would have complained and ask to move but my husband wasn't bothered by it all and could sleep through floorboard issue.

We stayed 4 nights and during the whole time not one person asked us how the room was, or the food, nothing or I would have complained then (maybe they have learnt by experience not to ask)

I didn't recognise any of the staff from 9 months ago who were more pleasant and very helpful.

The atmosphere in the dining room for our evening meal was non existant and the service very slow, so we did not eat there again.

On the plus side the bed was comfortable

Mistake. We booked this at short notice as we needed to be in Brighton.
The lady who checked us in was very surly and we didn't feel very welcome. 
The room was OK. Dated but clean. The bed was a disappointment. Very uncomfortable.
NO AIR CONDITIONING. This was the biggest  problem as it was 30 degrees. We were given a small fan, but it too had seen better days and was very noisy. 
We went out for dinner and arrived back around 9pm. We walked through the front door towards Reception and a different lady was manning it. She never even glanced up and we didn't  the get the friendly 'good evening' which we have come to expect from the usual hotels we stay in. 
Sleeping was very difficult due to the heat and the bed.

Breakfast seemed rather chaotic. Tables were waiting to be cleared but the young girl who was serving  was run off her feet. 
The food was OK. Nothing stood out as warranting a mention. 
An elderly couple  came in who appeared to have been staying a week or so, and she demanded the restaurant supervisor sit down with her while she slated almost every meal she had eaten.  She was demanding a refund on her bill. 
At this point,  We were feeling very uncomfortable so got up and left. 
I'm afraid we wouldn't  return, though the hotel is In a  lovely  setting

Quality and Convenience. My wife and I stayed at The Hickstead on a Saturday night - just B&B at the hotel, with family parties in Burgess Hill on Saturday night and Sunday lunchtime, to celebrate a landmark birthday for my brother.
For a 3 star hotel in its price bracket, we thought The Hickstead was excellent. The staff were helpful, friendly and efficient, breakfast on Sunday morning was fine, and our room was well furnished and fitted, and very comfortable.
We had asked for a quiet room, and the room we got on the ground floor at the back was very peaceful. And although there was a wedding reception in the hotel which included music and dancing in the evening, we heard absolutely nothing of it after we turned in.
I rarely enter a comment on Tripadvisor, but I confess that when I booked the hotel I was a bit concerned at a couple of other customers recent comments, and having been very happy with our stay I felt I should share our very positive experience!

Great stop over. We stay here when we visit family in Brighton, as not a million miles, huge car park with plenty of space, rooms are nice clean and have every thing, breakfast great and staff very pleasant, Very quiet, and there is a bar if you wish to have a drink

Not bad. Food was nice and service was quick. Was left waiting at the bar for drinks a few times with nobody in sight. hotel room looked fine. The Bed was so uncomfortable quite possibly the worst night sleep I have ever had and the pillows were uncomfortable. Woke up with a bad back and the room was boiling. Friends who also stayed in another room had the same issues as us. Would not stay here again which is a shame as it is in a nice location and has good points but the bad points outweighs those

Could do better. we had afternoon tea at the Hickstead.  Sandwiches, champagne, scones and bite size desserts were all bought to the table at the same time.  After that we didnt see one single member of staff - no one came to see if all was well.  There was only one small tea plate each for both sandwiches and cake.  I didnt fancy putting scone or dessert on my plate which had mayonnaise and sandwich crumbs on it so took a plate from the neighbouring table.  There was no napkin.  The tea on offer was Tetley! no offer of any other more up market or speciality tea.  I would not go back and was glad that we had not paid for this delight - we had been given a voucher

Nice family do. Really friendly staff for our recent family party. They were helpful but unobtrusive. Hotel could really do with a spruce up and our bed mattress was old and tired. The pillows left quite a lot to be desired as well. Good location very near A23

Special tea excursion. We were given their deluxe tea as a present. The tea, we had earl grey, was slightly unusual but very good
 
The selection of sandwiches, cakes and fancies were excellent. 

We had a really lovely time there. The staff were very friendly as well

Afternoon Tea. My partner and I were given an afternoon tea as a present and thoughroly enjoyed our visit. There was a very good choice of sandwiches and they were fresh with good fillings, cakes and scones also very good. The restaurant was light and airy and the girls on duty were lovely and couldn't do enough for us - a very friendly greeting at reception always helps and we weren't disappointed. Many thanks

Great location, service & food shame about the rest. I was given a Red Letter Day Tea for Two and thought this looked a great place. We arrived and thought it in a very lovely location, easy to park the car. Entering there was no one at reception, we walked around and found a dining room (looks as if it is in a time warp - as does the rest of the hotel we walked through - 70s shades of brown!!), the female member of staff could not have been more welcoming as she showed us to our table, for a hotel in such a great location it is a shame that all the windows of this room look onto the car park.

The tea was lovely, the sandwiches were made to our requirements (we were both impressed by this), everything was tasty and could not be faulted. Well done to the kitchen team for such a lovely tea. The room as said is very 70s and lacks any sort of atmosphere but, with such a sad view from the windows they would be hard put to give the place a good feel - I have ideas if they are interested.

When we left the lovely female staff member (the only member of staff we saw!!) was  on reception, she is a great asset and a credit to this establishment GIVE HER A PAY RISE this place needs her more than she needs it.

Location GREAT
Service GREAT
Food GREAT
Decor POOR
View POOR

This visit was saved by the service and food, thank you

So disappointed . Doors slamming constantly, room smells of damp, receptionist wasn’t available for 25mins which left us standing around waiting not knowing whats going on. Other guests are loud and staff don’t care. Upsetting and ruined our night

Highly recommend. A lovely venue for a wedding. Staff were all very helpful and friendly. The food was great and I am going to book an afternoon tea for my next visit as they look fantastic.
Special thanks to Andy, Imogen, Holly and Emily you were amazing

Would stay again. We visited for a wedding the room was good as a family room we had 2 interconnected rooms it was perfect. The food was fantastic and the staff couldn’t do enough to help at all given opportunities. We were very pleased with our stay

Booked an afternoon tea with a voucher from Yeo Valley.. Booked an afternoon tea with a voucher from Yeo Valley. As per their menu, we chose 2 types of sandwiches each (Coronation Chicken, Roast Beef with Horseradish, Cheese with a Red Onion Chutney and Egg Mayo with Chives). We have also been served Fruit Scones, Mini Pear Tarts, Strawberry Cheesecake, Summer Berry Meringue and Sherry Trifle. Our initial reaction was OK, they were looking just OK. Once we started eating and we realised that something was not quite right therefore we have left most of the sandwiches and the Strawberry Cheesecake on the table. As the time was passing by our gut feeling was telling us to stop as something was not right. Luckily we did stop. As soon after we left their premises our stomachs were starting to react. We end up with very disturbed stomach problems for the whole afternoon and evening.  Do AVOID THIS PLACE FOR AFTERNOON TEA unless you want stomach problems

Could do better. Stayed for a couple of nights as friends were having their wedding here. Car park was very spacious and outside of hotel looked today. Booking in at reception was swift with pleasant staff. Bedroom was reasonably well equipped but looking a little tired. Carpet was stained and a bit worn. Bathroom wasn't too good with a terrible shower curtain, main mirror with bits missing and the bottom corner of the bathroom door rotted away. Shower was good pressure and temperature. Bed was comfortable but pillows were very uncomfortable. During the evenings in the bar area there was a distinct lack of staff and we had to go looking for someone nearly every time we wanted a drink. Very disappointed at cleanliness of bar area with noticeable amounts of dust on glass shelves and displays. 
Dining area was pleasant and food was generally of a good standard. Breakfast one day was cooked to order which I prefer and quality of produce used was very good. Wedding ran very smoothly with good food which was commented on by most guests. To round it up. Good and bad points. Need to spruce things up especially in bedrooms and hallways and pay a bit more attention to cleanliness and employ more staff (those staff that are there were very friendly and professional) An average 3 star

Comfortable, Clean and Freindly. Booked in to attend a friends wedding,  stayed a few nights longer.  The rooms was comfortable and clean,  staff were very friendly and attentive.   A little noise from ongoing events and people moving about on the floor above/ door slamming but no more than you would get living in a family home or flat! All in all an extremely pleasant stay!

A truly quintessential experience. To celebrate "Fathers Day", my daughter presented me with an E voucher through 'buyagift' to experience "Afternoon tea for two at Classic Lodges Hotels UK wide". This meant we could chose any one of a number of hotels within that Chain across the country to enjoy this "experience".

We chose to visit the Hickstead Hotel, West Sussex.It was a simple matter of calling up the hotel and arranging a date and time.

The hotel itself is just off the A23 at Jobs Lane, Bolney, West Sussex and the sat nav took us to the front entrance.

As we drove through the impressive entrance and down the lengthy driveway, we were surrounded by countryside. First  sightings of the hotel itself presented a sense of grandeur and spaciousness.There is ample parking and the reception area is but a few steps from the car park.

Upon entering the hotel we were immediately met by a delightful receptionist who instantly made us feel very welcome.Having confirmed the boking, we were then handed over to one of the waiters who looked after us for the whole visit. He was both pleasant, very smart, attentive and inter reacted extremely well.

The menu for the actual "tea" was extensive with  a good selection of different sandwiches and fillings. Having made our decision as to what we would have in terms of sandwiches,we were given our pots of tea. Before getting through half the first cup, the  food arrived, beautifully presented. It was very apparent, all was freshly made and looked delicious!

We then settled down to a lovely relaxed afternoon enjoying the fayre. The sandwiches contained ample filling  and tasted divine. Accompanying the sandwiches were a large selection of assorted  dainty cakes and  a banoffee sweet, set in a shot glass. Exquisite!

Despite our best efforts, we were unable to consume all the goodies placed before us whilst at the table. When requested, our waiter  duly removed the remaining sandwiches and cakes and presented them to us in a purpose made box to take with us. They were subsequently enjoyed later at home with our friends.

The hotel itself has some 52 bedrooms and  suites  and being only  20 minutes from Gatwick Airport I could see this as an ideal stop over when using the airport.

The afternoon tea? A truly quintessential experience

Not bad, but used to be better.. Stayed here this week for the first time in about a decade. It's not blatantly obvious, but I could sense that things weren't quite as they used to be.

When we checked in, I think the grown-ups were having the night off and we got the work experience shift. The young chap on reception was pleasant enough, but looked really scruffy - shirt hanging out of his trousers, tie undone, long hair falling about. Not a great first impression.

It's a rare hotel these days which doesn't have at least a 5ft bed in its double rooms - but the Hickstead still only has 4'6" doubles, even though there's plenty of space in the rooms for something larger.

Cleanliness was fine, but the hotel is just beginning to look a tiny bit tired - it's getting to the point where a good spruce-up would reap benefits.

Walls, doors and floors/ceilings could benefit from increased soundproofing. Every step taken by whoever was in the room above us resonated into our room, and the noise in the corridor on our second morning was very disturbing.

Nice breakfasts, served by very pleasant and welcoming staff. (But see the accompanying restaurant review for my views concerning two-person and four-person tables being far too close to each other - we spend most of our time at breakfast the first morning listening in detail to the phone conversation that the businesswoman on the next table was having with her office.)

Price-wise - a room here isn't cheap, but that seems to be the norm for Sussex these days (pretty much impossible to get anything decent for less than £100 a night)

Afternoon tea . Our afternoon tea was delightful.
Being vegetarian there was only two options but both were delicious. The sone was the best that I have tasted lovely and fresh.
The cakes were great and they changed one for us as it had a glaze and therefore not veggie.
Excellent food and good service

Afternoon tra. We arrived on a beautiful afternoon for tea using a voucher we had been given. Hotel is tired and in great need of a makeover. Dining room was utilitarian and the carpet dated. Windows/doors also needed a wash and polish. China could do with replacing. This aside staff were very pleasant and we were surprised to find the tea very agreeable. We opted for the roast beef and horseradish creme fraiche which was tender and tasty and the lemon pepper chicken. Scones were warm, but were a tad hard but the cake selection was very nice. The icing on the carrot and walnut cake literally melted in your mouth. The banoffee dessert had me beat but my husband said it was delicious. Would I go again the answer is probably no and due to lack of ambiance /surroundings I would not buy it as a gift for anyone else

I wish it could be a five star review. First of all the negatives. The decore is tired, inside and outside of our room. The furniture, carpets and bathroom need replacing and the bed was hard and uncomfortable. Some new fluffy towels would be nice!
This is where the negatives end. 
We visited for a family wedding and stayed two nights.  The setting is beautiful. Our room was a decent size. The staff are excellent - everything and anything we asked for was provided quickly and efficiently and always with a smile. The wedding day was well organised and flowed beautifully.  The food was very good and served hot and quickly (again always with a smile).  
The hotel lights up with the smiles of the staff - but it needs some tlc to improve the surroundings they work in

Veteran Car Club AGM. Today was our AGM, once more we chose this hotel for our meeting with 70 attending we received superb service from all staff, especially Patricia on Reception and Justine in charge of our dining arrangements. The Chef prepared all food as ordered and all served with a professional team of waiting staff. It was a pleasure to have such good staff looking after us

“Excellent Afternoon Tea”. We went today for an afternoon tea and it was excellent.

We took along our Labrador and the staff were more than accommodating, they made a fuss of her too and ensured she had water etc. 

The afternoon tea itself was fresh, plentiful and very very tasty. So much food that we couldn’t actually finish it,  The scones were warm and served with plenty of jam and cream too. 

We go for a few afternoon teas throughout the year and I have to say this was one of the best. The coffee was excellent too!

The staff were all very friendly and went out of their way to ensure our exact needs were catered for (Vegetarian), our waiter Cameron was attentive and ensured we were looked after throughout our visit. 

Go ahead and book, you will not be disappointed. We will certainly be going back! 

So far the best Afternoon Tea in Sussex! 

Well done !

Afternoon tea. We were given this as a gift. The bread was standard sliced loaf, the turkey filling was from a packet as was the beef and ham. The cake selection was limited. And there was no choice of scone, just had to have what was given. And the desert was served on the same plate as the sandwiches. Very mediocre. Wouldn't come again

Christmas lunch. Had Christmas lunch with my group, 1st time I have visited. Not enough seats in bar area for our party, lots standing.

Lunch was really late in being served, despite us all preordering. 

The filio pastry main was tough and the roast potatoes soggy. Sprouts appeared to have been frozen veg, likewise the parsnips and carrots which were just think wedges... Really disappointing. 

The blackberry pannacotta was not much better.

This was my first visit, doubt I'll be back with my partner.... 

Really disappointed

Dreadful food. We went for a staff Christmas party and we all agree the food was utterly appalling. My main course was a piece of inedible dried duck that was impossible to get the knife through, with soggy roast potatoes sitting on some sort of mushy, not very nice tasting vegetable concoction.  All our dinners resembled something that looked like it was from a cheap canteen and been sitting in a bain-marie for hours. At £41 per head just for food it was disgraceful, and we would never eat there again. 
On the plus side, the staff were lovely, especially Sharon and the DJ played some great music

Most perfect wedding day. Me and my new husband can't thank everyone at the hickstead hotel enough for making our wedding day the most special day of our lives. Everything from the hotel rooms to all the staff that evening was just amazing. Kellie, the events manager at hickstead was perfect in every way, nothing was too much of a bother and she was extremely accomodating, made it a pleasure planning my big day with her for the past year.  

If you are considering the hickstead hotel as your wedding venue then don't look any further. They will create your perfect day for you with a smile.

Thank you hickstead!!

Sophie & Kaine

Nothing special . I had organised for my works Christmas party to be at the Hickstead Hotel. 
Dealing with the events co-ordinator was very easy and both ladies I dealt with were very helpful and accommodating especially when one of our party had to pull out about a week before the event offering their full refund. 
We arrived at the venue, there was no one at reception so we made our way to the bar. There was a tray with a couple of glasses of Buck’s Fizz on the bar but no one there to hand them out so I had to ask if we were to help ourselves. 
We were ushered to the events room and took our seats. 
my starter came out, although a little sparse it was tasty. I had the turkey for my main. It did look a bit like a stuffed turkey roll you would get from Iceland but the turkey was flavoursome. The potatoes unfortunately were very soggy and looked like they could have stayed in the oven a bit longer. The carrots and parsnips were not what I expected either. They looked like chips and they too were soggy. The sprouts in my opinion was the tastiest item on my plate. And yes I do love sprouts. 
If I’m honest it was a bit bland, nothing special, but I did clear my plate. The serving staff were great and the disco was enjoyable. The price was a bit high for the quality of food you get so I don’t think I will be booking again next year. 
I did get a phone call from the events coordinator after the event asking how our evening went as one person in our party had expressed their disappointment with their meal. They had the duck as their main course and I must admit, I did try it and it was very dry. Glad I didn’t have the duck! I had not planned on making any complaint as the evening with friends/work colleagues was really enjoyable and wasn’t going to let a soggy potato get between that

A very pleasant establishment. A one night stay for business,clean and tidy rooms with nice decor,restaurant very good with a good selection of freshly prepared food,breakfast outstanding, a lovely quiet location,only down side is not within a taxi route

Afternoon Tea, Christmas Party and overnight stay. Had afternoon tea here followed by our Christmas part and then stayed overnight.

Was happy with all aspects.

Staff were friendly and efficient, the hotel was clean and in good order and the food both for afternoon tea and for the evening Christmas meal was very good. Breakfast was also good.

Prices for drinks were pretty reasonable too.

We were in the standard room and it was good, had a desk, couple of seats with a table, comfortable bed, big towels, good bathroom, all of good standards.

Plenty of car parking spaces outside

Christmas Party. We visited in December for our works Christmas Party. The staff were very friendly and nothing was too much trouble. A special thank you to Justine, Cami, Tony and Andy who made sure that our evening was enjoyable. The disco was fun with the DJ playing plenty of cheesy tunes, which kept everyone on the dance floor. When the disco finished, there was no rush for us to leave the bar with the staff happy to serve us still. The breakfast the next morning was lovely with a choice of cooked and/or continental and the rooms were comfortable. Overall, a very enjoyable evening and stay

RUBBER DUCK!!!!🦆. Me and my family stayed here for New Years eve. We had all pre ordered our meals and out of the 10 of us not one person had a good word to say about the food. I have never tried so hard to cut through a piece of duck in my life, it was inedible. It was sat on top of some limp greens, along side dry dauphinoise potatoes. The starter wasn't much better. We had hoped the dessert would redeem the meal but again we were disappointed. We all came away hungry!! And I was thankful I had bought a pack of crisps in my  suitcase. We were disappointed to read other reviews of people complaining about the rubber duck, yet they continued to serve it. I would not recommend eating here. We paid a substantial amount each for a night stay so would have at least expected something better than chewy and dry food. To top it off there is a nasty smell floating around the foyer which isn't particularly pleasant

2018 Out - 2019 In. Pat and I stayed two nights (Sunday 30th Dec to Tuesday 1st Jan) so we could visit the area and see the new year in at a New Year’s function they put on.
The staff were helpful generally but we had to eat out on after we arrived on Sunday as the hotel didn’t do food on Sunday evening.
Our duck at the New Year’s do was very chewy (or like Rubber Duck as others would describe it) so could have been better.
I don’t want these factors to overshadow the quality of services we received as the staff were very courteous and helpful in all ways to make our stay welcoming and enjoyable, and this part of the evaluation was excellent. They were friendly and sociable as well and we got to know them a bit more.
The environment inside was also very nice and comfortable in a rural location away from busy roads, which would be ideal in summer when the weather is right.
We are looking forward to our next visit later this year

Friendly staff, lovely rooms and fantastic food. We came here for afternoon tea and it was wonderful! Huge amount of food and staff are so friendly and kind. We booked our wedding here also and the staff went above and beyond to make us comfortable relaxed and it was the perfect day. The food was lovely and the rooms are fab!! Can’t recommend this place highly enough

Burns Night . My wife and I stayed at the hotel for a Burns Night celebration along with friends and a fairly large party. 
The 5 course meal was outstanding, with excellent service, and before we knew it 11 o’clock had come round and it was time to retire to the bar. This is very well stocked with a great selection of beers, wines and spirits (including my favourite-Benedictine-, which is quite hard to find now.)
The rooms are warm and comfortable (our mattress was a bit hard though) with plenty of space.
As a final bit of good service; we left some things in the room, which were located and kept for us to pick up the next day. 
Would definitely recommend this hotel

Lovely afternoon tea. We had a lovely unrushed afternoon tea for three. It was the festive menu and the stolen cake was wonderful along with delicious scones and fresh sandwiches. The food more than made up for slightly tired surroundings. Would go back because it was so delicious

hard beds. stayed here for two nights first the good points, hotel clean,rooms clean,resteraunt food awesome,staff all friendly esecially Justine (total Fruitbat) lol. The only thing that will stop me returning is the beds, like sleeping or trying sleep on a pool table, mattereses were rock hard. unless they have a mattress change we will not be returning which is a shame as it is a lovely hotel

Comfortable surroundings. Call in midweek to assess hotel for a group stay. Found the staff exceptionally helpful and welcoming.  Roomsshown looked very clean and comfortable.  Stayed for light lunch which was promptly served and nicely presented.  Dinner menu was limited but adequate for our needs. Terms offered for our planned Group booking were reasonable and competitive and acceptable so we have confirmed our intention to make a party booking.  The bar was small but adequate.  The dining area was ideal and we were shown their function room which was available for weddings and large groups. The hotel's proximity to the A23 and Hickstead showground makes it and ideal base for our party or as a stopover.  We will happily revisit and recommend others to do so

Afternoon tea. Although an enjoyable relaxing afternoon, the sandwiches and scones were very good. Unfortunately the quality of the cakes was very disappointing the ginger cake tasteless and the rum and raisin cheese cake unpalatable. Lose the jelly improve the cake selection please. Also we had to ask for clean plates after finishing the sandwiches. A few tweaks here and there are required. Not sure if I would return sorry

Amazing staff. I've stopped here a few times when training with my company. The staff are amazing and look after all your needs especially Emily, Justine and Vicky. I would recommend stopping here great food and friendly atmosphere

Fantastic staff. Stay here for business on average once a week and wouldn't stay anywhere else.

Good points, the staff. Especially Emily.
Bad points, the hard beds, the lack of TV in the bar and the sewage smell on the way in to the car park

Absolutely brilliant staff. Absolutely brilliant staff had an amazing time with Emily, Justine and Vicky. Great food and personality is great I would definitely recommend stopping here. Rooms are to a 5 star standard and cleanliness is to a high recommendation

How awesome the hotel is and staff. The staff in the hickstead are brilliant especially Emily, Vicky and Justine, I always look forward to staying hear every time I come here for work. The food is brilliant all the time, I have to say Vicky is my favourite. Justine is brilliant as she is multi skilled as she fixed my radiator when it wasn’t working

Afternoon Tea. We had a lovely afternoon tea at the Hickstead hotel. This was a present from our daughter. The service and selection of food was great and we’d like to make a special mention to Holly who looked after us superbly. Would highly recommend

Absolute pleasure!. One of the best hotel experiences I’ve ever had. Stayed for six weeks whilst working in the area and was very impressed from start to finish. Particularly the staff, who didn’t put a foot wrong the whole time. They spoilt us most evenings and even when there was an issue it was quickly resolved and always with a smile 😁
Would definitely recommend!!

Home for a month!. Stayed at the hickstead for our RSPCA training course, the staff literally made it like home. So warming, welcoming and loving, all thanks to the incredible staff. You’re all an asset, especially Justine who is just AMAZING, what a brilliant woman. All loved her! Also, Victoria on reception with the most welcoming face I’ve ever seen on a reception desk! So helpful, so happy! Brightens up your day in a quick smile!
And last but not least, Holly! Fun, friendly and so kind! Another gem! But all in all, everyone was so helpful the entire time, the food was lovely aswell. 
Thank you again to everyone at the Hickstead Hotel!! 
From Nick! X

Our Veteran Car club lunch and film show. We arrived and parked with plenty of space for our 43 guests, we were greeted by Patricia on Reception and met the duty Manager Justine who introduced me to Ellie and Hollie the two waitresses who would be looking after us. Throughout the visit, especially at the lunch serving the service was outstanding with attention to detail at all times. The Chef (Jimmy) served good quality food and it was cooked to perfection, well done Justine and your team

NOT QUITE UP TO STANDARD. We (7) visited for Christmas lunch.  The arrangements were fine, the staff - friendly, attentive, professional and efficient.  However, we all felt that the meal was, basically, tired and lacking in innovation. The venue was OK but the reception and dining area all were in need of an upgrade.  Do not take this as an overall condemnation but, until some work is carried out and the menu is served feeling fresh and tempting, we will not be returning.  A shame as we are local

Poorly organised. Arrived and whilst it appeared slightly in need of an update, after a 6 hour drive I wasn't too bothered. Evening meal starts at 6.30pm is a bit late but I accepted that, and then I enquired how to turn radiator on, only to be told it's broken and won't be fixed for 2 days. So I asked why they didn't tell me, she said they had tried to let people know as they arrived..  not sure how she failed in trying. But happy to take my money. It's freezing. Still waiting for a heater as I type. Not good hotel. Not good at all, glad we don't run our business this way

Fantastic afternoon tea. Can’t vouch for the hotel as such but everything looks very nice.
I can, however, thoroughly recommend the afternoon tea which I think represents great value. Generous sandwiches with interesting fillings together with a large selection of cakes, scones, and even a little trifle/dessert in a glass tube.
Friendly staff and pleasant rural location all add to the experience.
Having seen the evening meal menu we are tempted to give this a try at some point

Lovely Relaxed Afternoon Tea. We came for Afternoon Tea at The Hotel on Wednesday 13th March, and we were absolutely delighted! I read the reviews before coming (we had a voucher) and I must agree that the decor is very dated, however we thoroughly enjoyed our tea! We were served by two lovely young ladies, who were the highlight, both lovely, bubbly and attentive, catered to our every need with a smile! You could see they were enjoying their job whilst remaining professional and attentive, and it was so lovely to see, and they’re an asset to the hotel! 

Cakes and Scones were delightful, all presented amazingly! And we were able to take what we couldn’t finish back home with us!

We will be returning in the summer with some friends, as the waitress kindly informed me we could have afternoon tea outside, we can’t wait to return!

Simply lovely! We'll definitely be going back.. Oh what a joy to stay at a hotel where the staff are fabulous and the hotel really gets ALL the basics absolutely right. 

I booked online and two weeks before our stay I received a phone call from the receptionist asking me if we would like to book a table in the restaurant for our evening meal as it was the Brighton Marathon that weekend, and the restaurant was getting booked up. Nice touch - we didn't know about the marathon - we were just visiting relatives, and we gratefully accepted her offer.

I had also requested a mattress topper when I booked, because I need a soft bed due to a back condition, and the housekeeper ensured that one was put on the bed.

We were received a very friendly welcome from Sharon, the receptionist, and found our room to be large, comfortable and immaculately clean. There was a bowl of fresh fruit and glass bottles of both sparkling and still water. No plastic bottles - great! 

There was a sewing kit and shoe shiner in the drawer, as well as information about laundry and dry cleaning services. There were tea and coffee making facilities, plenty of lights, a trouser press and lots of sockets - important for me, as I have to plug in a piece of electrical medical equipment near the head of the bed. There was a hair drier (attached) inside a drawer, and the cable was a bit short really, but there was a comfy chair and a mirror right next to it, and it reached OK if I sat there.

There wasn't a full-length mirror, which would have been useful or mini bar (which didn't bother me a bit) and there wasn't a safe either, which considering lots of people now travel with laptops, ipads etc, was a bit of a surprise. I think they ought to consider adding those.

The bathroom was big enough, and had some nice touches such as tissues, cotton wool balls in a jar and toiletries provided. There was a good make-up mirror, although the lights were almost too bright and they dazzle a bit. The bathroom was very clean and the bath and shower were a joy.... none of those dratted dials to do battle with... a simple hot tap and cold tap on the bath, and a shower with two controls that said "Hot / Cold" and "On / Off"... how delightfully sensible, simple and uncomplicated! There was no complicated air con, but a window that opened, a thermostatic valve on the radiator, and an electric fan, so the temperature was very controllable and comfortable.

There were extra pillows and towels in the wardrobe (and the receptionist told us that on arrival), and we didn't need them, so they didn't need to be washed unnecessarily... another tick for their green credentials.

We went down for our evening meal, and were extremely impressed by both the food and the staff. The restaurant was fairly busy, and only three staff were on duty: Justine, (the restaurant supervisor, who smiles ALL the time, and creates a great atmosphere), and Kelly and Cammy who were helpful, friendly and efficient. The staff are really attentive - you only have to look at them and change your expression, and they pick up on it straight away and come and ask what you would like. As well as being quick and efficient, they all make you feel as if they have lots of time for you - I really don't know how they manage that - it's a real skill. I've been in many restaurants where the staff look, but don't see - the staff here really DO see, and that's a skill, too.

The food was lovely. The pate was smooth and creamy, but served with only three quarters of a slice of toast (why do restaurants always do that? The toast is the cheap ingredient!), but Justine was wise to this, warned us it didn't come with much toast, and asked us when we placed the order if we'd like extra, which we gratefully accepted. She then came to see if we would like more part way through our starter, and we said we would - so she organised that for us. The chutney was very tasty, too.

We ordered the chicken supreme, and asked for the asparagus to be substituted with savoy cabbage, which was done with no fuss at all. There was a generous portion of lovely tender meat, and the fondant potato and savoy cabbage were perfectly cooked.

I was disappointed that there wasn't a really chocolatey dessert on offer, so Justine fetched me the lunch menu, which had profiteroles on it. I actually settled for the sticky toffee pudding, which was gorgeous.

I then asked if I could have some decaffeinated teabags and a jug of milk to take to our room which again they provided immediately.

Breakfast was busy, probably due to it being marathon weekend, but again the service was good. Some of the food ran out and took a while to replenish, and the scrambled egg was a bit lumpy, but everything else was good, and when I was looking through the yoghurts, Justine asked me if I was OK, and I said I was looking to see if there was a strawberry one, and as there wasn't one out, she went and fetched some straight away. The morning staff were Justine and Kelly, who looked as fresh as they had the night before and Holly who was also lovely.

If you like a hotel with all the extras - mini bars, turndown service etc, then this might not be the place for you, but if you want good food, good value (£147 a night for a double room, bed and breakfast), a clean and comfy room, and fabulous staff who seem to love their jobs and really want to help you, then Hickstead Hotel will meet all your needs. Best hotel we've stayed in for ages, and we'll definitely be back

Amazing afternoon tea!. We would like to thank the lovely staff on duty today for such a fantastic experience. They went above and beyond to ensure we had a great party. The afternoon tea looked and tasted great. They were flexible with setting up all the decorations and made the whole party a lot more relaxed! Highly recommend !! 

Big thanks to all of you.

 Duty Manager – Justine

Waiting staff – Camille and Ellie

Chefs – Jimmy and Joe

Receptionist – Vicky
