First class. We had our Golden Wedding Celebration and everything was perfect. 
Service second to none very attentive and special mention to Justine, Supervisor, Camille and Ellie waitresses and Vicky at reception,  nothing was too much trouble for them.  Made to feel very special.   Abundance of sandwiches, scones, cakes etc all freshly made and very tasty.
Highly recommend this hotel.

Great staff, great food, 5+. Enjoyed a family afternoon tea at The Hickstead and its fair to say it was one of the best afternoon teas I have had in Sussex. Staff were so attentive and checking regularly that we had enough sandwiches and food. Plenty to eat and good quality. Worth a try for anyone who has not been here before. We will be back!

Great hotel . Staff are friendly and helpful, hotel was very clean and the room was very good the food was excellent very good value for money. I would definitely recommend this hotel for anyone who wants a nice break. Once you come off the main road the hotel is very well signposted and easy to find

Afternoon & Evening (Family) Social Event. This afternoon I organised an afternoon event (informal) to celebrate the life of my late mother with family friends. The hotel team were extremely accommodating to organise this event, with drinks food etc. We then had evening meal in restaurant, where nothing was too much trouble for us from the team.  Thank you Hickstead Team for making this Friday day 24th May extra special!!!

Still first rate. Stayed for a 4day break from checking in to checking out ALL staff were extremely helpful and very pleasant. The hotel is well maintained and very clean.The breakfast was very good plentiful and varied.The evening meal was well presented and enjoyable the waiting staff had time to talk without being overwhelming.We have visited quite a few classic lodges and we agree this is the best.Thanks to all staff see you again

Great hotel, would recommend the afternoon tea.. What a friendly and relaxed atmosphere here. The staff are lovely and helpful and the rooms spacious and comfortable.  We had an afternoon tea here, went out for the evening and then stayed the night. Great food, both tea and breakfast were generous helpings. Set in lovely grounds if you have children with energy to run off.  Would highly recommend

Surprisingly good!. This hotel is lovely location and easy to find 20 mins from Brighton and we were there for a horse event at Hickstead which was easy to get to.
The staff were so friendly and helpful and the dinner surprisingly good...
surprising as the hotel is dated and tired and could do with some refurbishment.. the rooms however are large but beds hard and definitely needing new pillows and bed linen!
it is a wedding venue to so make sure ask for rooms well away!
we would stay again mainly because the staff and location so good! Thank you!

Superb staff. Really nice room, excellent meals.  Quiet environment.  Bowl of fruit, hot drinks and chilled bottled water waiting on arrival. 
Chatty friendly staff looking after our every needs! Would be very happy to visit again

Wedding. We stayed at this hotel from Friday to Sunday  for a wedding thought the hotel needs some updating but still ok the break fast was very nice hats of to the chef the staff very friendly  a special  thanks to Sharon in reception  who we both thought was lovely we would stay again

Short stop here for work - Nice midrange hotel.. I stopped here for 2 nights during April.
The hotel is in a nice location and has good conferencing and function facilities.
The room was ok which was reflected in the price.
The staff were very friendly and there was a decent choice of beers and ales available from the bar.
The breakfast was very good too!

Wedding at Hickstead Hotel. We had our wedding here at the beginning of May and from the start of the wedding planning to the day itself was looked after by everyone.

All the staff could not do more than enough to help us and keep the day going as smoothly as possible. You were all truly welcoming.

The wedding guests themselves could not stop singing the praises of one member of staff in particular, who we would like to give a special mention to, Justine. She was their for our food tasting through to the ceremony and reception itself. With her amazing bubbly personality she kept you calm and relaxed and was always making us laugh.

The food was amazing! I would definitely recommend the sticky toffee pudding this went down a treat! The bedrooms were all lovely and spacious.

All in all I would definitely recommend this hotel for weddings and a even for a simple romantic quiet night away

Business Trip, nice Hotel. I stayed just one night for work.  I arrived late and just made dinner in the restaurant - stops at 9:30pm.  But staff were welcoming and allowed me to order and eat dinner.  Rooms were clean and nice big bed. All the usual things for a room

Good service. Best receptionist ever: Toni the man!!!
Thanks for everything 
For sure, one place that everyone should came here. Very nice food and is placed in a really good place with a lot trees and good environment

Very pleasant overnight stay. Needed a place to stay en-route from France to a business meeting in the area and found The Hickstead! What a gem! Dog friendly and very comfortable room. Dinner and breakfast were superb and the friendly staff went out of their way to ensure that everything was perfect. Will definitely stay there again, and even Henri the dog gave the hotel a paws up!

relaxing and very friendly. The Hickstead is in a  quiet rural location, but only 10 minutes from Haywards Heath and 30 minutes into Brighton. On arrival,Patricia on reception was very welcoming and friendly, and even remembered our booking and email correspondence, and which room we had requested. The room itself, whilst a little tired, had everything we needed, and they even provided a fan as the weather was extremely hot. We had fresh toiletries provided throughout our stay, and the room was clean, fresh and aired upon our return (thank you Rosie).
Breakfast was absolutely brilliant. A choice of cereals, fruit,yoghurt, croissants, and a choice of hot foods from the menu. We had full English, and it was delicious. The waiting staff did an amazing job of keeping tables cleared and waiting times for food to a minimum. This was especially noticeable on the Sunday, when everyone seemed to arrive at the same time. Fair play to the lovely friendly staff, you did a fabulous job and deserve that pat on the back! What can I say...its not 5* luxury, but its a good honest hotel with friendly staff throughout. What more could you ask for. Would I stay again and recommend to friends and family...without a doubt

Nice Hotel . In a lovely setting and with excellent and friendly staff. However hotel is looking dated  and food only average. On balance over priced for what is offered. Having said that we enjoyed our stay.
It’s a great location and although not far off the M23 so very good for business and leisure stays but enjoys a very peaceful and quiet country setting in nice grounds

Lovely food. Hotel is easy to find , 20 mins from Gatwick but very quiet ! Clean , being updated , lovely food and helpful staff . I liked the fact there was not a television in the bar or restaurant area , it made for peaceful dining and drinking . I think it would be a good hotel to stay at if you were flying out the next day

Lovely Afternoon Tea. My wife and I enjoyed a really lovely afternoon tea. All the staff were friendly and helpful, the surroundings were nice, and the food was fresh and well presented. Really good experience. Recommended

Lovely afternoon tea. Enjoyed our afternoon tea. Good value, great selection of high quality tasty cakes, fresh sandwiches and scones, and very friendly staff. We weren't rushed and it was all very enjoyable. Will definitely return for another. Promised I'd write a review as we all had such a nice time, it was our anniversary. Sorry it's a bit late! Many thanks for a great afternoon

Funeral Celebration of Life. We recently held a dear family member's funeral wake at The Hickstead Hotel.  Emma McKay, the events coordinator, approached our requests with a calm and efficient manner. 
The table flowers were a delight and made a memorable gift for our guests when they left. The hot nd cold buffet was excellent and served by a professional and caring staff.
We highly recommend this hotel for similar events. 

We have also stayed at this hotel, connected with this events  and found the pet friendly room and breakfast to be more than adequate. Easy parking in lovely grounds

Tea for Two. Lovely staff who where only too happy to accommodate my dietary needs.
Afternoon tea for two was an absolute treat. Tasty sandwiches and fabulous cakes with freshly baked scones, jam and clotted cream for husband and delicious lactose free options for me.   Plenty of time to enjoy the experience.  Would definitely recommend

Great location. We were attending a wedding in the area and wanted somewhere quiet but convenient. The Hickstead was an ideal location for us. The Hotel is somewhat dated  , nevertheless we really enjoyed our stay. The staff are the most friendly and helpful you could wish for, nothing was too much trouble. The room was very quiet and clean and the beds comfortable.  There was a good choice at breakfast and the evening meal imaginative and well cooked.

Convenient for overnight stay . We went to a wedding nearby and this hotel was convenient and reasonable value as needed 2 rooms. Clean, comfy bed, ample parking and excellent full English breakfast. Near A23 but couldn't hear traffic noise. No other restaurants nearby but that wasn't an issue for us

A stunning wedding venue. My wife and I would like to start by sending a HUGE thanks to all the staff at the Hickstead for making sure our special day was truly special... Kelly, Emma, Vicky and Holly couldn’t have worked harder to make our dreams come true! From start to finish they worked tirelessly ensuing everything was just perfect. The hotel is spotlessly clean and tidy and just the perfect venue for a wedding, every tiny detail is catered for and nothing is too much trouble, we had such a wonderful time from the moment we arrived to the moment we left.... the staff even joined us on the dance floor for the final dance of the evening which really did top it all off... you guys are amazing and we would highly recommend the Hickstead Hotel to anyone thinking of holding they’re wedding there. We love you all xxx Nicky and John Prout xxx

Anniversary Stay. First time in this hotel and we were very impressed with the warm welcome. Accessible and easy to get around. The room was very clean and the hotel looked very clean. Yes it could do with an update in place's  not an issue, a good hotel is about it's the staff that make you feel at home and Hannah was outstanding like all the staff they make your visit special. A lot of modem hotels are soulless the Hickstead  is a traditional  hotel with values and put the customer first. We ate both nights with breakfast and again lovely food cooked fresh and piping hot. A return visit is a must. Big thank you to all at the Hickstead for making our weekend special

Great food, hit and miss service and tired facilities. Stayed for two days in the last week. The hotel in general and rooms in particular need modernisation and feels tired and lack lustre. Service is enthusiastic but a little haphazard. Food was fantastic quality only brought down by the same meal being presented differently on consecutive evenings by different staff. Outside eating facilities are all a bit B&Q. Budget entry hotel charged at 4 star rates. A regulation Premier Inn represents much better value

Afternoon tea. Had to ask to be seated at another table as, only 2 other couples in large restaurant , our table was next to someone else! Hardly saw a member of staff , she did manage to leave the kitchen once to ask if we wanted  more tea when we had almost finished. Sandwiches were hardly dainty as a third of sandwich with no crusts. Prawn sandwich was dry and meat was not very good quality processed meat. Gooseberry fool was nice but the cheesecake was tasteless and Victoria sandwich  dry and not particularly nice. The scones had fruit in ,no choice, and were more like rock cakes with poor quality jam

Attention to detail. We stayed here for one night. On the positive side, good location, very quiet area, room was good quality, fruit bowl roo, nice touch. Would recommend a second floor room, because the occupants above our ground floor room,  were attempting to get their snoring on the Richter scale. Also very creaky. 

A half finished bottle of beer on reception wasn't the best welcome, nor was the bizarre carpet, which had a repeating pattern that looked like someone had dropped a cup of tea, 50 times down the corridor.

With some TLC and a bit of attention to detail in the non-room parts of the hotel, it would make all the difference. 

Overall, it's fine for a night at present, but could be improved for sure.

Afternoon tea. Had afternoon tea here earlier this year.Tea was lovely we choose the sandwiches we wanted.The staff were very good and friendly.The tea was very filling and tea too ups a plenty.Plenty of parking space.Made very welcome

Impressed with service and attention. What a warm welcome we received at the Hickstead when we visited for afternoon tea on our way to start an extended holiday in France. We were very impressed with the quality of the tea in a relaxed atmosphere giving us the perfect beginning to a breakaway.  Service was good as was the choice of menu and variety of sandwiches available, although we overindulged on the choice of cakes generally a big well done

Overnight stay. When you arrive the outside of the building tells two stories older but pleasant modern but not up to date.

This goes for the inside too. The corridors  are in desperate need of new carpets  redecoration  and better lighting to bring up to a modern standard.

The rooms are a little dated couldn’t shut the window  bed normal double not that usual any more. 

The service is interesting it fluctuates sometimes attentive sometimes less so.

The lady on the reception does many jobs and is quite assertive and omnipresent.

The breakfast was quite good, but the decor in the dining room is awful.

I wouldn’t stay her again I’m afraid. Just not my cup of tea.

Brilliant ,helpful friendly staff clean comfortable rooms and amazing food. I stayed here in September at short notice when the local Travelodge lost my booking after I drove 8 hours from Devon ,the staff at this hotel despite being packed with a car show on and a wedding found us a room and helped us immediately ,
They managed to squeeze us in 3 nights and all the rooms were spotless comfortable and the food was amazing , we ate every night and had nearly everything on the menu and it was 5* , Hannah our waitress was so helpful and friendly and Sharon on reception was brilliant and couldn’t do enough to help us .
I would thoroughly recommend this hotel they made our weekend amazing can’t thank them enough x

Hannah Banana. Stayed here for two weeks and your staff make this hotel special. Nothing is too much trouble and they put up with our antics and looked after us every day. Special mentions to Kellie but by far the jewel in the crown is Hannah Banana. What an absolute star always has a smile on her face and looked after us every night. Thank you Hannah you are the best

Great customer service. The staff here are great, I stayed, but had accidentally booked for a different month. I arrived in the pouring rain with my dog, the staff made me feel welcome and helped check me into my room. 

The next morning they helped seat us somewhere in the hotel for breakfast with my dog, that wasn't in the restaurant. Honestly, very attentive and kind staff, especially Laura who made me feel so relaxed and like nothing was too much trouble. 

My Labrador, Rosie, was also very well looked after! 

Thank you!

Business Stop Over for Course. One checking in the Staff were polite and checked me in quickly.  The room was clean and large and more than adequate for my six day stay at The Hickstead.  The breakfast was more than adequate and you had a good variety on the menu.  The evening meal was good on the first night I ate in the restaurant and the following evening I ate in the lounge and took food from the lounge menu, which was good and hearty food.

Overall a very good stay and many thanks to all the staff..

Steve

Afternoon Tea. Had a very, very enjoyable afternoon tea here. Sandwiches, scotch egg, scones with cream and jam, treacle tart, raspberry cheesecake, carrot cake and banoffee  fool!  We never made it to the cakes !  Staff were very friendly. Thoroughly recommended.  Go there...you won't be disappointed!

Check before you book!. We had been given an afternoon tea gift and chose this hotel, the food was just beautiful, afternoon tea, dinner and breakfast were all delicious. The staff are some of the frienliest I have ever encountered at a hotel, that is why it is so sad that we had a very disappointing stay!
 We unfortunately stayed when there was a wedding! HUGE MISTAKE! We weren't told at the time of booking or later when actually speaking to a receptionist about a ground floor room that there would be a wedding on that day. OMG I have never experienced such noise at a hotel. Kids were being allowed by stupid parents to play up and down the corridor alongside the ground floor rooms. We did phone and ask reception if this was normal and the lovely lady that came go our door apologised and ask them to stop, which they did for a while but it happened again later as well. My advice to anyone booking is check when you book to see if they have a wedding booked! Although the staff were all lovely and helpful and so friendly our Weekend was spoilt by the REALLY RUDE INCONSIDERATE GUESTS AT THIS WEDDING. We could not really get out of there quick eenough on the Sunday morning. So unfotunately we would not come again. Sad really because it is a lovely hotel

Afternoon tea . A little afternoon tea with my mother and husband, turned out to be very generous, with  large selection of sandwiches, cakes and scones all for a very reasonable £17.50/head.  Nice little dining room but bizarrely the radio was the background music so we were treated to a selection of Taylor Swift, Rhianna, Justin Bieber, etc etc when perhaps more subdued music would have been more appropriate. The afternoon tea also took 50 mins to arrive from ordering.  The staff were delightful. It was just extremely slow delivery but on the plus side, at least you know it’s fresh; the quality of the food, friendly staff and the excellent value gives it my score of four stars!!

It's ok but it's getting tired & is in need of a refresh.. It's ok but it's getting tired & is in need of a refresh.
It's a little too expensive for the standard of the rooms & facilities provided.
It seems to rely heavily on weddings & corporate visitors to the nearby industrial estates in Burgess Hill. 
Breakfast quality varied from good to poor over the three nights I stayed there.
Having sampled the poor breakfast on my first day, I chose to eat my other meals away from the hotel.
On a more positive note, the staff are all lovely & very helpful. Unfortunately, the quality of the accommodation & public areas, bar & restaurant don't seem to have been updated in a very long time

Afternoon Tea. We were given an afternoon tea & chose to book the Hickstead Hotel & we would definitely recommend.

Easy to book staff friendly & attentive and the food was fabulous, really fresh and so many cakes & treats 

Really quiet relaxing setting we had a lovely time

The staff are superb. I think this is probably more of a business hotel, so weekdays would be busy. We stayed on a very quiet weekend and so there wasn't much of an atmosphere in the restaurant and we decided to go out rather than using the hotel bar.
We had dinner and breakfast and both were tasty and plentiful.
As some have mentioned, the shared areas are quite cosy and could possibly do with a refresh. The room was good and the bathroom had obviously been done up, the self demisting mirror with excellent lighting is particularly good. The heating is also powerful which is very much appreciated in a cold English winter. 
The staff were all friendly, attentive and thoughtful - they made the quiet hotel come alive and have driven the higher rating than the hotel alone might have scored

Great Afternoon tea. We were given an afternoon  tea  gift for a weeding anniversary present, and picked the Hickstead  as it was a drive out from Caterham taking about 45 minutes door to door.
Easy to find and plenty of parking.  the staff were helpful and friendly  and the afternoon was perfect all fresh and for us a surprise warm scotch egg  made a pleasant surprise.
We did not notice any background music to be honest as we had a bit to talk about.
As a hotel i would not be sure what the main clientele it would attract. 
Yes  we would go there again

Terrible value for a veggie breakfast . With nearly every eatery ramping up veggie and vegan options the Hickstead is seriously behind the curve...astonishingly expensive as a non guest £14.95 and whilst it includes an exhausted looking breakfast table with juice and cereal it's not a breakfast of great joy! When asked for the veggie choices it was basically whatever the full english was without meat, no hash browns and a tired veggie sausage, it arrived on a massive plate which made my 4 items even more pitiful and we had to literally ask for everything, sauce  , toast...I got to reception and asked if there was a discount as no meat...no...ha ha..fancy! I should have ordered full english and taken the mest back for my dogs ..I won't rush back

Excellent Christmas Day lunch. After a few hiccups with out booking prior to Christmas Day we were a little bit anxious but we needn’t have worried at all.

From the moment we arrived everything was five star and very good value for money too.  The staff were very friendly and could not do enough for you to make sure the lunch was perfect.  Our table was beautifully decorated with all the Christmas touches you would expect.  We all had chosen a wide range of choices from the menu and everyone agreed all of them were absolutely delicious.

Thank you to The Hickstead for a very special day and we can’t wait to book for 2020!

Wonderful Afternoon Tea. This was one of the best Afternoon Teas that I have had, and I have enjoyed a few over the years! All the food was superb, with great variety, and plenty of it. Even my husband was satisfied! The service was very good and friendly, and we would definitely recommend it

This Hotel needs attention !!! . Stayed here for a single night, have been a regular on and off over the years, and while the staff and food were good, this hotel badly needs some proper investment and TLC. It’s in a perfect location close to Hickstead Show Jumping Arena, up the road from the new expansion in Burgess Hill, it could be a premier location for many local businesses and location, but sadly it’s very tired. It needs investment in just about every area. From the reception areas (the always prevalent faint sewage whiff), to the carpets fixtures and fittings bedrooms, bathrooms and water pressure issues (which we were told about on check out - when shower flow was  complained about - very handy!) the food in the restaurant was good, as was the service and staff very polite. Just someone needs to take this hotel, spend some serious money...to make some serious money from local business, weddings and tourists. Why do I say this? The highest price local hotel in Burgess Hill is a Premier Inn (c £150 per night) and it’s always booked up, this hotel isn’t and that says it all

Very friendly staff make a very pleasant stay. Obviously you don't meet many staff in hotels during lockdown.  The ones I did encounter were great and a credit to a hotel which could do with a bit of modernisation. Hotel is clean and plenty of sanitizer to go around.
It would be nice to see a change in menu once or twice a week  if your running such a limited menu at the moment. Both the meals I had were delicious as was breakfast. 

Big thanks for the fried bread on Wednesday and Thursday.  Don't know why hotels have removed this from there menus. Thanks chef!

DISGUSTING. Absolutely disgusting
Receptionist unwelcoming and unaccommodating.
Miserable with no customer service skills
Filthy seating, dirty and a general unhygienic place 
Completely rundown interior  with no atmosphere
Didn’t expect this unpleasant experience 
Needs to be shut down immediately
Will never return

Avoid. Short review: Wish we'd booked the Travelodge on the roundabout - £80 cheaper for 2 nights including breakfast.
Long review:
FOOD:  no idea.  At check-in we were informed the kitchen was closed for the duration and breakfast (rate was B & B) would be delivered in a bag containing room-temperature orange juice, instant porridge, toffee yoghurt (for breakfast?), a yoghurt covered cereal bard and a croissant, plastic cutlery and no plate.  Yum!
ROOM:
Choice of views consists of car park or the Bolney Industrial Estate, which is great if listening to revving engines all day and evening (until 10:30pm) on a Saturday is your thing.  Hardly "Set in peaceful Sussex countryside nestling beneath the Downs" advertised on website.
Room was spacious... but decor reminiscent of 1980's mid-range hotel.  Carpet stained.
Bathroom: Shower had good water pressure and a 'quiet close' lid on toilet seat.  Wish the doors had been 'quiet close'... they slam... extremely loudly
TV - small and located as far away from bed as possible.  Freeview but would only access ITV channels and E4 (aside from shopping channels), which is great if Midsomer Murder & Friends repeats are all you want.
Tea/Coffee:  only decaf in room.
No room service.
Bed:  Uncomfortable - mattress with collapsed at edges.
COVID procedures:  Signage goes to great lengths to insist that guests wear face coverings over "Nose & Mouth", but this policy evidently doesn't apply to staff

Fantastic customer service. Visited the Hickstead hotel on our recent trip to Hickstead and had to post to say the customer service was fantastic. The staff couldn't do more to help and were so friendly and welcoming, definitely recommend

Wonderful afternoon tea. My friend bought an afternoon tea for my birthday. We went on Sunday and it was great. The sandwiches were tasty and well filled. The scones were the tastiest I’ve had in an afternoon tea. The Eton Mess in the little glass was divine. We ate everything and that is very unusual for me. It was actually normal things that I would eat for a change, my husband was disappointed there was no doggy bag!!

highly recommended, beautiful setting. As chief bridesmaid for my friends wedding, the minute we arrived  Alex, Mel, Karen and the team made us feel so welcome and helped in every way possible for the day to go smoothly. 
The venue is beautiful and the staff went above and beyond to make my friends dream day come true. 
We really can’t thank you all enough

Brilliant team!. We held a group event at the hotel over the first weekend in October and despite the fuel  supply issues we had a great time.  Alex, Karen, Elisabete and the rest of the team were brilliant, they worked extremely hard in trying circumstances, providing us with excellent food and great service and they managed to make our first weekend away as a group most memorable.  The only comment I would have about the hotel building is that it needs some looking after itself

Excellent hotel. I organised a reunion for 77 people for Saturday 16th October. Unfortunately, mainly due to Covid, 16 had to cancel. The Events Manager (Karen) was so very helpful throughout and we had a fantastic night. The rooms were clean and comfortable and the staff were superb. The evening meal for the function was perfect as were the staff waiting on the tables. The bar staff could not have been more helpful and the breakfast on  Sunday morning was excellent. There is a large car park and WiFi. I would highly recommend this hotel

Great Wedding Venue. Recently had our Wedding at The Hickstead Hotel. Beautiful surroundings and lovely staff. The wedding co-ordinators were very welcoming, helpful, thoughtful, supportive and nothing was too much trouble. The package was also very reasonably priced and we felt we got a lot for the money.
The only reason that I haven't given it the full 5 stars is because as a Hotel that promotes itself as a wedding venue, I feel it should have a proper Honeymoon Suite for the newly married couple. The room that is allocated is a normal Excecutive room, which is nice but smaller than other hotels Honeymoon suites

Faulty Towers. Recently went to a wedding. (Half the wedding contracted covid), although this was no doubt carried in by a guest the rooms were tropical with heating on full whack all day and night/stifling. The rooms are quite tired. Ours had ripped net curtains and the inter joining room had rips in the duvet covers. We ordered champagne the morning of wedding to brides room which arrived after some time but with no glasses or the orange juice we had asked for with it, these turned up 30 mins later. The breakfast (bearing in mind there were 4 of us and one couple and a single man dining) had been left to sit under a heat lamp for ages so everything was dry to the point the beans had a thick skin on them. The toast we ordered with breakfast turned up 30 mins after we’d finished and the butter another 20 mins after that. I think there were literally 2 staff members doing everything. They were pleasant but when you are charging money for a wedding at your establishment you need to have more staff. They looked knackered and obviously were working far too long hours. There were rubbish bags and mops and buckets from the day before in the front porch if hotel that one of us ended up clearing away so the guests would nit be greeted with it when they arrived. The bar in the room of wedding was supposed to be open but was not. All in all it was all a bit like faulty towers. Place needs a lick of paint, sprucing up  and more staff

Wedding at Fawlty Towers. We booked our wedding for June 2021 but had to move it due to covid. The hotel were very helpful and initially in touch to discuss all the arrangements but it went downhill after our Co-Ordinator went on maternity leave.  We had to chase relentlessly and whilst Karen was an improvement on her predecessor there were things that were completely missed in the set up.   The room I stayed in the night prior to the wedding left a lot to be desired with ripped bedding and torn net curtains. The day of the wedding we were served an inedible breakfast that had been under a heat lamp but to be fair there was only one lady serving who was also covering reception.  A friend of mine had to move all the cleaning equipment away from the front of the hotel as it was such an eyesore. The wedding went smoothly and the dinner was good but there was items that they got wrong on the menu which was disappointing.  There was also a dreadful loud buzzing coming from a fridge from the bar at the back of the room.    In the evening the bar in the function room was not used as promised and everyone had to use the bar in the lounge which was tiny.  There was no explanation to this and we had been promised that both bars would be open and there  would be no queuing for drinks which did not happen.  The staff became incredibly rude at the end of  the night - it felt like they had had our money and we were no longer important to them.  We were told that the bar would be open to all hotel guests but this was shut at 100am by a very grumpy member of staff who said that he would need a water cannon to make the guests leave and go to bed!  Absolutely appalling!    Obviously the wedding rates there are cheaper than other hotels but there is a good reason for this as the hotel is dilapidated and needs an overhaul.  A huge Covid infection for a third of the guests was the cherry on the cake - not blaming the hotel for that but it didnt help having over 130 people crammed in their lounge bar!!!

What more can you ask for. Great B and B shame that do not do any food at night and as they are away from any food outlets you have to eat before you return. Clean rooms friendly nice staff and warm and at the right price what more can you ask for. The breakfast is massive and fulls you up for the day. Just wish the bacon and sausages where nicer, but that’s only my opinion stayed at a lot of hotels this is a good one

Good resonable priced stay. Hotel a little way from main road but quiet and secluded once there.
Stayed during covid restrictions therefore a little difficult to summerise .
Rooms ok and pleanty of outside space in nice weather

Excellent Wedding Venue. We had my son's wedding at The Hickstead Hotel and we were so pleased with the service and the quality of food on the day.  Everything ran smoothly and the wedding planner did a great job.  Both the sit-down meal and evening buffer had gorgeous food.  Thank you to all staff that made the day go so well

In need of and update.. This was my third of fourth stay here and while it was ok, it really was just OK. £140 B and B was average price. 
It really could be a nice hotel with just a few updates. My room was well sized, but dated, with a tv and remote with the back missing, threadbare carpet and hairs in the bath, showing it hadn’t been thoroughly cleaned. 
Having said that the room was clean. 
Breakfast which costs £15. (Part of the £140) was not as good as I have experienced there before. 
No one to greet and find a table for you, tea and coffee now served in pump urns, which didn’t work properly. Toast and full English was ok, sausages were a highlight! And Heinz ketchup was on offer. 
All in all ok… but I think if Premier Inn had rooms, that would be my choice over here

Anti social guests. Very noisy anti social guests in room next to me who told me to f off you f...ing bitch when I asked them to be quiet at 2.30am. Poor quality sound insulation, banging doors, tired decor. Staff pleasant but ineffectual in managing situation. I won't be returning

Comfortable clean bedroom. Hotel recovering from Covid. This hotel is set up for weddings conferences etc. If you look for a peaceful stay avoid weekends. A very quiet location. They are busy at times and I'm happy for them because they have clearly had a tough time during Covid. The place is recovering.....I think. There isn't a large budget for maintenance. Friendly and helpful service. A good place for a short stay if you need to be in the area

Great location for Hickstead showjumping ground. Our granddaughter and her horse had qualified in our area for the national schools  competition to show jump in the final at The All England Jumping Course at Hickstead. As we live nearly 300 miles away we wanted to stay local to the show ground and required a hotel the was dog friendly. The Hickstead hotel fitted that requirement being just 5 minutes from the venue. All the staff we encountered were friendly and helpful from reception to restaurant. The bedroom was on the ground floor and was a good size with tea and coffee making facilities. The room was starting to look tired and the room was not well insulated as we heard guests upstairs walking about above us and also in the corridor. The price for B & B and the dog was good value and the cooked breakfast was plentiful. The hotel is set away from the main road so you are not disturbed by passing traffic and is surrounded by large gardens ideal for walking the dog. There are plenty of parking spaces around the hotel

Nice place, with a few pennies needed to be spent perhaps.. Fantastically located for commuting to all of West and East Sussex. Lovely building with huge grounds and ample parking. Very safe for the kids and very close to Burgess hill and Hayward’s heath if to live need a supermarket or somewhere different to eat.

The staff are really lovely and made our stay very comfortable. The receptionist upon check-in was very helpful arranging a return taxi for us all as we were in the area for a family wedding. We didn’t make use of the restaurant but the menu looked fresh with a few good options.

2 night stay was reasonable in terms of cost for a “room only” status.

A couple of small issues we needed to bring to the attention but we’re dealt with during and after our stay with some really good post communication.

With a little more investment perhaps in the rooms, bringing them up to date, this place will be a lovely place to return too.

Thanks

lovely staff poor food and rooms in need of update. The staff were all cheerful and helpful, however, our room was dark and airless, the bedding was low quality, foam pillows and cheap duvet. On our second day, our room was not cleaned and tea making items not replaced, loo paper had run out , bin not emptied and bed unmade and towels not replaced. The over bath shower had a cheap curtain that needed replacing, it didn't reach far enough down, to stop water running onto the bathroom floor. A staircase ran over our room so we were kept awake at night by constant running up and down. The food was dissapointing. I had chicken pie with mash and vegetables which arrived barely warm. I returned it and asked for a salad sandwich, which I was told, they did not do. I had an egg sandwich which was made with the cheapest supermarket, thin white bread, which I didn't eat. The breakfast bread was at least wholemeal, but again, the thin, cheap supermarket type. The scrambled egg that my husband had,was tough and unpleasant. No chef worth the name would ever produce such poor quality food so I assume they don't have one. Whilst I accept that Covid has hit hard in many places, this low level of experience really is not acceptable . We were expecting at the very least, decent quality food and a comfortable bed to sleep in. The hairdyer provided was fixed at the back of a drawer and required the on switch to be held down to keep working and the flex was much too short, making the whole experience very difficult. There were only very shallow and narrow drawers in the room, only big enough for socks and other very small items of clothing and the hanging space in the wardrobe was too short for dresses, meaning that the bottom 6 inches of my dresses were crumpled. Fine for men who just have trousers and shirts, but not good for us ladies whose dresses need more space to hang.All in all, not something we would want to repeat.The online pictures of this hotel show a large, impressive Edwardian property, but the rooms are in a tatty annex next to that. We were expecting big airy rooms, what we got was a million miles away from that, low ceilinged, gloomy and airless

Room filthy. The staff fabulous couldn't fault them, BUT the room omg it stunk so fusty to the point it made us feel sick the bedding & the decorative pillows & bed throw stunk so bad 👎 like it had just been fetched from a charity shop...the curtains when opened stains all up the back of the lining the windows & window sills was filthy,  i just wanted to leave so bad, tv didn't work no aircon or fan the room was so hot didn't sleep most of the night. the fusty smell was even worse from the umidity..would i stay here again Absolutely NOT could not wait for morning so we could leave? Left 2 hours early couldn't even eat the breakfast because could smell the fusty stinking room on our clothes the smell was awful 🤢 room needs a good clean and bedding washing & everything disinfecting

Wedding. I would just like to thank all the staff for thier wonderful service on our wedding day. Everything was so perfect and all the guests were very happy .The food was amazing and the rooms were very clean and comfortable

Business. I booked this room specifically as their website said they did 24 hour room service and I knew I wouldn't get to eat until around 2300 due to working.
Got back at 2300 and was told the kitchen closed at 2100 and they didn't do 24 hour room service, showed the manager that it was on their site. Was told that the bar was available 24 hours and this is classed as room service!
Was also told that if I knew I was going to be late I could have told them and they would have had something there, why would I do this when it said 24 hour room service.
When I asked for Head Office details, was told by the manager all complaints come to me.
Went to bed very hungry, will not be returning and I do a lot of visits to Hickstead as work in the Horse world

A fabulous stay at a first class hotel. We stayed at the hotel, as we were attending a wedding nearby. Well, I have to say that the hotel was absolutely first class. The staff were fabulous and super friendly and helpful. The rooms are very well appointed and included a trouser press. There was even a shoe cleaning set. I would recommend this hotel to anyone

Wonderful hotel. Stayed for a week. Lovely large double room. Food was amazing, from the full English breakfast to my absolute favourite the sticky toffee pudding ( best I’ve ever tasted). Every member of staff were friendly and couldn’t do enough for you . Beautiful setting. Will definitely be back . Thankyou 🥰

Not good….avoid.. Used this hotel on business pre pandemic and it has gone downhill.
Rooms are dated. Beds very hard. Bathrooms tired.
Restaurant…….not good at all. We were a group of 8 with only 5 other people in the restaurant. Food took ages to arrive. Burgers were like ice hockey pucks. Fish and chips dry. The worst thing was everything seemed be cover in salt….not nice at all. Then at 9.20 pm we asked for desert and were told that the chef had shut the kitchen…..unbelievable!
The only good thing about the hotel is the plentiful free parking when you stay there

Fawlty Towers is better. First the staff - they are not at fault and are helpful nice people. The hotel...where do you start, old, tired, carpets scruffy & stained, bedding feels stale, cant open the windows (Health & safety) so stuffy and airless, towels the same as the carpets. Decent shower to be fair and parking is great but burgers like ice hockey pucks, salt overload and a chef that went home before we could order dessert at 9.30! Breakfast average and for the price this isnt worth the money. Only stayed there because I couldnt get in my usual hotel and it was a great reminder of why we stopped using it

Weekend at the friendly Hickstead. I stayed here with my family whilst attending a convention at the Amex in Brighton.  The rooms were extremely clean, the breakfast was excellent and the staff were very friendly and helpful.  I would certainly recommend this hotel, and will be back again next year.  Thank you

Clean, friendly staff - avoid breakfast. Cheap and cheerful, didn’t expect much given the price and reviews but I have to say the rooms where better than expected and the staff so very helpful, my only downside was the breakfast, buffet breakfast with little options, most of the Tins were empty or only dregs left so a bit disappointed from that but otherwise if you need a bed for the night I would recommend

Ok. Catering mothers day it was OK. Finger sandwiches very basic with the crusts on. Cream cheese in smoked sal.on sandwich was not cream cheese but pureed cottage cheese. Yuk. Rest was mediocre and sat at its 3 star level. Staff were all pleasant but lounge off the reception area smelt like something had died under the floor boards

Absolutely amazing. This hotel is lovely.  We came here for a wedding .  Fantastic venue and really great friendly professional staff .  The hotel is immaculately clean.  the rooms are spacious .  The food served was perfection .  I’m not sure how they got so many fish dishes out and cooked them so delicately .  Impeccable

Nice hotel and staff just bit dated. Lovely reception staff, great parking,  hotel nice and clean and quiet. 
Didn't eat so unable to comment. 
Room was nice and clean and bed comfy,  shower good pressure. 
Not enough plug sockets, only 2 spare and 1 was used for the fan (hotel provided as hot and no aircon), so struglle to charge 2 phones.
Room nice but bit dated/tatty

Great stay!. Super helpful, considerate & friendly staff. Hotel was exceptionally clean, restaurant food was good - a little greenery-light but not 3663 / ping meal plates. Plentiful parking & welcomed dogs which although I didn’t bring mine is brilliant to know for future stays.
Some of the decor is a little dated but everything is in good order / super clean & you can’t fault the location for anyone competing at Hickstead!

Second stay there and I will return. My stay at the Hickshead hotel has been excellent in many respects, especially the helpfulness of the staff and their proactive attitudes. The Hotel is very convenient, the food is good and as I say the Staff make this place somewhere I will return. 
Unfortunately if you have a room facing the carpark not only do you get woken up at silly o'clock by patrons chatting in the carpark (not the Hotel's fault at all) but also by the smell of cigarette smoke coming in through the window, as patrons that are staying in the Hotel sit on the picnic tables to smoke.  Yes I did have to get up and close the window but then the room is too warm, catch 22. Other than that I would recommend staying there as it was very chilled and accommodating. Thank you Hickstead hotel

Comfortable stay. I stayed for just one night at the Hickstead Hotel, the staff were very welcoming on arrival, I was a little disappointed you could not get a snack in the bar in the afternoon, but they did arrange a sandwich for me.

Allocated a nice room on ground floor, well furnished with good desk area and reasonable WIFI, bathroom facilities were good, comfy bed with spotless linen and towels. Got a good night’s sleep.

The TV in my room was not working properly with “poor signal” appearing, while trying to watch football ☹

Never used the restaurant in the evening as was out but breakfast was good being cooked to order.

All in all a very nice stay for a 3 star hotel, well done!

Dog happy, guests happy ! . This was our 4th stay as it is very handily located for visiting family. Our stay there was the best so far and that was due to the very helpful and friendly staff. It has always been a good stay but the staff were noticeably a few notches above those on our previous stays. They are very dog friendly and even have a dog VIP photo wall. Room was clean and comfortable however our only feedback is that although we prefer to be at the back of the hotel the gravel staff / delivery car park makes for quite a noisy time of it when vehicles do park there or leave although this was not often but for some reason this seemed to occur between 11pm and 12pm when trying to get to sleep. Not a big complaint, just an observation. We had breakfast twice which was good and the service excellent, the dog even got a sausage! The ground are also excellent for giving the dog a walk, nice and secure away from main roads. Thank you, we will be back soon

Overnight stay. Just returned from an overnight stay at The Hickstead. We requested a quiet room and given a room overlooking the car park, which was quite busy as they had a function on. 

We booked Dinner in the Oak tree bistro which has not changed since we were there 8 years ago. 

Dinner was superb so compliments to the chef couldn’t fault it at all, but my fault is in the restaurant, bright lighting no atmosphere or candles on the table, which would create a better ambience and possibly attract more guests to eat in there. 
No upselling to encourage guests to spend more or encourage them to have a nightcap in the bar. 

It has now become a dog friendly hotel which I don’t have an issue with but it appears to be the main focus of the hotel! 

The rooms were very hot yet the restaurant was chilly

Staff and food are outstanding. This is the third time I have stayed here and I have to say, whatever they have changed in the kitchen has worked. Without doubt one of the most flavoursome delicious meals I have had in a long time. Value for money is beyond contestation. 

The staff are outstanding and will always try and do their best for you, a credit to the hotel. 

The rooms are always clean and tidy. Its not modern or designer but it's great for a couple of nights. The food and staff make up for it. Well worth staying here

Great south coast stay. Arrived late in the evening and was kindly great by the night reception start, big room and well stocked bar! Will be staying again. Big car park with quick access to  the motor way, London, Brighton and Gatwick airport. Reasonably priced too

A discovery.... We were invited to a party  at the burgiss hill football ground. Hickstead was the ferfect place to stay. very friendly staff notably the lebanese manager, so helpful.
Nice big bedroom, very quiet. Lovely breakfast. No teaspoons on the table to strir our tea or coffee??? The menu in the restaurant could be updated to some more gourmet food, was a bit food from the seventies. I believe that the restaurant could be the place to go by even a  good chef, cos the location is great

Satisfied. Vacation greenway Paris London by bike with friends. Stop at the Hickstead Hotel. Very warm welcome from the manager. Placement of bikes in a safe place and very friendly discussion with the manager who speaks French. Very good quality rooms and catering.
We will keep a very good memory and recommend the stopover

The Hickstead hotel, not as good as at first looks. We arrived a bit before check in time which is 3 pm however we had to wait till 4:30 before our room was ready for us. The room itself was very tired and in need of a makeover, the bed was very hard could have done with a soft mattress topper or ideally a new mattress. The wifi was terrible couldn’t get more than ten mins of wifi before it logged off again.

On a positive note the staff were very friendly and the meals in the restaurant were very good.

Not sure I could recommend this hotel or would stay again shame because it promised so much

An unexpected delight. We booked the  hotel simply because we needed a dog friendly, budget option that was convenient for an event, but it far exceeded our expectations. Rooms are spacious and bathrooms sparkling clean. We had asked for adjacent rooms  and  were given two  interconnecting rooms which was even better. 

The two standouts though were the staff and the food.

We arrived late so ate in the Oak Bistro simply for convenience. The menu was uninspiring so the delicious steaks that arrived  were a wonderful surprise .Breakfast was also far better than anticipated - and at least as good ifnot better, than at some hotels five times the price.

What really sets the hotel apart though are the staff. Everyone we came into contact with was charming and helpful. The nothing is too much trouble approach creates a really welcoming atmosphere.

As expected at this price point, not everything was perfect. The free wifi frequently dropped, the mattresses are a bit unforgiving and there are no sockets by the bed. These small negatives were easily outweighed by all the other things about the hotel that are great.

We stayed out at The Hickstead out of necessity, but would happily return by choice

A wonderfully warm welcome. A wonderfully warmth welcome to a charming hotel hidden down a lane! Booking in we were allocated a room on the first floor, as I have mobility problems I asked if a ground floor room could be arranged. No problem, all done with a smile. The room was spacious and comfortable, not too warm as the window had been opened. We visited the bar, served by the receptionist who appeared to be the only member of staff on duty who apologised that there was no choice of draught beer as there had been a wedding reception the previous day ( obviously drunk the bar dry of beer !) No deliveries on Sunday but the only beer on draught proved to be acceptable. My wine was certainly fine and the prices were reasonable.
There was an exceptionally good breakfast on offer in very pleasant surroundings. The waiter was attentive and prompt in his service. The only quibble would be the offering of marmalade which was the usual little packet of something more like a jelly! At a cost of £12.00 per person it was quite expensive but given the quality and quantity of items on offer well worth it.
This was a very pleasant overnight stay and would certainly visit again

Not the best place to stay. Booking in was terrible, only one person booked on reception so it took ages and then there is confusion from booking.com as they seem to say they have taken the payment but they have not. Also just display the WiFi code somewhere. It’s Hickstead in case you can’t find it

A very poor experience….. Decided to eat here as it was close to Hickstead Showground. Alarm bells should have rung when we arrived and the car park/outdoor areas were overgrown and generally unkempt. After waiting at the bar for 5 minutes with no one appearing to serve we asked at reception if we could have a table for dinner, we were seated at a dirty table in a very dated, empty dining room. Ordered bottle of pinot Grigio, were then given wine glasses so dirty you couldn’t see through them. Wrong wine was brought to the table, then to be told they had ran out of Pinot Grigio. We ordered gin  and tonic instead, waitress came back to the table embarrassed and explained they had run out of tonic! On the menu the nachos were served with salsa, guacamole and sour cream. When they arrived we asked where the sour cream was and apparently they had run out and chef had replaced it with yoghurt! The cheese was so burnt that the nachos were almost inedible. Overall a terrible experience from start to finish

Give it a miss !!. The first impression gained from a car park and entrance full of 3 months growth of weeds and a “lawn” that was probably last cut in Cromwells time sets the tone for this hotel. Note to the owners, If you’re intent on ripping people off by charging 4 times the normal room rate for Hickstead week at least try and provide a service and experience worthy of that rip off price. No tonic at the bar and complete apathy and disinterest from a night manager with the people skills of a hungry alligator, breakfast that was best avoided after the first mornings experience and the previous occupants medication left in the drawer in the room. It was Fawlty Towers without the benefit of the humour !!! Avoid unless you’re absolutely STUCK or happy to stay in a below par establishment that has zero interest in its customers

Worst Hotel i’ve ever stayed in in UK. bad experience. We had raw sewerage come up the pipes and into our toilet in the morning. We couldn’t shower as the bath would not drain or use the toilet. As it was the weekend we were told they couldn’t provide a refund but would get back to us. We haven’t heard anything. We paid 313 pound for 1 night

A fab birthday celebration. I liaised with Soli for a few weeks organising a 3 course meal for 35 family & friends to celebrate a birthday. He was very helpful & all the arrangements were easy to co-ordinate. Everybody has told me that they thoroughly enjoyed the food & the service was efficient & friendly. I would definitely recommend this venue if you are thinking of organising something similar. They even gave us a special room rate for those of us that stayed overnight

Friendly, helpful, very good prices on food and drinks. Super friendly guys that work there will do anything you ask, food spot on, beer is tasty and its realistically priced rooms are clean what more could you ask for ! And in a nice and quiet location nice little get away not far from burgesshill if you fancy a meal out or more drinks 😜

Middle of the road hotel. Stayed at the Hickstead for a few nights while in the area. The hotel is fine, its basic but clean and comfortable for a short stay. The shower was never able to quite get beyond moderately warm - fine for the summer, less so for winter.

Staff were very friendly and helpful throughout the stay

Desperate, dirty, dilapidated and depressing. This is a desperate place. There are staff and management that clearly couldn’t care less about this hotel. The approach is riddled with long grass and weeds everywhere and the front of the hotel is unkempt again with weeds. It’s clear no one thinks that it’s worth making an effort to tidy this place up, this is England after all where we are proud of our gardens and outside areas but clearly not here. 

Inside has no atmosphere - some rooms including meeting room stank of disinfectant or bug spray and on occasions so strong I had to leave the room. 

The young female receptionist was as helpful as she could be but woefully disorganised even when she tried hard. 

Give this hotel a wide berth and look for somewhere else anywhere

Awful breakfast. Hotel looks tired from the outside but friendly and helpful lady in reception offered a nice welcome. Room was clean and comfortable. However, breakfast was the worst I have experienced from a hotel. At £13 each we expected the full English to be freshly cooked. When the plates arrived the food had obviously been on a hot plate for some time. Bacon was like rubber. One small, tasteless, wrinkled sausage. Beans were dried out as if they had been reheated from day before. Worst of all was my wife's scrambled egg which was greasy, undercooked and had a strange smell. It was so bad it totally put her off the rest of her breakfast. Despite the restaurant being virtually empty we were not asked if everything was OK until the plates were cleared. I complained and the manager appeared a short time later. He was full of apologies and offered a free "late check out". We declined this and I asked for a refund of the price of one breakfast but this was refused due to "not our policy!!" Digusting. We won't be back

The Hickstead Hotel,   amazing honest and lovely staff. Hi Sam,

Thanks for sending the email below.

We personally cannot thank you all enough for a very enjoyable stay and the complete honestly of the staff after my partner left her purse with £175 inside.
(the staff honesty is why we gave a reward)

I asked you kindly to give me the names of the people whom we had interaction with whilst we stayed and would like to personally thank them.

Housekeeping - Helen, Jasmine, Isabelo and Allen
Dinner and Breakfast server - Sharma 
Receptionist: Sam 

Our gratitude and appreciation to you all thank you for making our stay very enjoyable.

Our only issue as we said, the beds are very very uncomfortable and the bathroom was in need of updating along with the dinning area as it is very dated.

However all that aside, again we are very grateful to the staff as previous mentioned.

Hopefully we will come again.

Steve & Faye

Accident waiting to happen. Having stayed here before I was looking forward to staying again but what a mistake

I have seen reviews for the same period (11-14th September) regarding the food I was also refused a refund despite only eating one of the breakfasts paid for due to the quality of the food - inedible. Clearly a theme

The fire evacuation in the middle of the night was chaotic and we were directed to a fire exit that wasn’t the closest and didn’t align to the written fire safety instructions. Also the door we were guided to was locked!

No one outside to co-ordinate where we should go, no one taking names to make sure everyone was outside, and we were then told to re-enter the building before the fire service had even arrived let alone check the building was safe

Again these concerns were raised and dismissed by Souliman the following day despite having experienced everything I have stated above and the number of fire safety breaches they had made. 

I was given the details of Khalid the director (which I will happily share should anyone want them) who has chosen not to acknowledge the concerns raised or the ropey nature of his food

I will be making sure that as many of my equestrian contacts - and there are many - are aware of this in the spirit of duty of care to people I know

Crimes against the Full English. Only been here for one night so far but felt I had to share. The plus side , the staff are absolutely lovely , its a bit dated  although very clean , bring your own USB chargers as there none . The down sides are the beds are very hard and pretty uncomfortable but the true crime against food is in the breakfast . The coffee was cold, I asked if we could have some fresh which was duly replaced with the same , my disappointment was rising and then it appeared . Honestly my 7yr old granddaughters would have done better . The mushrooms were deep fried to the point of being brown bullets , the hash browns greasy and horrible and the black pudding couldn't be cut with a knife and the scrambled egg was dripping in grease  . Who ever is in that kitchen should be ashamed of themselves . There is no excuse to dish up such abysmal fayre ,

Avoid!!!. I don’t know where to start!

Room itself was clean but I shared my bathroom for the duration of my stay with hair from what I can only imagine to be multiple other people (yes I reported this and yes the room was “cleaned” during my stay). 

The hotel broke more HSE regulations than I thought possible following an early morning fire alarm. All of which have been reported to the local fire authority as the hotel were not interested in discussing sensibly with us. 

Last but not least….breakfast! Wow! What an absolute disaster! I don’t even know where to start. Apart from advising you not to start yours! For your own health! It was revolting. Pretty sure the scrambled egg had been scraped off the car park, the bacon had been cooking longer than my entire stay and as for the black pudding…you could play ice hockey with it!

The hotel management were originally unavailable for discussion, then available, then laughed at us and are now avoiding us and our booking agents. 

What that says about this establishment speaks volumes. 

Avoid!!!

Nice quiet hotel. Sharma and Daisy first class employees.. Stayed 16th 17th & 18th August 2025
Made welcome by lovely receptionist Daisy.. Sharma was our waiter and he couldn’t have been any more polite and helpful. The hotel room was clean and comfortable. I did a review when we left but can’t find it anywhere!  so that’s why I’m doing another..  Your staff are first class, which helped us enjoy our short stay…

This hotel / hostel should be condemned. I had 1 night here and never again. I came here on a business trip. The first room was awful and had to move rooms because the plug sockets did not work, so they stated they will upgrade me to ab executive room.....!!! Exactly the same type of room, not an upgrade at all. The rooms are dirty and very dated and unsafe. The kettles are rusty inside, so I did not drink from them. The sink in the (executive room, joke) did not have any form of socket or plug / grid, so you could quite easily loose jewellery done it. The smell of the rooms is awful. Dinner again, awful, I had the chicken burger, and the chicken tasted off.  The banging and noise above you does not help when trying to sleep. You only have 2 plug sockets in the room which are the other side of the room, so no good for business use. I think HSE would have a field day at this hotel. Like I said the hotel should be closed down and condemned l. On a positive note, the reception staff were lovely, but you can see they were embarrassed.  The owners should be ashamed with themselves to allow a hotel / hostel get this bad. I would rather sleep in a tent

A very pleasant short break in the Sussex countryside at The Hickstead Hotel. Although hotel  little off beaten track a very warm welcome awaited us on our late arrival.  Room spacious lots of good points. Strong bedside reading lights.  Reachable electric sockets. Mirror over dressing table.  Extra pillows. Table and chairs.   Good location for National Trust.   Free parking a bonus.  Thanks to Soli and staff.  Very courteous

A wide berth required here. This hotel isn't tired, it's exhausted. The rooms are shabby but OK, but the food is terrible. They also charged us for breakfast we didn't have (having experienced their dinner) though the receptionist said we would have a refund.
On the up side, the Bolney Winery nearby was really great. Just find a different place to stay

Solaiman and sharma took care of us very well  was very happy staying here. Good place if your looking for peace and silence 
Specially Mr solaiman and sharma were very helpful during the whole stay for 8days very kind guys 
Will visit the Same place agian in next few months
All right.. Very good welcome. Very friendly staff, especially at the restaurant. Clean, well-placed and comfortable. I said what I wanted

Overnight business stay. Food and welcome were excellent. Great value. 
Attentive staff and all round quality for such a good price. 
Great location too!

Very warm and welcoming.. Really warm and welcoming, staff are very helpful, room, facilities and food all lovely, very accommodating. Nothing too much trouble. Very clean and comfortable. 
Particularly would like to commend the exceptional waiting service we received at dinner times from Sharma, really attentive and served very politely and with great warmth and friendliness. Thank you Sharma, really made the stay away from home feel more enjoyable and easy. :)

Loved it. I wonder if I stayed in the same hotel as some of the other reviewers. I stayed for 4 nights and was delighted with the service I received. Upon pulling into the large carpark I was greeted by a charming receptionist. Booking in was simple and off I trotted to room 105. Upon entry I was greeted with a nice bathroom with shower over bath and plenty of bright lighting. I do hate a dull bathroom. I moved into the room which is like any other room in a hotel, tv/kettle etc. The bed was a king size, great since it was only me and I had a nice table and chairs to sit at. The bed was absolutely fine but the carpet did look abit old but there were no holes or tears in it. I certainly preferred this room to the travel lodge I’d just stay at in Guildford (never again will I use that brand).

I had paid for breakfast and it was lovely. On week days it is cooked to order, you just stipulate what you want and the quantity of each item. I had 3 sausages and 6 rashers of bacon with lots of mushrooms but a person next to me had smoked salmon. At the week end it was buffet style just like TL & PI and again was fresh and plentiful. I didn’t have the evening menu as was eating out each night but I did notice the restaurant was closed Sunday nights.

I’d certainly recommend this hotel to everyone

Great service. Lovely hotel - very clean and comfortable. Great food. Staff excellent and very friendly especially sharma who served us dinner.
Best thing about this hotel is that it is that they welcome dogs and really treat them as vip guests . Certainly would visit again

Excellent service. Excellent customer service and extremely friendly service particularly from Sharma in the restaurant for my evening meal. Staff are so friendly helpful and nothing is too much for them. My 4th visit
Very friendly service!!. Extremely friendly service particularly from Sharma in the restaurant for my evening meal. All very friendly and helpful!!

Do not hire function room. Recently used this hotel for a 50th birthday function, however the experience deserves a 1 star review 

The DJ had not turned up, when we questioned the managers they kept saying he will be here in 15 minutes. He arrived 1 hour late with minimal equipment and continued to play unsuitable music, when we spoke to the DJ who appeared quite frustrated informed us he had been booked last minutes and had no idea who or what he was catering for ! Suggesting the hotel had forgotten to arrange a DJ in advance . 
To make matters worse only 1 member of staff serving drinks all evening this was clearly too much for 1 person to deal with. 

We tried to raise these concerns with the on site managers who were very dismissive and showed no empathy or or interest in resolving the issues! 

Overall the event was poorly organised and handled unprofessionally I would not recommend this hotel for hosting any functions

Mixed feelings. Clean and comfortable with friendly staff.
BUT
20 people were booked for dinner at 7.30.  
All food was odrdered in advance.  No food appeared until around 8.20.  Mains around 9.00.  This was disruptive for some older folk whose sleep was affected by eating late. There was only ONE waiter.
And some of the food was poor.  Eg Melon boat, my grandsons' starter, was a pile of little melon chunks that I could hardly get my fork into.  And their "pizza" was very disapppointing.  It looked like four triangles of toast with something wierd on top.  Not suitable for a 6 and a 9 yr old

Poor experience. Customer service poor.. Car park on arrival dark, reception was manned. Room tv didnt work first off as being wiped clear of channels(fixed later) ..ladybird  in room kept on appearing. Mentioned and said they had a problem upstairs.
 Sewage smell from toilet that was glugging bubblesall night. Poor breakfast and very expensive. 
The door connecting another room was thin and you could hear what was being said. The light from it also was very bright, they stayed up late.
In all the worst stay at this hotel we have had.
We did mention all this to the staff ...
Would i stay again... NO
Would i recomend..... NO

Have I now sent 5 emils to the hotel and two to their head office. No reply so far.

Breakfast for one contrntal and one cooked was £28. The toast was served 20 mins after I had eaten the food. 
I have even rung them up.. but nothing

Couple getaway. Amazing. Nice country area. As soon as we checked it. Warmest welcome. Warm lobby.  Very easy check in.  Brilliant value.
Warm rooms. Very spacious. Love the tables and chairs so eating in the room was easy and better. Very big desk area in the room, so my partner can work on the laptop and lots of space all around.
We had breakfast in the morning lots of choice. And dinner too. Food has been fabulous.  Partner got the fish and chips. 
Fish was perfect. As not lots and lots of batter on just a perfect amount. I got steak. I asked med to med well. Sadly got it rare. The lovely waitress got it sorted brilliantly for me.  The staff was so friendly and made sure we were happy.  Definitely coming back 100 %

Never again. After setting foot inside the building my stomach sank. The smell of drains swept right through. The idea of eating anything here was a non starter. The rooms smelt and the carpet must of been used for rolling up dead body's covered in stains. If your a light sleeper then you can bet you won't get much sleep with people walking up and down the corridor. On a plus side the staff were polite

Terrible, won't be back. Do not recommend staying here and will not be staying again.

Bugs in the bathroom, poorly cleaned bath tub and questionable hygiene overall. I queried this with them and received one initial response saying they couldn't do anything other than a future discount, and then proceeded to ignore any further communications and even ignored booking.com themselves
Christmas Fun. Sharma was a wonderful, attentive host throughout our evening. We all had a lovely meal and a brilliant time 😊. Thank you for having us

Nice clean hotel. Nice relaxed atmosphere. Sharma was a fantastic host.
Rooms ere clean and good value.
Location is close to main road but in quiet location

Excellent. Excellent - comfortable hotel and rooms, friendly service especially from our waiter Sharma and the Receptionists, delicious food and drink, and excellent value for money. Very nice location, too. Many thanks - highly recommended!

Just what we needed. We stayed for 1nt NYD. The hotel staff could t do enough for us. The restaurant was closed due to the holiday but that impeded our stay at all. The room warm spacious, warm & allowed our dog too. Break fast was good & the even gave a sausage to our dog! Looking forward to returning

better than expected. I have to say the rooms where better than expected and the staff very friendly if you need a bed for the night I would recommend.
Evening meal was excellent no problems there at all. 
Breakfast was good The only comment me and the wife would make is the eggs were a bit bland but apart from that no complaints
Extraordinary. Lovely customer experience and lovable experience from the staffs would like to stay here again
😍. Great service and very friendly staffs,especially Gautham.The location and atmosphere was good

The worst hotel I have ever stayed in over 40 countries/hundreds of hotels ,Check ALL the reviews not just the top rated. Where do i start! People can't have stayed at the same hotel as us! Arrived 3.30pm (8C outside so cold). Given a key, corridors stank of sewage, room cold as heating not on, toilet bubbles and emits sewer gasses when room above and to sides flush the toilet. Paper thin walls and ceilings - we could here people farting, adjusting position in bed, TV's, footsteps upstairs (or maybe it was a herd of zebras running around for hours?). Asked reception for help e.g. change of room in a near empty hotel but apparently "there is nothing we can do...". Went into the bar then went back to our room to get jackets as it was so cold (this was 4 hours after we were told heating was going on). Went into restaurant and still needed coats - it had wall to wall radiators that were all switched off and they were relying on what looked like 4 or 5 small portable electric heaters (the type my Gran would use to keep her feet warm) to heat a LARGE area. We got a taxi to eat elsewhere and told the staff why. 2 other guests were also in the bar area with coats on and we did not see anyone eat at the hotel - they all went out! After an awful night listening to other people's bodily functions, sewer smells wafting up from the toilet on each flush we left early the next morning (we had paid for 2 nights but there was no way that was going to happen!!) and told reception exactly what we thought. It is NOT the staff's fault, but the owners or managers need to spend a night in their own caravan like rooms with the heating off and see what its like for paying  guests. This property used to be 5 stars, now its 3 stars and I think 2 of those stars were stolen! The rooms and the toilet operations need to be inspected by environmental health - So glad we didn't eat at the hotel as I wonder what other shortcuts they have been taking!
Do not bother with "dear guest we are sorry to hear that..." rubbish. Fix it for others, we were in room 112. You could, however, refund us for the night we didn't stay!
Last minute booking - warm welcome. My team and I was provided with very good customer service by Parv and Soli

We arrived late but we’re made welcome and as always found the accommodation just right.. We were warmly welcomed to the hotel by Soli, who quickly found our room and welcomed us back as returning guests

wonderful. A wonderful hotel for a quick last minute trip. We arrived in the evening to a warm, friendly welcome. The room was clean, spacious and warm. We ate in the bistro. The steak dinner was absolutely sublime-cooked to perfection. It’s not particularly modern or swanky, but it’s clean, comfortable and friendly. Couldn’t ask for anything more fir a very reasonable price. Thank you !
